skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Art, Negotiation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus.

keyAbilityRef.name: Toss a coin
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: As an Errand action, you may perform in a city or town and earn Clim equal to your relevant Art roll x 10 up to a maximum of 300 clim.

ability1Ref.name: Inspiring Song
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
All allies within range (including yourself) may add 1d4 to a single roll they make until the start of your next turn. They may choose to add this after seeing the roll.

Upkeep: At the start of your next turn and every turn thereafter, you may spend 1 AP to continue this performance, granting all allies within range the benefit once again.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
All allies within range (including yourself) may add 1d4 to a single roll they make until the start of your next turn. They may choose to add this after seeing the roll.

Upkeep: At the start of your next turn and every turn thereafter, you may spend 1 AP to continue this performance, granting all allies within range the benefit once again.

ability2Ref.name: Dirge of Despair
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
All enemies within range must make a save against your Potency or become affected by this Song. While affected, you may subtract 1d4 from a single roll they make until the start of your next turn. You may choose to add this after seeing the roll.

Upkeep: At the start of your next turn and every turn thereafter, you may spend 1 AP to continue this performance. Enemies that successfully made their save, do not have to save again against this continued performance, but those who were affected continue to be affected.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
All enemies within range must make a save against your Potency or become affected by this Song. While affected, you may subtract 1d4 from a single roll they make until the start of your next turn. You may choose to add this after seeing the roll.

Upkeep: At the start of your next turn and every turn thereafter, you may spend 1 AP to continue this performance. Enemies that successfully made their save, do not have to save again against this continued performance, but those who were affected continue to be affected.

ability3Ref.name: Shatter
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: Every enemy within range must make a save against your Potency or take Light Damage. Any unattended objects made of glass or a similar vulnerable material also shatter.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: Every enemy within range must make a save against your Potency or take Light Damage. Any unattended objects made of glass or a similar vulnerable material also shatter.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Siren Song
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
All enemies in range must make a save against your Potency. If they fail, they may only spend AP on their next turn to move closer to you using the most direct path possible and cannot make attacks before that. To move closer, they may take actions that would help in getting closer such as standing up from prone or using fae flash. 

If this path leads them through an obviously harmful hazard such as off a cliff, they are no longer affected by this ability. Otherwise, this ability lasts until they are within 5ft of you or until they take damage. While playing this Song, you can take no other actions. Taking any other action cancels the Song, immediately freeing all enemies from its effects. A creature can only be affected by this ability once per encounter.

name: Battle Mage
descriptionHtml:
There was once a time when pure, unbridled magic was the most powerful force in the land. A single mage could hold off entire enemy battalions with ease. But that changed with the invention of Artificing. Suddenly, battles began to shift, with more and more fighters targeting mages directly, exploiting their difficulty in close combat. As the martial world integrated rapid uses of internal mana and artifices, mages found themselves on the defensive, facing enemies up close like never before.

In response to this new threat, the first Battle Mages emerged from Northi. These warriors developed a unique spell weave, while incredibly mana-intensive, it allowed them to cast spells rapidly, even in the heat of close-range combat. This innovation took full advantage of their physical prowess as Chimera, turning them into formidable opponents both near and far.

The invention of the "Shooting Form" further enhanced their capabilities. This technique allowed Battle Mages to imbue their melee weapons with the properties of staves, enabling a seamless transition between long-range spellcasting and close-quarters combat. With this versatility, Battle Mages became a force to be reckoned with, combining the raw power of magic with the physicality of martial arts.

requirementsHtml: Have at least 1 spell, Be proficient in at least 1 melee weapon.
requirementsText: Have at least 1 spell, Be proficient in at least 1 melee weapon.
guideHtml:
The battlemage is a competent switch striker, being able to swap between melee and ranged combat instantly. Weapon Swap has no cost and can be used even on an enemies turn, allowing you to swap to melee form if an enemy thinks he can just walk away.

Mage Armor gives a lot of flexibility which is normally unavailable. When fighting a horde of weak monsters, you can start in heavy armor and watch their attacks bounce off almost harmlessly. Against a hard hiting boss, starting in light for maximum evasion might be better. If you made a mistake or the situation changes, a 2 AP cost is an acceptable price to pay for a change. Your RP changes as well, so starting in heavy armor and then moving to lighter armor as the fight progresses is a valid strategy.

Dragon Breaks The Ranks is a long range gap closer that moves you forward with devastating force. The high AP cost may make it hard to use, but don't overlook the AoE damage part of the charge. It can also be used to escape from a tough situation as it gives flight, lots of movement and you don't provoke, provided you have a target you can charge to.

Because the battle mage wants to spend time in melee combat, battle cast is a necessity to be able to cast spells without putting yourself at risk.

Circle Swing is their main melee attack. It allows you to create space and follow up with a cheaper spell or ranged attack. Be aware that you may still provoke attacks from other enemies you are engaged with, even if you main target is now unable to.

The most common weapon for a battle mage is a polearm. The extra range on attacks makes it easier for it to weave melee and spells without provoking.

skills: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Intimidation, Magic, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness or Reason.
soul: You gain +1 Focus, Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Mage Armor
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Mage Armor

associatedAbilityRef.name: Mage Armor
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You gain magical Light Armor, Medium Armor or Heavy Armor until the end of the encounter. This function just as normal armor of that type would, giving the same benefits and penalties. Does not stack with other armor, apply the effects of the Mage Armor This magical armor does not require proficiency of any sort and does not cost any Burden to wear and carry.

You may apply the effects of Barrier, Fortress, Manaweave, Protective Coating or Spidersilk to this armor (It must still meet the requirements, you cannot put fortress on a light armor for example). This can only be done when you first apply it, not if you later change your armor during combat.

If Mage Armor is active, you may spend 2 AP to change its type or to remove it.

ability1Ref.name: Dragon Breaks The Ranks
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You gain Flight and surround yourself in fire, then Sidestep to a point in a direct line. You destroy low cover you pass through, though the GM may rule that some cover is too sturdy to be destroyed. In either case, you continue until you reach the target and may make a single melee Heavy Attack against an enemy in range with your weapon. Any other characters in your path may spend 1 RP to move 5ft out of your charge or take Light Fire Damage.

If you have an element mastered, you may change the Fire damage of this ability to the element you have mastered.

This spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

ability2Ref.name: Battle Cast
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: The next Spell you cast before the end of your turn does not provoke an attack of opportunity from being casted in Melee Range of an opponent.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: The next Spell you cast before the end of your turn does not provoke an attack of opportunity from being casted in Melee Range of an opponent.

ability3Ref.name: Shooting Form
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You transform a melee weapon you’re holding into a Wand Channeling Weapon or return a transformed weapon back to normal. While transformed, the weapon keeps any relevant enchants and functions exactly like how a wand would. You are considered proficient in this wand, even if you normally wouldn’t be and can fire Arcane Elemental shots with it, even if you normally wouldn’t be able to.

Lasts until the end of the encounter, for 5 minutes or until the weapon leaves your possession.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Circle Swing
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you may move the target to anywhere in your weapon range (Max 10ft) , then push it 5ft. The target must remain at the same height it was before this ability was used. Your next Spell or Channeling Weapon attack this turn costs 1 less AP (Min 0).

The target must remain at the same height it was before this ability was used. Your next Spell or Channeling Weapon attack this turn costs 1 less AP (Min 0).

name: Battlemaster
guideHtml:
The battlemaster is an expert in all weapons. If you find a good weapon, whether its an artifice, relic or even a divine arms, you will be able to use it to its maximum effect.

You also don't need to worry about being unable to use certains attack with your new weapon due to the Unconventional Weapon ability. It has the potential for silly combos, such as using Iai while a greataxe.

Being a master of battle, they posses ways to punish both Dodge and Guard focused characters. Skillful blow has lock-on to make it good against high agility where as Mordschlag's guard crush allows it to punish heavily armored enemies.

Disarming Attack is their last ability and it can give them the edge in a duel against most martial enemies. If they forgot to bring a spare weapon, the disarm all but disables them for a turn.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Arms Mastery
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You are considered proficient with every melee weapon you wield (This does not count as being proficient for class requirements).

ability1Ref.name: Mordschlag
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You attack the target with the pommel or crossguard making a Heavy Attack against the target.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You attack the target with the pommel or crossguard making a Heavy Attack against the target.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Longsword, Heavy Blades and Bludgeoning weapons only.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Longsword, Heavy Blades and Bludgeoning weapons only.

ability2Ref.name: Skillfull Blow
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.

ability3Ref.name: Unconventional Weapon
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may treat the weapon you’re currently wielding as a weapon of a different group for the next attack in addition to its normal group. In addition to receiving the base weapon properties of both weapon groups, this allows it to meet the requirements of abilities that require a certain group, and allows you to change its damage type to the chosen weapon’s damage type. The GM may rule that some abilities still cannot be performed with your equipped weapon.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may treat the weapon you’re currently wielding as a weapon of a different group for the next attack in addition to its normal group. In addition to receiving the base weapon properties of both weapon groups, this allows it to meet the requirements of abilities that require a certain group, and allows you to change its damage type to the chosen weapon’s damage type. The GM may rule that some abilities still cannot be performed with your equipped weapon.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Disarming Attack
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you Disarm the target until the start of your next turn.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you Disarm the target until the start of your next turn.

name: Berserker
guideHtml:
Berserkers are highly aggresive fighters that rely on allies to keep them alive. They are centered around their rage stance, which gives them 2 bonus AP in exchange for disabling RP abilities. A person without RP is extremely vulnerable, so you will need allies to cast thing like the acolyte's Angel's Embrace on you for protection. Alternatively, you can wait with using it until you are have burned through some RP already. You will lose out on some power, but it may be better than going down.

Both Break Free and Fallen Friend fit the playstyle of waiting with Rage Stance instead of using it immediately. Break Free especially is powerful against enemies with heavy CC effects.

Into the fray is a gap closer that lets you approach an enemy without spending AP, allowing for a maximum amount of attacks after. The RP cost makes it so its best used when already in rage stance where your RP is less useful.

The same applies to Reckless Strike. Normally trading 1 RP for 1 AP is not worth it, but when you can't use your RP anyway, it is a good way to get some extra damage in.

skills: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Perception, Survival, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness.
soul: You gain +1 Power or Focus.

keyAbilityRef.name: Fallen Friend
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When an Heroic tier ally reaches 0 HP, you may immediately enter Rage Stance for 0 AP and RP as well as 0 mana. You can use this even if you don’t have the Rage ability yet.

ability1Ref.name: Rage Stance
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You enter a Stance in which you prioritize offense over defense. The Stance lasts until the encounter ends, you get Downed or up to an hour. While the Stance lasts, gain 2 additional AP that can only be used on melee attacks each turn (including the turn you use this), but cannot use actions that use RP other than attack of opportunity unless they have the Rage keyword. You also cannot use abilities with the Spell keyword, unless they also have the Rage keyword. You are treated as having 0 RP for abilities with that as a requirement. While in your Rage Stance, you gain a +1 bonus to Guard and a +5 bonus to all Fitness checks.

While the Stance lasts, gain 2 additional AP that can only be used on melee attacks each turn (including the turn you use this), but cannot use actions that use RP other than attack of opportunity unless they have the Rage keyword. You also cannot use abilities with the Spell keyword, unless they also have the Rage keyword. You are treated as having 0 RP for abilities with that as a requirement. While in your Rage Stance, you gain a +1 bonus to Guard and a +5 bonus to all Fitness checks.

ability2Ref.name: Into the Fray
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may move up to twice your movement speed. You must end this movement in range to make a melee attack against an enemy. Can only be used on your turn.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may move up to twice your movement speed. You must end this movement in range to make a melee attack against an enemy. Can only be used on your turn.

ability3Ref.name: Break Free
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may use this ability when you would be affected by a Stun, Root, Fear or a similar effect that would prevent you from acting that's inflicted by an enemy. You may immediately enter Rage Stance without spending any AP or RP. You gain immunity to this effect for the duration of the Rage.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may use this ability when you would be affected by a Stun, Root, Fear or a similar effect that would prevent you from acting that's inflicted by an enemy. You may immediately enter Rage Stance without spending any AP or RP. You gain immunity to this effect for the duration of the Rage.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Not in Rage Stance.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Not in Rage Stance.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Reckless Strike
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.

name: Biochemist
descriptionHtml:
Yanyan stood in front of the mirror. Her cat ears twitched nervously as she inspected herself. Not good enough. She murmured to herself. Camielelileananu would never notice her looking like that.

With a sigh she stepped away, the cat ears and tail disappearing as if they were never there. She laid down on the bed and began to browse through her large collection of information on her idol. Articles Camielelileananu had written, interviews with her and even just a map of where she traveled.

She showed great loyalty and courage. Yanyan read a comment from her about a dogfolk guard that saved a child. That was just praising an individual person, nothing to do with the race. Camielelileananu spent a lot of time around some slimes, but that was to be expected. One of the greatest minds in her field was a slimegirl after all.

Yanyan had studied this over a dozen times already, trying to figure just WHAT type of chimera her love was into. Her eyes fell onto the map again and landed on Houkaina. It was a country of Merfolk, relatively small. However, Camielelileananu had been there multiple times already for book signings. Could it be?&

She wasted no time. Yanyan immediately got up from her bed and began to pack her stuff. She couldnt yet transform into a merfolk, but she knew where to get the samples required for that.

guideHtml:
The biochemist class is a specialist class that is very dependent on having the correct gene.

You can collect samples through… various methods and use them with Biohacking to temporarily have access to the gene. You will really want the increased gene limit from mastering the class to give yourself more options.

You activate these samples through Gene Activation, which gives you a temporary bonus. Your usefulness heavily depends on what genes you decided to bring with you.

Helix Siphon lets you work around the usual limit on genes, as long as you're fighting chimera's or are partied with some. Your power as biochemist greatly increases if you have allies with you that give you useful genes.

G. Shift is an encounter start that lets you mimic the effects of an elixir, at the cost of 1 extra AP or RP. It also still costs HP, so not necessarily what you'd want to use every fight but free elixirs are good.

Anima Synthesis Form is your secret art and it gives you the entire subrace for the whole encounter. Like with gene activation, it heavily depends on what gene you decided to bring. Some races are always good though, such as horse for horse step accel or harpy for flight.

skills: You gain +5 skill points in Riding, Appraise, Medicine, Magic, History, Animal Husbandry, Art or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Transmuter of Life
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Biohacking.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You can store up to one gene in your gene inventory.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Biohacking
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
At the start of an arc, you can transmute a gene from a Chimera Subrace into your DNA as long as you have a sample (a vial of blood).

Transmuting the gene does not consume the item. Add a single Chimera gene to your gene inventory. 

You can perform this action again for an IP or EP. Biohacking can store genes equal to your gene limit.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
At the start of an arc, you can transmute a gene from a Chimera Subrace into your DNA as long as you have a sample (a vial of blood).

Transmuting the gene does not consume the item. Add a single Chimera gene to your gene inventory.

You can perform this action again for an IP or EP. Biohacking can store genes equal to your gene limit.

ability1Ref.name: Gene Activation
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You transmute yourself, consuming a gene. You transform,  granting yourself a single chimera racial ability that corresponds with the gene. 

You are transformed until the end of your next turn. Doing this consumes the gene from your DNA. You can deactivate the transformation for 0 RP.

You cannot obtain and use abilities that require an IP or EP to use through this ability.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You transmute yourself, consuming a gene. You transform,  granting yourself a single chimera racial ability that corresponds with the gene.

You are transformed until the end of your next turn. Doing this consumes the gene from your DNA. You can deactivate the transformation for 0 RP.

You cannot obtain and use abilities that require an IP or EP to use through this ability.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must have the required Gene in your gene inventory.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must have the required Gene in your gene inventory.

ability2Ref.name: Helix Siphon
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
When you make a melee attack against an enemy that is a Chimera, you copy some of their DNA, granting you a gene for any ability of their race.

This gene is temporary and only lasts until the end of combat. Alternatively, you can touch an ally Chimera or downed enemy and copy a single gene.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
When you make a melee attack against an enemy that is a Chimera, you copy some of their DNA, granting you a gene for any ability of their race.

This gene is temporary and only lasts until the end of combat. Alternatively, you can touch an ally Chimera or downed enemy and copy a single gene.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Melee attack deals damage against a known Chimera.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Melee attack deals damage against a known Chimera.

ability3Ref.name: G. Shift
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You take 11 true damage, then choose to either lose 1 RP or reduce your AP by 1 for your first turn. 

You temporarily mutate your body, granting an effect similar to one of the following Alchemy Elixirs:  Rabbit, Reaper, Shark, Tunnel, Bull, Bear. This effect does not stack with their corresponding Elixir Effect. This effect cannot be dispelled and lasts until the end of the encounter.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You take 11 true damage, then choose to either lose 1 RP or reduce your AP by 1 for your first turn.

You temporarily mutate your body, granting an effect similar to one of the following Alchemy Elixirs:  Rabbit, Reaper, Shark, Tunnel, Bull, Bear. This effect does not stack with their corresponding Elixir Effect. This effect cannot be dispelled and lasts until the end of the encounter.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Anima Synthesis Form
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You consume a gene and transform, granting yourself all abilities of the chimera subrace of that corresponding gene. 

This lasts until the end of the encounter. 

In addition, learning this skill grants you +1 to your gene inventory for the Biohacking skill. Despite being transformed, you can still use Gene Activation to add additional abilities.

You cannot obtain and use abilities that require an IP or EP to use through this ability.

This lasts until the end of the encounter.

name: Black Oath Style
descriptionHtml:
Fordu D Nali stood, centered in the middle of the rough road. Behind her, a village was evacuating in the face of the incoming armies of Northi and Westria. Before her, a scouting party of Westrian Crusaders were dismounting, all shining silver armor and shields. At their head, a hulking brute of a man stomped forward, a massive greatsword already licking with holy light.

I am Ser Kael, Paladin of the Church of Light. In Karis name, surrender so that you may be granted a peaceful end, the man boomed.

Fordu scoffed and took a heavy stance, her fists clenched. You want to get past me? You dont have enough blood to pay that toll.

The men behind Ser Kael shifted as if they could feel the weight of power gathering around her. The paladin leveled his sword at her, golden fire ripping down its length, casting an incandescent glow across the shadows of the canyon wall. He took first one step, the ground cracking slightly underneath his might, then another. Then he was running, his sword pointed to skewer her.

She took a slow, deep breath and released the power singing in her blood. Shadowy wings burst from her back and time seemed to slow. The onrushing paladin no longer barreled down at her like a charging horse. The pain and ache of her Divine Release was captured, channeled, stored. Licks of dark fire flickered around her fists as she waited and watched. Ser Kael closed the distance and she did nothing to stop him from plunging his sword directly through her chest. She barely felt the massive sword, but the holy light ate at her like acid. Even with every bit of her training, she couldnt hold in the scream that ripped from her throat.

But she didnt fall. Her wings stabilized her, her stance grounded her, and the pain was only fuel. Gathered, condensed, mixed, converted into a dark fire. As close as they were, she could see Ser Kaels eyes widen in shock through the gap in his helmet. Then she struck back. Shadow Counter. Taking the pain of her Divine Release, the agony of being stabbed and burned by holy fire. Her mana flooded with the growing counter, empowering and bringing the potential to its height. Even the impact of her attack she reflected upon herself, amplified, and unleashed to join the rush of force and shadow.

Ser Kael ceased to exist from the waist up. Fordu stumbled forward a step without his weight holding her up and she pulled the sword still impaling her painfully out of her chest. It clattered loudly against the canyon floor. She took another step forward and the crusaders Ser Kael brought with him took a step back. She grinned and wiped the blood dripping from her mouth away. Her wings flexed, the Divinity of her blood sustaining her even through a devastating wound.

Youre not getting away! She rushed forward, dark fire gathering around her fists and the screams of the crusaders echoed off the walls.

She barely felt the massive sword, but the holy light ate at her like acid. Even with every bit of her training, she couldnt hold in the scream that ripped from her throat.

But she didnt fall. Her wings stabilized her, her stance grounded her, and the pain was only fuel. Gathered, condensed, mixed, converted into a dark fire. As close as they were, she could see Ser Kaels eyes widen in shock through the gap in his helmet. Then she struck back. Shadow Counter. Taking the pain of her Divine Release, the agony of being stabbed and burned by holy fire. Her mana flooded with the growing counter, empowering and bringing the potential to its height. Even the impact of her attack she reflected upon herself, amplified, and unleashed to join the rush of force and shadow.

Ser Kael ceased to exist from the waist up. Fordu stumbled forward a step without his weight holding her up and she pulled the sword still impaling her painfully out of her chest. It clattered loudly against the canyon floor. She took another step forward and the crusaders Ser Kael brought with him took a step back. She grinned and wiped the blood dripping from her mouth away. Her wings flexed, the Divinity of her blood sustaining her even through a devastating wound.

Youre not getting away! She rushed forward, dark fire gathering around her fists and the screams of the crusaders echoed off the walls.
guideHtml:
The Black Oath style is an unarmed fighting style that sacrifices its own health for extra damage.

Your main tool for this class is the Inexorable Shadow Stance. It lets you deal damage to yourself to boost your damage to enemies by the same amount. Enemies CAN still dodge attacks after you declare your HP loss, but because it would make your action invalid your HP would get refunded. It has some strong uses in overwhelming enemies with light attacks that can suddenly hit extremely hard or punching through his guard and block enemies with overwhelming force. And with Focused Power, you simply guarantee that you deal max extra damage. Dark Surge also synergizes with this, simply giving you a nice small accuracy bonus for using your stance.

Eclipse Breaker is a setup move that can deny an area from an enemy. It has no mana cost and and you can start charging it with 1 AP, simply canceling it if nobody is in it.

Shadow Counter is another setup move with multiple uses. As you get lower on HP, the damage of this ability increases drastically. This forces an enemy into uncomfortable choices of potentially spending a lot of actions to escape from your attack or risk taking a hard hitting move. You do become weaker defensively due to attacks getting trick attack against you, but if you survive your damage goes up even more.

Overall, the black oath style is a high risk/reward playstyle. Deciding on when to take your damage is key to staying alive.

FAQ:

Q: What happens when an enemy reacts to my attack after I use Inexorable Shadow Stance?
A: If an enemy decides to Dodge or take a similar sort of action to make your attack miss, that attack would no longer fulfill the ‘on hit’ condition and your HP would be refunded. If any enemy decides to Block however, you would still have spent your HP.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Deception, Intimidation, Insight, Medicine or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 Focus, Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Dark Surge
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: After you deal damage to yourself, you gain Dark Surge for your next unarmed attack that turn. Dark Surge gives you +1 Accuracy.

ability1Ref.name: Inexorable Shadow Stance
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You enter a stance that enhances your attacks at the cost of your own life. Whenever you hit an enemy with an unarmed attack, you may deal Light or Heavy true damage to yourself to increase the damage of the attack by the same amount. This may be done after reactions to the attack have been declared, though an enemy may still choose to react to it again or differently afterwards.

If used with an attack that hits more than 1 target, this extra damage only applies to one of the damage rolls.

You may do this effect twice per round. If an enemy dodges to make you miss and refund the effect, it still counts as one of your uses. When enhanced, these attacks gain the Augment keyword.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:

If used with an attack that hits more than 1 target, this extra damage only applies to one of the damage rolls.

You may do this effect twice per round. If an enemy dodges to make you miss and refund the effect, it still counts as one of your uses. When enhanced, these attacks gain the Augment keyword.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance.

ability2Ref.name: Eclipse Breaker
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Heavy attack against all targets in a 15x15ft line.

You must choose the line direction when starting the ability.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Heavy attack against all targets in a 15x15ft line.

You must choose the line direction when starting the ability.

ability3Ref.name: Focused Power
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: When you’re about to take the extra damage from Inexorable Shadow Stance, you may choose to maximize the damage instead of rolling. Affects both the self damage and the damage you deal with it.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: When you’re about to take the extra damage from Inexorable Shadow Stance, you may choose to maximize the damage instead of rolling. Affects both the self damage and the damage you deal with it.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Must be in Inexorable Shadow Stance.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Must be in Inexorable Shadow Stance.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Shadow Counter
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You prepare a powerful heavy attack against a single enemy. This attack deals extra damage based on half your missing health.

If that enemy provokes an AoO while you are charging this ability, you may immediately use this ability as if you finished charging it.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You prepare a powerful heavy attack against a single enemy. This attack deals extra damage based on half your missing health.

If that enemy provokes an AoO while you are charging this ability, you may immediately use this ability as if you finished charging it.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Must be in Inexorable Shadow Stance.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Must be in Inexorable Shadow Stance.

name: Blacksmith
descriptionHtml: Forgers of Lyr's weapons.
descriptionText: Forgers of Lyr's weapons.
guideHtml:
Blacksmith is a crafting class that creates weapons. A lot of your abilities and use will come during interlude phases where you can craft your weapons.

Outside of crafting, you can use Forged Knowledge to boost the damage of an attack you made. It requires you to use a weapon you crafted yourself and you only maximize one dice, so use it when you are a few points of damage of from killing an enemy or to turn a 1 into a 6 or 8.

With Weapon Maintenance, you give a weapon a +1 bonus to damage or accuracy. It only costs an encounter start action, so there's no reason not to do it if you aren't using it for anything else.

When crafting, Steady Craft II is better than rolling on average. You can delay using it to see if you need a higher number at the end before using it, but it will be better most of the time.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Power, Focus or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Craft Weapons
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Blacksmith Core Crafting Abilities.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You can craft using the Iron and Tamahagane base materials, as well as any alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You can use a Smith’s Hammer as a crafting tool for blacksmithing crafts. You may also use it as a Bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient.
keyAbilityRef.benefit4: You gain proficiency in 1 smithing weapon.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Blacksmith Core Crafting
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Blacksmith’s Modification A
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an A-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon or Crafting mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Weapon Alloy
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the smithing weapon. Each material has its own crafting point cost.

Create Ingot
Cost: Errand
Description:
You create an ingot from blacksmithing base materials or from blacksmithing special materials.  See Item: Blacksmithing Materials.

ability1Ref.name: Repair Weapon
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: Errand. You repair a broken smithing weapon.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: Errand. You repair a broken smithing weapon.

ability2Ref.name: Forged Knowledge
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: Using your knowledge of weapons, you infuse the strongest point of your weapon with mana and then strike at the perfect angle dealing maximum weapon damage.  Maximize one damage dice for the attack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: Using your knowledge of weapons, you infuse the strongest point of your weapon with mana and then strike at the perfect angle dealing maximum weapon damage.  Maximize one damage dice for the attack.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Attack must be made with a weapon you crafted.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Attack must be made with a weapon you crafted.

ability3Ref.name: Steady Craft II
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You add a crafting check to the item. The crafting dice is automatically a 7.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You add a crafting check to the item. The crafting dice is automatically a 7.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: 1 Crafting Dice.
ability3Ref.requirementText: 1 Crafting Dice.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Weapon Maintenance
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You recall having previously assisted an ally in maintaining their weapon, causing it to be in top shape for battle. This weapon gains a +1 Power Material Bonus or +1 Focus Material Bonus. If you smithed the weapon it gains both. This Artisan bonus is considered the same bonus as from a weapon's material like Tamahagane and does not stack with it.

If the weapon you choose gets no bonus from this ability, it's critical range instead improves by 1. (A normal weapon now crits on a 19 and 20, a katana on a 18, 19 and 20).
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You recall having previously assisted an ally in maintaining their weapon, causing it to be in top shape for battle. This weapon gains a +1 Power Material Bonus or +1 Focus Material Bonus. If you smithed the weapon it gains both. This Artisan bonus is considered the same bonus as from a weapon's material like Tamahagane and does not stack with it.

If the weapon you choose gets no bonus from this ability, it's critical range instead improves by 1. (A normal weapon now crits on a 19 and 20, a katana on a 18, 19 and 20).
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: 1 Ally’s Smithed Weapon, or your own smithed weapon.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: 1 Ally’s Smithed Weapon, or your own smithed weapon.

name: Bloodbinder
descriptionHtml: Healers that use their own lifeforce.
descriptionText: Healers that use their own lifeforce.
guideHtml:
As a Bloodbinder, you’ll want to hover around 50% HP to get the most out of Crimson Enmity. It’s a bit of a balancing act, but the extra healing boost is worth it.

Your bread-and-butter combo is Transfusion followed by Blood Drain. Start by healing your allies with Transfusion, then use Blood Drain to top up your own HP. It’s a great way to keep both you and your team in the fight without leaving yourself too vulnerable.

Blood Shield is super effective when you cast it on someone with high guard. It’s a two for one—blocking damage while also healing them up, so it’s a great move when you need to protect and patch up a teammate at the same time.

Life Sacrifice takes you down to just 1 HP, but all that lost health goes directly into healing an ally, which can be a real game-changer when things get rough. If you pair it with Crimson Enmity, you can really boost its impact and turn the tide when your team needs it most.

FAQ:

Q: Does my healing get increased if using the ability lowers my HP below Crimson Enmity threshold as part of the ability? IE: Through Life Sacrifice
A: No, the increased healing threshold is determined at the point the ability is used.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Medicine, Magic or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.
soul: You gain +1 Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Transfusion
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Transfusion.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Transfusion
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You lose HP up to your Toughness x 2. Target ally within range heals for the same amount of HP you lost.

This HP loss cannot be mitigated in any way and ignores temporary HP. This does not trigger effects that would trigger upon taking damage.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You lose HP up to your Toughness x 2. Target ally within range heals for the same amount of HP you lost.

This HP loss cannot be mitigated in any way and ignores temporary HP. This does not trigger effects that would trigger upon taking damage.

ability1Ref.name: Crimson Enmity
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: When your HP is at or below 50% of your max HP, you gain Crimson Enmity. When you would heal an ally or yourself with an ability from this class (Bloodbinder), you heal for 1.5x that amount.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: When your HP is at or below 50% of your max HP, you gain Crimson Enmity. When you would heal an ally or yourself with an ability from this class (Bloodbinder), you heal for 1.5x that amount.

ability2Ref.name: Blood Drain
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light Attack that deals Dark damage against the target. On damage, you recover HP equal to your Toughness + 3.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light Attack that deals Dark damage against the target. On damage, you recover HP equal to your Toughness + 3.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Target must be a living creature. Target cannot be an ally.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Target must be a living creature. Target cannot be an ally.

ability3Ref.name: Blood Shield
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You grant the target 1 RP that must be spent on the Block action against the triggering attack. They gain a +5 bonus to their Guard for this Block action. After the attack, the target regains HP equal to half the damage blocked (Blocked meaning the damage reduced through block, not necessarily your total block value).
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You grant the target 1 RP that must be spent on the Block action against the triggering attack. They gain a +5 bonus to their Guard for this Block action. After the attack, the target regains HP equal to half the damage blocked (Blocked meaning the damage reduced through block, not necessarily your total block value).
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Target is the target of an attack.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Target is the target of an attack.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Life Sacrifice
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You lose HP until your HP reaches 1. Target ally within range recovers that much HP. Any HP that exceeds the target’s max HP becomes temporary HP.

This HP loss cannot be mitigated in any way and ignores temporary HP. This does not trigger effects that would trigger upon taking damage.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You lose HP until your HP reaches 1. Target ally within range recovers that much HP. Any HP that exceeds the target’s max HP becomes temporary HP.

This HP loss cannot be mitigated in any way and ignores temporary HP. This does not trigger effects that would trigger upon taking damage.

name: Bodyguard
guideHtml:
The bodyguard is focused around protecting a single target. You can choose who this target is with VIP Protection. They are well-suited to escort and protection missions, but even in normal ones they synergize well with squishy melee allies. Since they automatically get hit and can't react when using the redirect part of VIP Protection, having high Toughness and wearing armor is recommended.

With Temporary Protection, the bodyguard can pay a heavy mana cost to protect another ally. It should only be used in emergencies, but using it on a low health ally can keep them in the fight without them having to play super defensive.

Shelter is a more limited, but powerful version of the re-direct of the key ability. Heavy Attacks will do a lot of damage if you take them without reacting, so Shelter is best saved for those attacks.

At your side helps you with positioning. It can save your VIP target when they do something stupid or if they get forcefully moved, but it can also be used offensively to teleport next to them when they charge ahead.

Vengeance is a cheaper heavy attack that can be used if your VIP target was attacked. If the condition is met, there's rarely a reason not to use it. It becomes even more powerful if your VIP target is below 0 HP, though you obviously do not want that situation to happen to begin with.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: VIP Protection
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: At the start of each arc, choose one person other than yourself as your VIP for that arc. Your VIP is the target for several of your abilities.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use VIP Protection.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: If you choose a VIP, you cannot become the target of a VIP protection yourself.

associatedAbilityRef.name: VIP Protection
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You change the target of the attack to you instead of your VIP.

This attack automatically hits you and you may not use the Dodge and Block or similar reactions against it. 

The re-directed attack is treated as having most the requirements met as if it was still targeting the original target, including effects that are conditional such as Full Pierce on a downed target, and you are considered to be in the original target's spot for the purposes of range of the attack you're re-directing. 

GM's may rule some conditional effects are based on you rather than the original target, such as attacks with effects based on race.

Does not work against Attacks of Opportunity and cannot be used against an attack that's already redirected.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You change the target of the attack to you instead of your VIP.

This attack automatically hits you and you may not use the Dodge and Block or similar reactions against it.

The re-directed attack is treated as having most the requirements met as if it was still targeting the original target, including effects that are conditional such as Full Pierce on a downed target, and you are considered to be in the original target's spot for the purposes of range of the attack you're re-directing.

GM's may rule some conditional effects are based on you rather than the original target, such as attacks with effects based on race.

Does not work against Attacks of Opportunity and cannot be used against an attack that's already redirected.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Your VIP is within 5ft of you and is the target of an attack that does not target you as well.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: Your VIP is within 5ft of you and is the target of an attack that does not target you as well.

ability1Ref.name: Shelter
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: The attack targeting your VIP instead targets you. You may use this even if you’re also a target of the attack and both attacks will resolve against you. You also gain the benefits of the Block action against the triggering attack.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: The attack targeting your VIP instead targets you. You may use this even if you’re also a target of the attack and both attacks will resolve against you. You also gain the benefits of the Block action against the triggering attack.

ability3Ref.name: Vengeance
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. If your VIP is at 0 HP or lower, this attack gains Lock On and Guard Crush.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. If your VIP is at 0 HP or lower, this attack gains Lock On and Guard Crush.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Your VIP was attacked last turn and you re-directed an attack from them to you or your VIP is downed.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Your VIP was attacked last turn and you re-directed an attack from them to you or your VIP is downed.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: At your side
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You teleport up to 40ft. This must place you next to your VIP.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You teleport up to 40ft. This must place you next to your VIP.

name: Botanist
descriptionHtml:
The frigid passes surrounding Mt. Merlin were inhospitable places. Cutting winds, icy ground, and regular blizzards made them nearly impassable during the winter months, not that dangerous conditions stopped the adventurous from making the trek into the mountains. Small villages populated by hardy men and women were scattered through the passes and valleys. And each had their share of myths and legends passed down through the years. For the village of Cartrmyn, the Princess of the Mountain was theirs.

As the story was told, the Princess of the Mountain influenced the chill, the snow, and the storms of the little valley Cartrmyn was nestled in. Said to have the form of a woman made of ice, offerings were left regularly at the beginning and end of winter. A common game among the children of the village was to stay up into the deep night in an attempt to catch a glimpse of the Princess as she collected the offerings. Gifts of wild berries, mosses, lichens, mushrooms of all kinds, stacks of wood from unknown trees, and unheard of herbs would be given by the Princess in thanks. And the types of gifts exchanged would tell the villagers how the winter would go or&as an apology for a harsh season. When piles of berries and nuts and extra firewood was left, the village knew they were in for a dangerous winter; but gifts of wild flowers and herbs told of a mild winter approaching. By heeding the signs left by the Princess, Cartrmyn was able to thrive in even the most extreme seasons.

However, it wasnt long before word spread about their guardian Princess and those seeking riches and knowledge would come in search of her. For those who were respectful of the village and of the mountain, they would leave with wisdom and the grace of the Princess and would forever find safe passage in the winter chill. But for those with greed in eyes and evil in their hearts, the Princess was as unforgiving as a sudden winter storm. The unearthly shrieks of those that crossed her echoed across the mountain side like the roars of a hundred mountain lions and the gnashing of a thousand teeth, a dire warning to all that would cross--

Miyu closed the book with a sharp snap and sighed. It was just a chainsaw, she complained to no one. And why do they make it sound like Im hundreds of years old?! Grudgingly, she tucked the book back into the pack that had been prepared by the villagers. Dried meats and fish, metal tools and knives, and a small collection of glowing crystals that she could use to produce more magical fuels. In return, she had laid out hand woven baskets of whatever shed been able to find over the last week that she wasnt keeping for herself. Berries, tubers, and bundles of medicinal flowers.

Why is it so hard to get people to leave me alone?

Why is it so hard to get people to leave me alone?
guideHtml:
The botanist, like the forager, is a gathering and utility class. Their only in combat ability is Felling Strike. At first glance it may look like a normal knockback move, but you get to move before doing the knockback, which lets you more easily do things like push enemies of ledges or knock an enemy back into your team.

Craft Pemmican lets you convert food units into rations without the cost of any actions. These rations are easier to carry, don't require a kit to prepare and less likely to be ruined. It's a nice little bonus and there's little reason not to do it whenever you can.

During gathering, Memory of the Grove synergizes with the forager abilities as well as the other botanist ability, Stalks of Comparison. Memory can be used in several ways, you can reroll a very low dice to get a better roll, but you can also use it on a higher value one to store it for later use with Stalks or Divining Petalfall.

For stalks of comparison, it's always a good ability since it can apply to either Node points or Lucky points. Getting the bonus for it can be made easier or even guaranteed with the use of other abilities. Using the stored value of memory along with the pre-determined rolls of Verdant Instinct can get you that bonus.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Reason or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Toughness or Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Botany Study
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain the ability to detect if foraged food is poisonous or unsafe to eat, nearly guaranteeing its safety.  If it is a common poison, you can also identify its effect.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You gain the ability to properly process some poisonous mushrooms, fruits and herbs, making them edible as a Camp Action.  You can process up to 100u at a time.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You gain the ability to harvest Tier 2 Foraging Outcrops.
keyAbilityRef.benefit4: You gain proficiency in Artifice Chainsaws and may use it as a gathering tool.

ability1Ref.name: Memory of the Grove
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: After making a gathering check, you may choose to reroll the die. If you do, store the original die result. You may replace a future gathering die result (before bonus) with the stored value.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: After making a gathering check, you may choose to reroll the die. If you do, store the original die result. You may replace a future gathering die result (before bonus) with the stored value.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Usable after making a gathering a check.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Usable after making a gathering a check.

ability2Ref.name: Trail Mix
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: For a camp action you create up to 5 servings of a trail meal.  It costs 20u of food per serving and only gives 1 mana (0 for Mirane), but it keeps starvation away.  Does not require a culinary kit, and does not count as cooking for the purposes of culinary actions such as using stocks.

Does not require a culinary kit, and does not count as cooking for the purposes of culinary actions such as using stocks.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: 20u of Food Per Serving
ability2Ref.requirementText: 20u of Food Per Serving

ability3Ref.name: Stalks of Comparison
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: Make a gathering check and apply it to either Node Points or Lucky Points. If the result of this check (before bonuses) is higher than your previous roll this session, gain a bonus to the result equal to your Foraging skill (max +10).
ability3Ref.descriptionText: Make a gathering check and apply it to either Node Points or Lucky Points. If the result of this check (before bonuses) is higher than your previous roll this session, gain a bonus to the result equal to your Foraging skill (max +10).
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Costs 1 Strike Dice
ability3Ref.requirementText: Costs 1 Strike Dice

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Forager's Eye
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Initiate a sight and sound based search for hidden plants, fungi, creatures or living enemy units in a 30x30 area within range.  You become automatically aware of the targeted species of plants, fungi or creatures or you can choose to be aware of all hostile enemy units.  You must have line of sight to the target in order to spot them.  After revealing the target your Lifebeat Sense gains (if used immediately after):

Combo:  If used after Forager's Eye, the AP and mana cost of this is reduced by 1 and the target's location is known to you and your allies until the end of your next turn. This removes Hiding from them.

name: Brawler
descriptionHtml: Scrappy fighters that are willing to get into the fray and trade blows.
descriptionText: Scrappy fighters that are willing to get into the fray and trade blows.
guideHtml:
A brawler is a scrappy fighter that takes hits to dish them out.

Counter Stance is a risky stance in which you cannot react to attacks through normal means, but you get to make a counter attack afterwards. A brawler has to carefully weigh his options before entering the stance, as he cannot end it until his next turn starts. One option is to only use it when you are already low on RP, as being unable to Dodge or Block has little effect when you lack the RP to do so to begin with.

To help survive while countering, a brawler has access to Vital Drive II. While the healing itself isn't the most efficient, being able to use it as reaction allows you to stay standing after attacks that should have knocked you out.

Suckerpunch is an amazing opening move, if you can land it. It can only be used when already in melee range of an enemy at the start of the encounter, so getting up in someone's face before you fight them is a good move.

Knockout Punch instantly takes out grunt level enemies and leaves them alive, which sets it apart from similar abilities. Useful for when you need to interrogate them after. The 4 AP cost for a heavy attack makes it risky to use against enemies who still have RP left, so ideally you let your allies set you up before using it.

Wind-up punch is an attack meant to punch through tough guards. While it lacks the full pierce or guard crush properties, by adding all the damage together into 1 big attack, the guard is less valuable than what is it applied multiple times.

keyAbilityRef.name: Shrug it off
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Vital Drive II.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Vital Drive II
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You regain HP equal to five times the mana spent. Vital Drive II cannot heal more than the damage you took from the triggering attack.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You regain HP equal to five times the mana spent. Vital Drive II cannot heal more than the damage you took from the triggering attack.

ability1Ref.name: Counter Stance
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You enter a stance in which you freely counterattack against enemies that attack you. After an attack against you has resolved, you may make a Melee Attack of Opportunity against that target. While in this stance, you cannot use the Dodge or Block reactions.

You may spend 0 AP or 1 RP to cancel this stance. It otherwise lasts until you enter another stance.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You enter a stance in which you freely counterattack against enemies that attack you. After an attack against you has resolved, you may make a Melee Attack of Opportunity against that target. While in this stance, you cannot use the Dodge or Block reactions.

You may spend 0 AP or 1 RP to cancel this stance. It otherwise lasts until you enter another stance.

ability2Ref.name: Wind-up Punch
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy attack against the target. Overcharge: You may spend additional AP when using this ability to deal an additional Light Damage per AP used. Add all of this damage together before applying damage reduction from Guard or abilities.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy attack against the target. Overcharge: You may spend additional AP when using this ability to deal an additional Light Damage per AP used. Add all of this damage together before applying damage reduction from Guard or abilities.

ability3Ref.name: Suckerpunch
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Unbalanced.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Unbalanced.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Knockout Punch
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage if the enemy is a Grunt, they immediately drop to 0 HP and fall unconscious.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage if the enemy is a Grunt, they immediately drop to 0 HP and fall unconscious.

name: Cannoneer
descriptionHtml:
Pop. Pop. Pop.

Bix shook her flare pistol to dislodge the stuck casing and then hastily stuffed it back into its holster once the spent case landed with a soft thud on the ground. Above, a trio of flares slowly fell back toward the earth under little parachutes, each burning brightly with a different color. It was the third series of flares shed sent up and these last three would be the final signals for the artillery battery miles away to get their firing solutions set. She brought her binoculars up to her eyes, knocking her hat off in her haste, scanning the terrain in the distance until she caught sight of the enemy Colors. It was so strange to see Northi Mage and Spear Corps forming up alongside Westrian Cavalry and Knights. And all of it being organized by a Westrian paladin.

She could see skirmisher elements already rushing toward her position, the flares she was using to signal the artillery obviously pointing directly to her own position. But its what was needed. Out of habit, she reached back over her shoulder and unhooked the handset for her radio and pressed the stud to send.

Comm-check, comm-check. Bix, three-three-seven-one-four. Comm-check, comm-check.

She could feel the whir of the crystal core spin up in the pack but there was only faint static when she released the transmit stud to wait for any reply. Something the catfolk were doing had been disrupting communications ever since theyd crossed the border in Jav. Combine that with some new type of air magic that had taken out entire flights of aerial mages and&well, there was a reason she was stuck miles out in front of the lines using a flare gun of all things to give basic ranging details. Blessed Kari, signaling in shell fall and corrections was going to be the worst.

Behind her, the deep throoms of Helenas heavy battery sounded as the first guns began their ranging shots based on her flares. The shells shrieking overhead rattled her bones and the trailing energy of the condensed mana leaking from containment made them look like shooting stars. They hit the ground like them, too. Ground shattering explosions behind the enemy formations. Overshot. And the enemy started moving to make the ranging even harder. Smart, but she knew what she was doing and its not like they could move very far and in ways she couldnt predict.

Binoculars stowed as she began pulling out flare cartridges, laying them in the dirt in front of her to compose her message.

That should do it. And a little bit of lead for that redeployment, she said with a pleased smile. Then she began loading and firing the flares in sequence. Pop. Pop. Pop. Pop.

By the time she had fired the last flare, a gleaming crimson that indicated full barrage, fire for effect, she was about to have her own problem and little war to fight. A skirmisher unit was finally getting close, the catfolk spearmen rushing toward her in a distance devouring four-limbed sprint. Well, they wouldnt be able to get close enough to be a threat. She was an artillery woman after all.

With a grunt, she hefted her cannon from the ground. It was massive, nearly as tall as she was. In some other life, something like it had probably been mounted to a gun carriage. But this one was specially designed to be carried and fired by one person. She gripped the handles tightly and angled the barrel toward the approaching catfolk. Her cannon roared just as the battery behind her unleashed its full fury. Huge mana explosions silhouetted her targets as her shells landed among them, spraying dirt, stone, and red hot metal fragments in all directions. She watched for a moment as the dust settled and nodded in satisfaction. Direct hit. They were either dead or too wounded to be her problem.

She set her cannon down and pulled out her binoculars again, to examine the damage of the main barrage. Looked like it had ripped through the Westrian lines, scattering their knights and cavalry. She took out her flare pistol and signaled good hit adjust right 3 fire for effect. That should take care of those Northi units nicely.

She would make these invaders regret coming into her home and threatening her family.

guideHtml:
The cannoneer is a simple striker class that uses the cannon artifice weapon.

Rocket Jump is a low distance sidestep with the noticeable quality of not reducing the range of your cannon like normal movement would.

Cannonball is a double heavy attack put into a single ability and basically your way to basic attack without caring about cannon limitations.

High Power Shell is both a minor control tool by pushing an enemy back, but also a self peel tool by allowing you force yourself backwards. Note that the attack that pushes you still provokes from anyone next to you.

Armor Crushing Shell is a simple ability that helps you an your team do more damage to that target. 

Quantum Bomb is a 1 AP heavy that comes at the cost of it being a Setup move. Setup moves can leave you vulnerable, but as cannons have large range you may find yourself being able to use it more than you'd think.

You make a Heavy attack against the target. On damage, they are pushed back 10ft.

You are pushed back 5ft away from the target regardless of whether it hits or not.

name: Carpenter
descriptionHtml:
On Sorthen's Marlone coast, where Lizardfolk pilehouses rise above the crashing waves, to the spires of Merlin's Academy of magic in Northi—wherever Carpenters go, civilization follows.

Since the first dawn of Lyr, these artisans were the backbone of civilization.  Between their awl, saw and hammer they built up military camps into fortresses and small villages into thriving metropolises.

Carpenters are far from just competent in their art and tools, they also understand that cooperation is necessary for great works.  It doesn't matter if its to erect a guildhall estate or to confront the threat of a siege of hobgoblin raiders—Carpenters always work together with their allies like hammer and nail.

Crafting far more than just still buildings, their carvings can be seen in hand and belt for many denizens of the realm.  From the first wooden tools, to finely tuned bows from the finest Yew—their equipment are trusted by all professions.  Expert carpenters also have the understanding of specialized oil coatings, imbuing their creations with special material qualities.

In every community, these artisans are known to be indispensable building blocks of society who work tirelessly to push civilization forward, one nail at a time.

It doesn't matter if its to erect a guildhall estate or to confront the threat of a siege of hobgoblin raiders—Carpenters always work together with their allies like hammer and nail.

Crafting far more than just still buildings, their carvings can be seen in hand and belt for many denizens of the realm.  From the first wooden tools, to finely tuned bows from the finest Yew—their equipment are trusted by all professions.  Expert carpenters also have the understanding of specialized oil coatings, imbuing their creations with special material qualities.

In every community, these artisans are known to be indispensable building blocks of society who work tirelessly to push civilization forward, one nail at a time.
guideHtml:
The carpenter is a crafting class that performs like a striker in combat. With Hammer and Nail, you get the option to do guaranteed damage at the cost of some RP and mana pretty frequently. Useful for ensuring you can finish of weakened enemies.

Felling Strike may look like a normal knockback move, but you get to move before doing the knockback, which lets you more easily do things like push enemies of ledges or knock an enemy back into your team.

Repair Wood Weapon is a simple repair ability. In the zone is a crafting ability that you want to use as late as possible. If you're not using another finishing move such as Standard Finish, it's best to always save it for the last dice. Gains more value the higher quality materials you use, as each extra dice you add to the total gives a +1.

keyAbilityRef.name: Carpenter Basics
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You can use a carpenters chisel as a crafting tool for carpenters crafts and buildings.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You can craft using Pine and Maple, as well as alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You gain proficiency in axes. You also gain proficiency in either bows or crossbows.
keyAbilityRef.benefit4: You can use the Carpenter's core crafting abilities.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Carpenter Basics
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Carpenter's Modification A
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an A-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Joining and Assembling
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the carpentry project. Each material has its own crafting point cost.

Create Oil Coating
Cost: Errand
Description:
You create an Oil Coating from Carpenter materials.  See Items: Carpenter Materials.

Create Wood Block
Cost: Errand
Description:
You create a Wood Block from Carpenter materials.  See Items: Carpenter Materials.

Infuse Wand
Cost: 5 Crafting Points
Description: 
You add a drop of Oil Coating into a wand, changing the built in damage type to the element of the oil. Only works with oils that change the damage type. Does not consume the oil.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Felling Strike
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: Make a heavy attack against a target enemy that is no larger than large size category. On damage, sidestep to any unoccupied space next to the target, of the same elevation as the target, and knockback the target 10ft.

name: Cavalier
descriptionHtml:
The origin of mounted combat and cavalry specifically has been debated among historians since nomadic riders hunted game across the Axian Plains. Cavalry, as it’s recognizably known in a more modern sense, was first organized by humans, utilizing their more advanced techniques of animal husbandry to domesticate and breed horses into mounts and beasts of burden. With their reliance on various animals for labor, humanity had a head start and also had a distinct need for increased mobility on the battlefield. With open plains and rolling hills making up a generous portion of their homeland, humans were at a distinct disadvantage in mobility to the Chimera of Northi.

In the long conflicts before the advent of Paladins, Westrian cavalry stood as the only near parity force humans could deploy against Northi and Sorthen. Light scouts and mounted archers were the primary uses of cavalry at the time to both maximize the strengths of mounted soldiers and to preserve both the precious skills of the riders and their trained mounts. While there have been many techniques surrounding cavalry over the centuries, modern understanding has codified the most basic of elements into the art of the Cavalier. 

Today, the use of cavalry has spread to every corner of Lyr. No matter the type of mount, be it horse, wyvern, or even the humble dragonfly, the Cavalier lays the foundation for all cavalry techniques.

guideHtml:
The cavalier is a mounted combatant that excels in wide open plains. Being a tier 1 class, you get some proficiencies with Lance being the stand out one. It's a speciality weapon that can only be used while mounted, but it's effectively a polearm with a potential for increased damage. Make sure you always carry a backup weapon in case you are forced to fight dismounted.

Because mounts are such an important part of the cavalier, your mount choice will matter a lot. A horse is the most common choice (or a pony for smaller races). It is the fastest land mount, but it can only move in a straight line and has little other special qualities. The boar is the optimal choice for smaller races, as it will buff their trample and make it work against medium creatures. Wolf is the slowest of the three, but it is not restricted to line movement and has a special attack it can use. For most cavaliers, the horse is the best choice as it will make it easier to activate your Trample.

The core ability of the cavalier is Trample. It requires you to build up some speed by moving, but once you do you can trample a large amount of enemies in a single movement. It's a sure hit attack that knocks enemies prone, making it extremely punishing if it lands. It does however have some counters to be aware of. First being that you can't trample enemies that are the same size as you, so large enemies are unaffected. Secondly that the enemy can block your attack to stop it. Attempting a direct charge through the enemy tank may not work out very well.

To help with hit and run attacks, you get Canter. It must be used immediately after attacking, but it lets you sidestep 10ft which can let you move out of range of the enemy. After your out of range, you can then reposition and prepare for another charge.

Rider & Mount as one is a passive ability that lets you re-direct attacks to you instead of your mount. Mounts are not as durable as you are, so you will want to almost always use this. AoE attacks cannot be re-directed with this, so be careful of those. It also doesn't work if you're using a flying mount, so you'll want to stick with land based ones.

Horse Whistle is what you can use to get your mount back to you after you're dismounted. This lets you use other abilities such as Faerie Flash or abilities from other classes to temporarily get off your mount without having to walk back to it. 

Spirit Jump can be used as an anti-air tool, but it can also be used to help your mount maneuver across difficult terrain such as cliffs or wide pits.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Animal Husbandry, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Mounted Basics
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain proficiency in Polearms, Lances, Light armor, Shields and one other common weapon group of your choice.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You gain 5 skill points in Riding. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You may use Spirit Jump.
keyAbilityRef.benefit4: If you have mastered cavalier, your mount can withstand one lethal blow. If your mount would be reduced to 0 HP or below by an attack, it instead is reduced to 1 HP. Only works once per encounter.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Spirit Jump
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You and your mount jump up to 60ft vertically straight up into the air.  You can additionally move 5ft horizontally afterwards.  Unless your mount can fly, you automatically land straight down from your position at the end of your turn or for 0 AP, this falling movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity and you do not take fall damage from it.  You cannot make any non-teleport movement actions while in the air, but can still take other actions (such as attacking) while airborne.

You may trade height to move horizontally as part of the initial jump, moving 5ft horizontally as part of the jump in exchange for 5ft of height. You may move up to 40ft extra horizontally with this.

Instead of spending mana, you may instead spend a minute preparing this ability before activating it. When you do, this ability costs 0 mana.

You cannot make any non-teleport movement actions while in the air, but can still take other actions (such as attacking) while airborne.

You may trade height to move horizontally as part of the initial jump, moving 5ft horizontally as part of the jump in exchange for 5ft of height. You may move up to 40ft extra horizontally with this.

ability1Ref.name: Canter
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You may Sidestep 10ft.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You may Sidestep 10ft.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must be mounted and can be used immediately after making a weapon attack.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must be mounted and can be used immediately after making a weapon attack.

ability2Ref.name: Horse Whistle
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: Your mount (the last mount you were riding on this arc) may sidestep up to its speed towards you and it gains Trample (as per the ability, if you have it) for this movement. If this movement moves your mount onto your square, you may mount it for 0 RP. If you were prone and mount your mount, you are no longer prone.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: Your mount (the last mount you were riding on this arc) may sidestep up to its speed towards you and it gains Trample (as per the ability, if you have it) for this movement. If this movement moves your mount onto your square, you may mount it for 0 RP. If you were prone and mount your mount, you are no longer prone.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Must NOT be mounted.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Must NOT be mounted.

ability3Ref.name: Rider & Mount As One
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: While you are mounted and not flying you and your mount are considered the same entity for the purposes of what can target you. This means that while you are mounted, your mount cannot be targeted by anything (including allied effects) unless the effect specifically says it does. Any debuff or buff on you also affects your mount's movement and speed as if it was placed on the mount.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: While you are mounted and not flying you and your mount are considered the same entity for the purposes of what can target you. This means that while you are mounted, your mount cannot be targeted by anything (including allied effects) unless the effect specifically says it does. Any debuff or buff on you also affects your mount's movement and speed as if it was placed on the mount.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Trample
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You may activate this after moving at least 20ft in a movement action to gain Trample until you stop moving. Abilities with the Teleport keyword do not apply for this, but declaring multiple AP to move at the same time counts as 1 action. While under the effects of trample, you don't provoke attacks of opportunity by moving and you can move into enemies that are at least one size smaller than your mount. An enemy may spend 1 RP to move out of the way to an empty spot within 5ft of them.

If they don’t move out of the way, you make a Sure Hit Light attack against them with your mount, using your mount’s stats. On damage, you knock them prone into the square you came from and keep moving, occupying the square they were in. Otherwise your movement action ends.

Each enemy can only be affected by trample once per activation of the ability, regardless of whether they are attacked or move out of it.
