name: Chronofighter
guideHtml:
Chronofighter is a complex class that relies in setting up future turns to take advantage of attacks from the past.

Attacks from the past is a difficult ability to use. Because everything is exactly mirrored based of the position of your previous turn and you cannot move before using it, it comes down to planning and a bit of luck to make good use of. However, when it does, the result is amazing. Being able to double your attacks are almost no cost is incredibly powerful.

Accelerating Strike is a tool that should be combined with the Delay encounter start action, which is free for a Chronofighter. This lets you loop enemies at a crucial point, letting you take two turns in a row before they can respond.

Delayed Hit is a good tool getting around temporary guard buffs. The target can still Block the damage when it hits the turn later, but less intelligent creatures may get faked out by the weird nature of the attack and try to react to it from the start.

Butterfly Effect is a ramping ability. The initial mana cost is extremely high and it is not until the third use that it becomes a good use. The total mana cost before it becomes free is 9 mana and it takes at least 3 turns of stacking. Keep this in mind before using it, as it is a heavy commitment and a big waste of mana if you can't get max value out of it. When a fight does last long though and you get to keep stacking it, it is one of the most powerful abilities that exists.

keyAbilityRef.name: Past Self
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Past Self.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: Using the Delay encounter start action does not consume your encounter start action use and may use it multiple times.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Past Self
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You place a past self token at your current position that lasts until the end of the encounter. Your past self tokens are used for other abilities and cannot be attacked normally.

When you make a weapon attack, you may have the attack come from a past self token. After a past self token attacks, it becomes targetable by attacks and effects for 1 round as if you were in that position though you cannot be hit more than once by the same attack because of this.

Your past selves cannot be moved, willingly or forcefully.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You place a past self token at your current position that lasts until the end of the encounter. Your past self tokens are used for other abilities and cannot be attacked normally.

When you make a weapon attack, you may have the attack come from a past self token. After a past self token attacks, it becomes targetable by attacks and effects for 1 round as if you were in that position though you cannot be hit more than once by the same attack because of this.

Your past selves cannot be moved, willingly or forcefully.

ability1Ref.name: Accelerating Strike
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy Attack against the target. Afterwards, you gain Accelerating Strike, which increases your Initiative by up to 2.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy Attack against the target. Afterwards, you gain Accelerating Strike, which increases your Initiative by up to 2.

ability2Ref.name: Delayed Hit
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy Attack against the target, but don’t deal damage. On hit, even if it was guarded, the target takes Heavy Damage at the start of your next turn. (This delayed damage is calculated of the target’s Guard at the time it’s applied, not when the attack hit)
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy Attack against the target, but don’t deal damage. On hit, even if it was guarded, the target takes Heavy Damage at the start of your next turn. (This delayed damage is calculated of the target’s Guard at the time it’s applied, not when the attack hit)

ability3Ref.name: Attacks from the Past
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
After making a melee weapon attack, you may repeat the attack from one of your past self tokens. You may target the same enemy as your original attack. Cannot repeat Secret Arts or other attacks with a once per combat/arc etc type limitation.

Can only repeat attacks with a base cost of 2 AP or lower and any mana cost must be paid as normal.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
After making a melee weapon attack, you may repeat the attack from one of your past self tokens. You may target the same enemy as your original attack. Cannot repeat Secret Arts or other attacks with a once per combat/arc etc type limitation.

Can only repeat attacks with a base cost of 2 AP or lower and any mana cost must be paid as normal.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Everything, Everywhere, All At Once
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You make a Heavy Attack against the enemy. 

You may then repeat this attack from each of your past selves and may target the same enemy multiple times.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You make a Heavy Attack against the enemy.

You may then repeat this attack from each of your past selves and may target the same enemy multiple times.

name: Chronomancer
descriptionHtml:
Chronomancers are rare mages who wield the mysterious power of time itself. They can slow down moments, speed them up, or even rewind them, turning the tide of battle in ways that few can predict or understand. In a world where such magic is almost unheard of, Chronomancers stand out as an often feared force.

Their magic traces back to one source: Chroma-Daria Lichtvog, a wandering Witch and Thaumaturge who’s been around for over a thousand years—at least, that’s what the stories say. Daria teaches her craft on a whim, picking students when it suits her. Most Chronomancers have either learned directly from her or stumbled across her ancient writings, devoting their lives to uncovering the secrets within.

Running into a Chronomancer means you’re up against someone who can twist the flow of time itself. Whether they’ve been taught by the Witch herself or have painstakingly studied her elusive texts, their powers are as astonishing as they are unsettling.

In a world where such magic is almost unheard of, Chronomancers stand out as an often feared force.

Their magic traces back to one source: Chroma-Daria Lichtvog, a wandering Witch and Thaumaturge who’s been around for over a thousand years—at least, that’s what the stories say. Daria teaches her craft on a whim, picking students when it suits her. Most Chronomancers have either learned directly from her or stumbled across her ancient writings, devoting their lives to uncovering the secrets within.

Running into a Chronomancer means you’re up against someone who can twist the flow of time itself. Whether they’ve been taught by the Witch herself or have painstakingly studied her elusive texts, their powers are as astonishing as they are unsettling.
guideHtml:
Chronomancers are all about controlling the battlefield, and their abilities make them a vital part of any team. When you’re not sure what move to make, use Store Time to bank it for later.  Having one extra action the next turn can open up powerful combinations, or help to strike during a more opportune time.

Slow is a go-to for dealing with those quick and slippery foes. It never misses, so you can lock them down or force them to waste their precious resources on defense. Accelerando is a lifesaver for a teammate who got a bad roll on initiative or just needs a speed boost to make an important move.

The heart of a Chronomancer’s toolkit is Haste. This ability lets your ally get the most out of their buffs or strike during a crucial moment. In a pinch, Galaxy Stop is your trump card. It time stops all the grunt enemies on the battlefield, giving your party the breathing room to either regroup or take out a few foes while they’re defenseless.

While a Chronomancer is great at supporting and controlling the flow of battle, they don’t do so well on their own. They shine best when they’re working in tandem with their allies in nearly any party setup, using their versatile abilities to keep momentum.

keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You can use Store Time.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Store Time
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You gain Store Time, granting you 1 extra AP at the start of your next turn.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You gain Store Time, granting you 1 extra AP at the start of your next turn.

ability1Ref.name: Slow
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: The target is slowed until the start of your next turn unless the target pays 1 RP to resist this effect.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: The target is slowed until the start of your next turn unless the target pays 1 RP to resist this effect.

ability2Ref.name: Accelerando
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: Grant yourself or an ally +4 initiative.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: Grant yourself or an ally +4 initiative.

ability3Ref.name: Haste
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: Grants the target Haste, granting them 2 AP for the rest of this turn.  A target can only be affected by haste once per turn.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: Grants the target Haste, granting them 2 AP for the rest of this turn.  A target can only be affected by haste once per turn.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Galaxy Stop
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You create a space-time anomaly that freezes time itself in a local area for a short while, affecting all but the strong-willed.  All non-heroic units in range cannot take any actions until the end of their next turn.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You create a space-time anomaly that freezes time itself in a local area for a short while, affecting all but the strong-willed.  All non-heroic units in range cannot take any actions until the end of their next turn.

name: Colossus
descriptionHtml: A warrior that fights with two heavy weapons to crush their enemies.
descriptionText: A warrior that fights with two heavy weapons to crush their enemies.
requirementsHtml: Any tier 2 class mastered. Adventurer or Fighter mastered.
requirementsText: Any tier 2 class mastered. Adventurer or Fighter mastered.
guideHtml:
A colossus wants to use heavy weapons and Titan's Grip means that there is very little reason not to do so. They can use Mighty Leap get into range of an enemy and disrupt all enemies around the target, allowing the Colossus to engage into groups of enemies without severe risk. You can also jump to an empty spot if your goal is just to land an AoE unbalanced debuff.

Double Slam is two hit combo of which every hit can inflict a debuff on the enemy. You can combo it after apply Unbalanced with mighty leap for a double chance to knock someone prone.

Two Sword Crush is a powerful ability that lets you push past the guard of an enemy. Slam and Double Slam both only apply their effects on damage and are light attack, making an enemy likely to Block against it. If their guard isn't super high, you can then use Two Sword Crush to do more damage than their Block and apply the effect.

Climax Art: Titan's Blade is a powerful AoE attack that will annihilate people that cannot avoid the initial strike. Can be used as a finisher, but also an opening move to immediately burn some RP from enemies if they line up for you.

keyAbilityRef.name: Titan's Grip
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may wield a Two-Handed weapon in 1 hand allowing you to use a second (Two-Handed) weapon or shield in your offhand.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: While dual wielding Two-Handed weapons, you may make dual wield attacks as if they were One-Handed weapons.

ability1Ref.name: Mighty Leap
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You jump to a target within range and may make a Light attack against them. All enemies within 5ft of the target must make a save or become Unbalanced.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You jump to a target within range and may make a Light attack against them. All enemies within 5ft of the target must make a save or become Unbalanced.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Two-Handed melee weapons only.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Two-Handed melee weapons only.

ability2Ref.name: Double Slam
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may make up to 2 Light attacks against the target, paying 1 AP and 1 Mana for each. On damage of either hit, the target becomes Unbalanced. If the target is already unbalanced, they are knocked prone.

If you are wielding only 1 Two-Handed weapon, you are limited to 1 attack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:

If you are wielding only 1 Two-Handed weapon, you are limited to 1 attack.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Two-Handed weapons only
ability2Ref.requirementText: Two-Handed weapons only

ability3Ref.name: Two Sword Crush
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may add your Light damage to the attack.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may add your Light damage to the attack.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Can be used after hitting an attack with a Two-Handed melee weapon. Must be dual wielding a second Two-Handed melee weapon.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Can be used after hitting an attack with a Two-Handed melee weapon. Must be dual wielding a second Two-Handed melee weapon.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Titan Blade
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You greatly increase the size and weight of your weapons, then slam them down in a direction. You make a melee Heavy attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide line up to the range. On damage, a target is knocked prone.

 

After the initial hit, a shockwave follows. You make another Heavy attack against any enemy still in the original line’s range. This second attack has the Sure Hit and Full Pierce qualities against a prone target.

ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Must be wielding two Two-Handed melee weapons.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Must be wielding two Two-Handed melee weapons.

name: Cryomancer
requirementsText: Any class mastered or Frost mastery.
skills: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to your Focus, Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Ice Cold
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You are immune to Slows and difficult terrain effects with the Frost keyword.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You are immune to regular weather effects involving cold.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You gain Frost Mastery.  Mastery of an element allows you to use it in Channeling weapon attacks and use it to perform minor tricks relating to it.

ability1Ref.name: Icicle Nail
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You fire a shard of ice at a target unit, making a Light Attack that deals Frost Damage. On hit, the target is Slowed until the end of their next turn.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You fire a shard of ice at a target unit, making a Light Attack that deals Frost Damage. On hit, the target is Slowed until the end of their next turn.

ability3Ref.name: Glacial Wall
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You create 4 * X connected ice structures that are 5ft x 5ft cubes that last for up to 24 hours. These cubes have HP equal to 20 + Focus. These structures can function as low cover. Ice Wall segments take double damage from Fire Attacks.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You create 4 * X connected ice structures that are 5ft x 5ft cubes that last for up to 24 hours. These cubes have HP equal to 20 + Focus. These structures can function as low cover. Ice Wall segments take double damage from Fire Attacks.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Frost Coffin
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You cast a Spell that flash freezes a target Grunt, killing them instantly and creates a two Glacial Wall cube stack at their location. These cubes have HP equal to 15 + Focus. These structures can function as cover. Ice wall segments take double damage from Fire Attacks.

These cubes have HP equal to 15 + Focus. These structures can function as cover. Ice wall segments take double damage from Fire Attacks.

name: Culinarian
descriptionHtml:
The clamour of half a dozen meals cooking at once, pots and skillets sizzling along with the clank of utensils frantically flipping or stirring. A chaotic dance of food preparation found in the busiest cities in Lyr. 

The quiet crackle of a campfire under the stars, a small pot bubbling gently, wafting steam to mix with the smoke of the fire above. A simple stew nearly ready to be served to friends resting from a day of adventure.

Tense concentration as an exotic dish was prepared with an eye for presentation. A drizzle of sauce here, an edible garnish there. The stark white of the plate a spotlight of contrast to draw attention to the artful arrangement fit for a king.

Culinarians came from all walks of life and could be found preparing meals from the austere kitchens of Northi royalty to a simple farming cottage in Sorthen. They shouldn’t be mistaken for anyone able to light a fire and char a slab of meat on a stick. Cooking was their passion and their art. Ingredients simple and elegant, mixed with skill and care, could become soul warming meals. After a day of hiking up a mountain pass, sailing through a storm, or even completing a day of schooling, the comfort of a well cooked meal was bliss beyond words.

guideHtml:
The culinarian is a crafter and controller hybrid that uses various cooking tools to annoy its enemies.

Oil Slick is an incredibly powerful zone control tool. It forces an enemy to move only 5ft per action or fall prone, effectively working as a double difficult terrain. By tossing it directly on someone, if they want to move towards you they have to spend at least 2 AP to leave the oil making it a pretty AP efficient trade. It does fail against enemies who can teleport or fly though.

Pepper Spray is another powerful tool, giving a guaranteed blind that can be used defensively as well. Blind makes an attack a lot more likely to miss, making up for RP you would have spent  on dodging an attack. It can also be used on your turn for no mana cost. It also hits your allies though, so be wary of that.

On the crafting side, Perfect Seasoning is just a free bonus you can use whenever you add in an ingredient. You're always going to be adding at least 1 and may even get multiple uses out of it when using Stir Layered Flavors.

Craft Pemmican lets you convert food units into rations without the cost of any actions. These rations are easier to carry, don't require a kit to prepare and less likely to be ruined. It's a nice little bonus and there's little reason not to do it whenever you can.

skills: You gain +5 skill points in Culinary. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Art, Medicine, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Culinary Basics
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Culinarian Core Crafting Abilities
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You can craft stocks using advanced and base materials. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You can use a Frying Pan as a crafting tool for culinarian crafts. You may also use it as a Light Bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient.
keyAbilityRef.benefit4: You gain proficiency in small weapons.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Culinary Basics
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Mushroom Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Mushroom Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Focus Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours. Requires 1 Box of Fresh Mushrooms. 

Meaty Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Meaty Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Power Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 Box of Fresh Meat.

Fish Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Fish Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Toughness Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 Box of Fresh Fish.

Vegetable Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Vegetable Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Agility Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 Box of Fresh Vegetables.

Spicy Stock
Cost: 30 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Spicy Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Crafting Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 bundle of Spicy Peppers.

Herbal Stock
Cost: 30 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Herbal Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Gathering Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours. Requires 1 bundle of Fresh Cooking Herbs. When gathering from a node with a bonus from this stock, you keep the benefit for that entire node unless you go a day without harvesting from that node.

Stir Layered Flavors
Cost: 30 Crafting Points
Description: 
Allows you to add another flavor, allowing you to add a second different flavor.  Choose from Mushroom, Meaty, Fish, Vegetable, Spicy or Herbal—then add the corresponding ingredient.  For example:  A Mushroom stock + Meaty stock becomes a Mushroom-Meaty stock that grants +1 Power / +1 Focus Food Bonus.  

Use Every Last Drop
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description:  
Increases the stock yield by 2.

Secret Ingredient
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the stock.  Each material has its own crafting point cost.

Create Box or Bundle of Food
Cost: Errand.
Description: 
You create a perfect mix of ingredients that would be worthy of a stock with 500u of matching material.  Example:  500u of Meat can make 1 Box of Fresh Meat.  You can make 5 boxes of any kind for one errand action.

ability1Ref.name: Perfect Seasoning
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: Make a crafting check with a +3 Bonus.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: Make a crafting check with a +3 Bonus.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must be in a crafting session. Must have added a special ingredient (box of food, alloy materials) in your last action.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must be in a crafting session. Must have added a special ingredient (box of food, alloy materials) in your last action.

ability2Ref.name: Oil Slick
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You throw a bottle of oil on the ground within range, creating a 15x15 area of slippery oil. If the target contacts the oil at any point of movement (even the start or end) and they moved more than 5ft in a single movement action, they immediately fall prone. The slick lasts until the start of your next turn.

If the target contacts the oil at any point of movement (even the start or end) and they moved more than 5ft in a single movement action, they immediately fall prone. The slick lasts until the start of your next turn.

ability3Ref.name: Craft Pemmican
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
Errand. You turn up to 1000u of food into 25 Pemmican Bars (40u of food per bar), which are incredibly shelf stable and easy to eat.  They perform about the same as Military Rations but they are wrapped by special leaves that deter pests—useful and consistent adventure food.

You may also use this as an IP action to convert up to 2000u of food into 50 Pemmican bars.

You may also use this as an IP action to convert up to 2000u of food into 50 Pemmican bars.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Pepper Spray
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You reach into your pocket and pull out a handful of ground chili pepper that you throw in front of you in a 15ft cube. Inflicts all targets with Pepper Spray, causing Blinded 4 and the inability to smell.  The target or an ally of the target within 5ft can spend 1 AP to wipe it off. Otherwise the effect ends at the end of the target’s next turn.

You may spend 1 RP and 1 additional mana instead of paying the AP cost.

name: Cultivator
descriptionHtml:
To cultivate is not merely to gather power, but to decide how one will exist within it. While Ki can be sensed and refined by many, a cultivator distinguishes themselves by the principles they choose to shape that power around.

Every cultivator begins by learning to draw Ki inward and circulate it through the body, but this practice alone does not define the path. What matters is the intent behind it. Some cultivate to stand firm, others to strike decisively, and others still to remain unbound. Over time, these choices influence not only a cultivator’s techniques, but the way they move, react, and behave.

Cultivation places constant pressure on belief. Power responds more readily to consistency than contradiction, and cultivators who act against their own principles often find their progress stalling or their techniques failing at critical moments. As a result, most adopt a guiding philosophy early on, refining their practice around it as they grow.

Because of this, cultivation traditions vary widely. One sect may prize endurance and rootedness, teaching its disciples to weather any storm. Another may emphasize adaptability or freedom, encouraging practitioners to preserve choice and avoid being fixed in place. These differences are not merely cosmetic. They shape how Ki is circulated, how arts are used, and how conflict is approached.

A cultivator’s strength is therefore not measured solely by the amount of power they possess, but by how well their actions align with the path they have chosen. Those who cultivate without direction may achieve temporary gains, but rarely endure. Those who commit to a philosophy, however subtle, find their Ki responding with greater clarity and reliability over time.

guideHtml:
The cultivator class is a utility class with several useful abilities.

Ki Circulation lets you remove a variety of debuffs from yourself. You'll most likely use it to remove poisons or unbalanced, as Bleeding and Shaken will likely already have taken effect before you can use it.

Ki Sense is a simple tool that lets you know where to search for stealthed targets when they are in range.

Ki Recovery is an upgrade from the basic Draw Mana encounter conclusion action and is very useful for anyone that uses a lot of mana.

The Sword Riding Art is a way of flying that's both better and worse than other ways of flight. You gain some weaknesses to pushbacks and aren't as good in aerial combat, but the upside is that you never get rooted even if you're lowered to the ground level by normal attacks. It does require you to bring a spare weapon that you can't use for combat, so keep that in mind.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Athletics, Deception, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation, Stealth or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Cultivator's Journey
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: Your unarmed attack damage is treated as a One-Handed Weapon in which you are proficient.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You gain proficiency in light armor, light swords and another weapon of your choice.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Athletics, Deception, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation, Stealth or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

ability1Ref.name: Ki Circulation
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You cycle the Ki inside your body and purge it from negative energy. You remove Bleeding, Shaken, Unbalanced or any Poison effect from yourself.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You cycle the Ki inside your body and purge it from negative energy. You remove Bleeding, Shaken, Unbalanced or any Poison effect from yourself.

ability2Ref.name: Ki Sense
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You send out your Ki and become aware of any creatures without presence concealment within 40ft of you, giving you a rough idea of their location. This does not remove Hiding from them, but their attacks against you do not gain Trick Attack or any other stealth or surprise bonuses until the start of your next turn.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You send out your Ki and become aware of any creatures without presence concealment within 40ft of you, giving you a rough idea of their location. This does not remove Hiding from them, but their attacks against you do not gain Trick Attack or any other stealth or surprise bonuses until the start of your next turn.

ability3Ref.name: Ki Recovery
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You spend your Encounter Conclusion action to regain 2 mana.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You spend your Encounter Conclusion action to regain 2 mana.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Sword Riding Art
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You jump onto a light sword, versatile melee weapon, greatshield, bayonet musket or two-handed melee weapon you’re carrying and use it to move around. While riding on top of the sword, you gain Flight. You can fly this way for up to 5 minutes.

If you take damage while flying higher than 5ft above the ground, your altitude drops by 20ft. If this would reduce your altitude to the point where you touch the ground, you may hop off your weapon and put it back into your hand if possible for 0 RP. After this happens, your Sword Riding ends. If the attack that reduced your HP came from a flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude but instead become unbalanced.

If you are knocked back by an effect, you must make a save against the attacker's potency. Else, you are blown off your sword.

After this happens, your Sword Riding ends. If the attack that reduced your HP came from a flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude but instead become unbalanced.

If you are knocked back by an effect, you must make a save against the attacker's potency. Else, you are blown off your sword.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Must have a Light Sword or two-handed melee weapon in your hand.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Must have a Light Sword or two-handed melee weapon in your hand.

name: Curse Knight
descriptionHtml: A name given to warriors who suffer from a terrible curse. Their frequent misfortune is well known with even their weapons often managing to strike themselves instead of their enemies. However, there are some who manage to channel this misfortune and use its power against their enemies.
descriptionText: A name given to warriors who suffer from a terrible curse. Their frequent misfortune is well known with even their weapons often managing to strike themselves instead of their enemies. However, there are some who manage to channel this misfortune and use its power against their enemies.
guideHtml:
The Curse Knight is a class that can deal a high amount of damage at the cost of their own health.

Backlash is guaranteed damage on an enemy that they almost cannot stop, but you also take some damage yourself. It's good for finishing off low health targets or in fights where you feel the HP loss won't matter.

Devil's Blow is another good damage dealing ability that comes with a risk of potentially hitting yourself. The mana cost is low, so the attack can be spammed as long as you feel lucky. If you have an oracle in your party, then this attack is almost always safe to use.

Devi's Curse relies on the enemy missing to deal damage to them. Best used on their heavy attacks, as the mana cost may not be worth it to reflect a light one.

The Secret Art: Life Bet lets you do one massive hit, but the damage is based on how much HP you lose from it. Finding an optimal timing may be difficult, as you don't want to use it while an enemy still has reactions left but as the fight drags on, your HP will most likely be lower. Having support from your allies or your other abilities will be key in using this secret art.

Finally, the key ability On my terms allows you to continue using your abilities even as your HP gets lower. You won't need to worry about accidentally killing yourself. You can also use it to effectively ignore enemy attacks of opportunity while you're at low HP, since they will never kill you anyway or stand in effects that trigger at the start of your turn such as a fire.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Appraise, Athletics, Deception, Perception, Insight, Negotiation, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: On my terms
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: While you are at 1 HP or higher, damage (including true damage) you take during your own turn cannot reduce you below 1 HP.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: When you roll a 1 on a skill check, something unexpected may happen (GM's discretion). This is usually bad, but it could also turn out to be a blessing in disguise.

ability1Ref.name: Backlash
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light attack against the target. You take true damage equal to half the damage dealt.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light attack against the target. You take true damage equal to half the damage dealt.

ability2Ref.name: Devil's Curse
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
The attacker takes damage equal to the damage roll of the triggering attack. They cannot Dodge or Block this damage.

Does not work against Secret Arts, Climax Arts or similar level abilities.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
The attacker takes damage equal to the damage roll of the triggering attack. They cannot Dodge or Block this damage.

Does not work against Secret Arts, Climax Arts or similar level abilities.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: An enemy accuracy check against you rolls a 1.
ability2Ref.requirementText: An enemy accuracy check against you rolls a 1.

ability3Ref.name: Devil's Blow
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Heavy attack against the target. This attack does NOT have Full Pierce.

If you roll a 1 on the accuracy check, you take full pierce damage of same the type as the attack equal to the damage roll of the attack. You also regain the mana spent on this.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Heavy attack against the target. This attack does NOT have Full Pierce.

If you roll a 1 on the accuracy check, you take full pierce damage of same the type as the attack equal to the damage roll of the attack. You also regain the mana spent on this.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Life Bet
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You reduce your HP to 1 and make a Heavy attack against the target. You gain a bonus to the damage roll of this attack equal to the amount of HP you lost through this ability.

If this attack fails to meet the base evasion of the target, the HP you lost is refunded. It still counts as your one secret art use of the fight regardless.

name: Cutthroat
skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Stealth, Deception, Roguecraft, Perception, Insight, Negotiation, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Surgical Precision
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When you deal damage with a one-handed melee weapon that is both Sure Hit and Full Pierce or a critical hit, you inflict Bleeding on the target for 1 turn. This effect stacks with other Bleeds effects and itself up to 3 times.

ability1Ref.name: Exposing Stab
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, you may use your Devastating Strike against the target until the end of your turn as if the target had 0 RP.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, you may use your Devastating Strike against the target until the end of your turn as if the target had 0 RP.

ability2Ref.name: Feinting Stab
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy attack against the target. If they react to it in any way, you may cancel your attack on them and instead make it against a different enemy in range. Any RP spent by the first target is not refunded.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy attack against the target. If they react to it in any way, you may cancel your attack on them and instead make it against a different enemy in range. Any RP spent by the first target is not refunded.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: At least two enemies within range.
ability2Ref.requirementText: At least two enemies within range.

ability3Ref.name: Devastating Strike II
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light attack against the target. The dice are maximized instead of rolling.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light attack against the target. The dice are maximized instead of rolling.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: The target has no RP.
ability3Ref.requirementText: The target has no RP.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Artery Cut
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You change the arc of your weapon and go for a vital spot, turning the hit into a critical hit.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You change the arc of your weapon and go for a vital spot, turning the hit into a critical hit.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: One-Handed weapon only, can be used after a light attack hits and is not reacted against.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: One-Handed weapon only, can be used after a light attack hits and is not reacted against.

name: Daionmyoji
descriptionHtml:
Paper scattered away from him in a gust of unnatural wind, talismans shredding even as he was blown off his feet from the attack. Mamouru landed hard and rolled a few times before sliding to his feet. His opponent in the massive melee was grinning, wind magic whipping around her and causing her clothes to snap sharply. The catfolk girl was one of the many aeromancers that had devastated his units aerial mages and while the enemy mages hadnt gotten out of the fight unscathed, there were enough of them to drop into the demon backlines. Which was why he was dealing with the psycho catgirl instead of supporting the frontline troops.

Oh nyo, all of your little spells just fluttered away! Whatever will the big bad demon do?

She wasnt wrong. He had lost a bulk of his talismans and while he did have his wand and could cast spells himself without those talismans, the windy barrier around her would make actually hitting her incredibly difficult. Still, he leveled his wand at her with grim determination, his divine blood itching underneath his skin, begging for release.

Surrender! he called out over the wind. And I promise youll be treated fairly!

The girl laughed and took a step forward, foot crunching on a talisman that was doggedly stuck to the ground.

Oh, youre funny! Youve already lost, idiot!

I may be an idiot, but Im not the one that just stepped on a trap. The girls eyes darted down to the talisman now stuck to her foot and then back up at him. With a showy move, he snapped his fingers and a pulse of magic surged out for more than a hundred feet in every direction.

Spells fizzled and died as the mana interference of his dispel disrupted their delicate matrices. Half a dozen aeromancers feel screaming to their deaths as the wind keeping them aloft stripped away from them. But importantly, the windy barrier around the catgirl in front of him died down. In an instant, he drew a very special talisman and held it between his fingers as his hands formed the required seal to unlock it.

Ultimate Spell: Divine Disjunction!

The talisman dissolved in a rush of energy as a series of magical circles unfolded before him. The augmented magic missiles flashed away from him in a swarm, trails of magical energy marking their travel. The girl tried to dodge but the missiles were relentless in their homing and their empowered nature slashed through her light armor with no resistance. She had just enough time to look up from where she landed, clutching her bleeding shoulder, to see the second wave of missiles launch from the echo of the first magical circle. By the time the dust settled, there was one less aeromancer to deal with.

guideHtml:
The Daionmyoji is a class that is heavily dependant on the types of talismans you bring.

With Spell Transfer, you will have more flexibility in that as long as you can find an ally willing to help you.

Prepared Talisman has a lot of possible applications primarily being used as a sort of prepare action that can be held for longer. You could put a cure touch talisman on an ally at the start of combat and only pop it when they get injured allowing you to stay at range. Place a fissure talisman down on the ground in front of you and if an enemy ever tries to approach you, you send them into the pit. It does consume the talisman when used in this way though, so be aware of that.

With Talisman Recursion you are able to get more use out of the unique talismans from spell transfer, allowing you to for example double fireball even though you only have 1 fireball talisman and you're not a pyromancer yourself.

Spiritual Reading is a mostly utility ability that is a more effective version of several abilities such as aurasense and sense magic at the cost of 1 mana. It has situational use, but if you need to figure out magic in the middle of combat or want to figure out how strong this NPC is, it's good.

The secret art is the ultimate versatility option. While it does cost you your secret art, you get to replicate any basic spell in the game as you need it. Need to get rid of a magic effect? Get dispel magic. A flier giving you trouble you want to get rid of instantly? Downburst II. Entire team is injured and needs healing? Radiant Halo.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Appraise, Medicine, Religion, Magic Perception, Insight, Art or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Spell Transfer
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When you use the Create Talisman ability, an ally may spend their Errand action to use one of their spells to put on the talisman instead of your own. You only create 1 copy of this talisman per Errand action and cannot use Secret Art spells with it.

ability1Ref.name: Prepared Talisman
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may place a Talisman on a surface or on a target within range. This costs the same amount of AP and Mana as it would normally cost and placing it on an unwilling target requires an accuracy roll that the target may dodge as normal. A target may spend AP equal to the AP cost of the talisman (before reductions) to remove the talisman from themselves.

After placing it, until the end of the encounter or for the next 5 minutes, you may spend 0 RP to instantly trigger the effect of the talisman. You cannot trigger it anymore once a talisman has been removed or is in the process of being removed and a removed talisman loses its power entirely. A talisman placed on a creature gains Sure Hit against the creature it's on when triggered.

You can only have 1 talisman prepared in this way at the same time and Sealing Talisman cannot be prepared like this. A placed talisman cannot have an AP cost of greater than 4 (or 10 seconds for casting times).
ability1Ref.descriptionText:

After placing it, until the end of the encounter or for the next 5 minutes, you may spend 0 RP to instantly trigger the effect of the talisman. You cannot trigger it anymore once a talisman has been removed or is in the process of being removed and a removed talisman loses its power entirely. A talisman placed on a creature gains Sure Hit against the creature it's on when triggered.

You can only have 1 talisman prepared in this way at the same time and Sealing Talisman cannot be prepared like this. A placed talisman cannot have an AP cost of greater than 4 (or 10 seconds for casting times).
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Special
ability1Ref.requirementText: Special

ability2Ref.name: Spiritual Reading
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You are able to check the aura of a creature or object within range. 

If you check a creature, you can see whether a creature is non-heroic, weaker than you, around the same as you, stronger than you or much stronger than you. The GM may also give you their Spirit Core value if it has one. You also are aware if someone is concealing their spirit core level, but can’t get an accurate reading of their strength if they are. In addition, you are able to see their mana % until the end of the encounter.

If you check an item, you can determine whether it’s magical in nature. If it is, you may immediately make a Magic check against it to determine its effect as if you were using Decipher Magic with a +5 bonus.

In addition, you are able to see their mana % until the end of the encounter.

If you check an item, you can determine whether it’s magical in nature. If it is, you may immediately make a Magic check against it to determine its effect as if you were using Decipher Magic with a +5 bonus.

ability3Ref.name: Talisman Recursion
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
After using a Talisman, you may immediately spend the cost of that talisman (before reductions) to repeat the effect without consuming another talisman. If the talisman had a target, you must choose the same target.

Does not work with Secret Talismans.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
After using a Talisman, you may immediately spend the cost of that talisman (before reductions) to repeat the effect without consuming another talisman. If the talisman had a target, you must choose the same target.

Does not work with Secret Talismans.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Within This Ink, The Perfect Answer
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You draw and immediately use a special talisman.

This talisman may have any spell in the base game on it (meaning it’s on the website), though it cannot be a secret or esoteric art. Any costs for this spell must be paid as normal. You must pay the required calligraphy units as if you made the spell yourself. If you can’t pay the required amount, you go into ‘debt’. While in debt, you cannot create any more talisman or use this ability.

To clear the debt, you must pay the required amount of calligraphy units at any point.

After you use this ability, you cannot do so again until the end of the arc.

name: Dark Priest
descriptionHtml: By CCeles.
descriptionText: By CCeles.
skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Religion, Magic, History, Appraise, Deception, Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason or Cunning.
soul: You gain +1 to Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Soul Absorption
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Elemental Mastery: Dark
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use Soul Absorption.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Soul Absorption
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Light Attack against a willing target that deals Light Dark damage. Your next Dark type Spell or Attack other than Soul Absorption gains extra damage equal to damage taken by the target. (Does not stack)
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Light Attack against a willing target that deals Light Dark damage. Your next Dark type Spell or Attack other than Soul Absorption gains extra damage equal to damage taken by the target. (Does not stack)

ability1Ref.name: Gloom Infusion
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You surround a willing target in gloom and shadow, changing their typing to Dark II. As a Dark II type, they take half damage from Dark Damage or attacks with the Dark keywords, unless the attack deals true damage. They take 50% more damage from Holy Damage or attacks with the Holy keyword. While the ward lasts, they also gain 60 feet Darkvision and a +4 bonus to saves against mind affecting effects. 

The ward lasts until the end of the encounter or until their type is overwritten by a more powerful type.

The ward lasts until the end of the encounter or until their type is overwritten by a more powerful type.

ability2Ref.name: Dark Healing
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You take Light (1 AP) or Heavy damage (2 AP), then heal a target within range an amount equal to damage taken.

You may choose to ignore Dark resistance or damage reduction on yourself for this damage.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You take Light (1 AP) or Heavy damage (2 AP), then heal a target within range an amount equal to damage taken.

You may choose to ignore Dark resistance or damage reduction on yourself for this damage.

ability3Ref.name: Dark Barrier
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You and up to 4 other willing allies within range take Light Dark damage. Afterwards they gain temporary HP until end of encounter equal to double the damage taken.

You may choose to ignore Dark resistance or damage reduction on your allies for this ability.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You and up to 4 other willing allies within range take Light Dark damage. Afterwards they gain temporary HP until end of encounter equal to double the damage taken.

You may choose to ignore Dark resistance or damage reduction on your allies for this ability.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Stygian Screen
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You create a wall of darkness that is 20ft long, 5ft wide and 10ft high. This wall must be a straight line.  This wall blocks line of sight. Targets inside of the wall can only see 5ft away
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You create a wall of darkness that is 20ft long, 5ft wide and 10ft high. This wall must be a straight line.  This wall blocks line of sight. Targets inside of the wall can only see 5ft away

name: Deadeye
requirementsText: Marksman, Gunslinger, Scout or Archer mastered.
guideHtml:
The deadeye is a ranged striker with some minor control and utility. Status Assessment III is similar to the previous version, but it no longer has the quick keyword meaning it can be used without worrying that it increases the cost of other abilities.

Aimed Shot is a simple attack meant to punish enemies that rely on dodge.

Disarming Shot is a powerful tool that can be used to disarm enemies for a long range. Disarming melee enemies may seem like the obvious choice, but many ranged enemies will not carry a spare ranged weapon. Disarming them will most likely lead to them being unable to act for the turn.

Countershot is another tool the Deadeye has to show it is the superior ranged attacker. It is not guaranteed, but it can assist allies and defend yourself against opposing ranged attacks.

Secret Art: Perfect Repetition is not the easiest secret art to use. It has the upside of having no mana cost and relatively low AP cost, but if you roll badly the attack is wasted. On the other hand, a good roll almost ensures a guaranteed hit every round unless the target takes cover. It is more reliable when the enemy has no RP, but don't be afraid to throw it out early and go for a gamble. It's worth the risk.

heart: You gain +1 Fitness, Cunning or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 Focus or Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Superior Assessment
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Status Assessment III.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Status Assessment III
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You can assess the approximate HP and RP percentage of a unit until the end of combat. (The DM should enable health bars for the unit). You can also assess any damage type weaknesses and strengths that the enemy has at the current time. You can also determine which parts of a creature can be targeted and destroyed, if any.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You can assess the approximate HP and RP percentage of a unit until the end of combat. (The DM should enable health bars for the unit). You can also assess any damage type weaknesses and strengths that the enemy has at the current time. You can also determine which parts of a creature can be targeted and destroyed, if any.

ability1Ref.name: Disarming Shot
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. If the attack beat the passive evasion of the target (before Dodge bonuses), you Disarm the target until the start of your next turn. The target may spend 1 RP to avoid the disarm effect. This is in addition to any RP the target may spend to avoid the attack itself.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. If the attack beat the passive evasion of the target (before Dodge bonuses), you Disarm the target until the start of your next turn. The target may spend 1 RP to avoid the disarm effect. This is in addition to any RP the target may spend to avoid the attack itself.

ability2Ref.name: Countershot
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Precise Attack against the incoming attack. If your accuracy check matches or beats the enemy’s accuracy check, you shoot down the incoming arrow and the attack is negated.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Precise Attack against the incoming attack. If your accuracy check matches or beats the enemy’s accuracy check, you shoot down the incoming arrow and the attack is negated.

ability3Ref.name: Aimed Shot
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy Attack against the target.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy Attack against the target.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Perfect Repetition
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage, on the next turn, you may repeat this attack against the same target using the same accuracy check as the initial attack. You may continue to repeat this attack on subsequent turns until you spend a turn not using this move or if the attack fails to deal damage.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage, on the next turn, you may repeat this attack against the same target using the same accuracy check as the initial attack. You may continue to repeat this attack on subsequent turns until you spend a turn not using this move or if the attack fails to deal damage.

name: Death Knight
descriptionHtml: By Sabbrewolf.
descriptionText: By Sabbrewolf.
requirementsHtml: Armorsmith or Fighter mastered and any tier 2 class mastered.
requirementsText: Armorsmith or Fighter mastered and any tier 2 class mastered.
guideHtml:
The Death Knight's versatility comes from their ability to switch between dealing damage and using defensive options depending on the needs of the encounter. They thrive when they have a steady supply of corpses to convert to Bone armor to protect or sustain themselves. As such, timing and resource management are crucial for a Death Knight player.

Bone Armor is a core defensive tool that enables you to mitigate a powerful incoming attack.
Unholy Pact serves as a potent self-heal and buff, converting your Bone Armor into a substantial amount of health. Making the right choice between the defensive boost of Bone Armor or using your resources to heal and focus more on offense will be crucial.

Call of the Grave allows you to force enemies into close-quarters combat, where Death Knights excel, but will have difficulty landing on evasive targets. It's a great opener to control the battlefield and ensure enemies are where you want them while applying pressure with Can’t Escape Death. Just be wary when using that ability lest you find yourself isolated from your allies.

Unholy Strength is a powerful self buff but it requires careful management of your resources so be mindful of your mana.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Bone Armor
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Bone Armor.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Bone Armor
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You target and destroy a corpse within range to give yourself Bone Armor, which gives you a +2 bonus to your guard. Bone Armor lasts until the end of the encounter or until you take 2 or more damage from an attack.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You target and destroy a corpse within range to give yourself Bone Armor, which gives you a +2 bonus to your guard. Bone Armor lasts until the end of the encounter or until you take 2 or more damage from an attack.

ability1Ref.name: Unholy Pact
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You remove your Bone Armor to heal yourself for Power x 2 and gain a +2 Power bonus on your next attack.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You remove your Bone Armor to heal yourself for Power x 2 and gain a +2 Power bonus on your next attack.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Bone Armor is active on you.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Bone Armor is active on you.

ability2Ref.name: Call of the Grave
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy Attack against the target that deals Dark damage. On hit, if the target is large or smaller, you pull them to you. They are Challenged until the start of your next turn. This ability does not provoke an attack of opportunity.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy Attack against the target that deals Dark damage. On hit, if the target is large or smaller, you pull them to you. They are Challenged until the start of your next turn. This ability does not provoke an attack of opportunity.

ability3Ref.name: Can't Escape Death
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may use this after an enemy leaves your melee range. After the target finishes moving, you may sidestep up to your speed towards them. For 1 RP, you may move double your speed instead.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may use this after an enemy leaves your melee range. After the target finishes moving, you may sidestep up to your speed towards them. For 1 RP, you may move double your speed instead.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: A target Challenged by you leaves your melee range.
ability3Ref.requirementText: A target Challenged by you leaves your melee range.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Unholy Strength
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You Gain Unholy Strength which grants +2 Power and Focus. While Unholy Strength is active your attacks/abilities that use your melee weapon to deal physical damage inflict Dark Damage instead and your Bone Armor will not break from damage. 

Upkeep: 1 Mana.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You Gain Unholy Strength which grants +2 Power and Focus. While Unholy Strength is active your attacks/abilities that use your melee weapon to deal physical damage inflict Dark Damage instead and your Bone Armor will not break from damage.

Upkeep: 1 Mana.

name: Dragoon
requirementsHtml: Lancer mastered. 10 Points in the Athletics (Jumping) expertise skill or be a Jiangshi.
requirementsText: Lancer mastered. 10 Points in the Athletics (Jumping) expertise skill or be a Jiangshi.
guideHtml:
Dragoons excel at fighting at heights. They do not need to worry about fall damage when using their dragoon abilities and can turn it into damage for them by using Skydive. When not fighting on an airship or something else that flies, Dragoons can use buildings or trees to gain the height advantage.

If there is nothing else around, Jump can be used. It is a decent initiation option, though Jump is often better used to reposition or escape from a bad situation than pure initiation.

Dragonfire Dive is a powerful AoE tool that puts you in range to follow up with more attacks right after.

Jump Back is an ability that allows the dragoon to perform hit and run attacks. You can engage, then jump back to force enemies to waste their movement in coming to you. When fighting in the air, you can jump on an enemy, kill it, then jump back to your airship. There's a lot of possibilities, just keep in mind that it has to be used directly after a Jump ability. Don't stick around for too long, or you aren't able to get back.

Their Secret Art: Dragonslaying Jump is what makes them masters of aerial combat. It is effectively a guaranteed heavy hit on a flying target that forces them to the ground. Against a target that is flying high above the ground, this last part can easily be fatal.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception, Common Knowledge or a relevant Expert Knowledge skill (By DM Approval). You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness.
soul: You gain +1 Focus or Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Jump
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When using an ability with the Jump keyword, you do not take fall damage.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use Jump.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Jump
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You leap up to 30ft into the air and then Sidestep up to twice your Speed. You may use Skydive at any point during this movement.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You leap up to 30ft into the air and then Sidestep up to twice your Speed. You may use Skydive at any point during this movement.

ability1Ref.name: Skydive
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You target an enemy below you, no more than 5ft away from you horizontally. You then immediately dive down to that target, making a Heavy Attack against them. You deal an additional point of damage for every 10ft you fell before making the attack, up to 10 extra damage.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You target an enemy below you, no more than 5ft away from you horizontally. You then immediately dive down to that target, making a Heavy Attack against them. You deal an additional point of damage for every 10ft you fell before making the attack, up to 10 extra damage.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must be airborne, polearm only.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must be airborne, polearm only.

ability2Ref.name: Dragonfire Dive
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You jump to the target, not provoking any attacks of opportunity, and attacking the target plus all other creatures within 5ft of the target for Heavy Fire Damage.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You jump to the target, not provoking any attacks of opportunity, and attacking the target plus all other creatures within 5ft of the target for Heavy Fire Damage.

ability3Ref.name: Jump Back
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You Jump Back to your previous location. This movement does not provoke any attacks of opportunity.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You Jump Back to your previous location. This movement does not provoke any attacks of opportunity.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Dragonslaying Jump
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You Jump up to the target, not provoking any attacks of opportunity, and make a Heavy Attack against it. On damage, the target plummets to the ground taking falling damage as normal. Overcharge: You may spend additional AP on this ability to increase the range by 60ft for every extra AP spent. If this increases the AP cost to more than what you currently have, it gains the Setup keyword.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You Jump up to the target, not provoking any attacks of opportunity, and make a Heavy Attack against it. On damage, the target plummets to the ground taking falling damage as normal. Overcharge: You may spend additional AP on this ability to increase the range by 60ft for every extra AP spent. If this increases the AP cost to more than what you currently have, it gains the Setup keyword.

name: Duelist
guideHtml:
The duelist, like the fencer it builds on, is a 1v1 expert and off-tank on the battlefield. With deflect missiles, they gain some defense against ranged attacks.

Riposte II is a more defensive version of the original riposte. Where the fencer traded the RP they saved from the Parry ability into damage, with Riposte II the duelist can dish out heavy attacks while sitting on all their RP still. It does cost mana, so it's nothing something that should be used after every Parry.

Duelist Blow is a powerful tool in 1v1s, but since the benefit disappears when forced to use RP against other targets it may be hard to use in fights against multiple enemies unless you can find isolated those 1v1s.

Forward Momentum is a simple and cheap ability that helps with that. It's best used with light attacks if the target still has RP remaining. A few attacks and the duelist can fight the target without interruptions.

The Climax Art: Endless Waltz has the potential for a ton of attacks. It's especially strong against enemies out of RP, against who it may repeat until they are dead. Against grunts, it can still be powerful although less consistent. The first attack would remove their RP, while the second would deal damage. Of course, bad rolls can happen and the ability may end early.

heart: You gain +1 Fitness, Cunning or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Deflect Everything
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use your Parry ability against every kind of attack, as well as against Save effects that you could Block.

ability1Ref.name: Forward Momentum
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: Until the start of your next turn, on damage of every melee attack you make, you may push the target back 5ft. You must then move 5ft towards that target (if possible to do safely) without provoking attacks of opportunity. This only works with attacks that target a single enemy.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: Until the start of your next turn, on damage of every melee attack you make, you may push the target back 5ft. You must then move 5ft towards that target (if possible to do safely) without provoking attacks of opportunity. This only works with attacks that target a single enemy.

ability2Ref.name: Riposte II
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target that you used your Parry ability against.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target that you used your Parry ability against.

ability3Ref.name: Duelist Blow
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy Attack against the target. Your next Block or Dodge reaction against that target costs 0 RP. This benefit disappears when you use an RP ability against a target other than the target of this ability.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy Attack against the target. Your next Block or Dodge reaction against that target costs 0 RP. This benefit disappears when you use an RP ability against a target other than the target of this ability.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Endless Waltz
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You make two Light Attacks against the same target. On damage of this attack, you may repeat this attack for 0 AP.

Killing Blow: You may move up to twice your speed without provoking attacks of opportunity.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You make two Light Attacks against the same target. On damage of this attack, you may repeat this attack for 0 AP.

Killing Blow: You may move up to twice your speed without provoking attacks of opportunity.

name: Earthcleaving Style
descriptionHtml:
A crowd of warriors was a uniquely noisy experience. Armor clanking and rattling, leather creaking, weapons ringing. There were all types of Youkai at the festival from hulking Oni men with bulging muscles to elegant Kitsune with armor that looked like they cost multiple seasons worth of work to afford. A serene faced Ryujin was just stepping up to the head of the crowd, his horns gleaming in the sun and his scaled armor a masterwork.

The Stone Splitting Festival shall now begin. There are boulders from all corners of the world and the prize for splitting each will go to the one who makes the final, splitting stroke. Rewards are, obviously, determined by the quality of the stone. He gestured toward the field behind him at the decorated areas containing various sized boulders of different materials. Our centerpiece is a ShMki infused basalt from beyond Ezochi, sourced from the depths of the Kita no ShMheki.

Left unsaid was that something like that would be nearly impossible to crack, let alone split. Even still, a significant chunk of the largest and most confident of the crowd immediately went toward the basalt boulder to prove their worth. Yuzu followed behind that group, the wooden carapace covering the mysterious metals of her limbs was hand carved by her grandmother, replacing the cracked, porcelain-like covering she used to have. Her fingers clacked against the handle of her greatsword, tilting it slightly to prevent the tip from dragging on the ground. A Raijin scoffed next to her as he adjusted the katana at his waist.

This isnt a game for a doll, he sneered. You need real muscle for this.

Yuzu was tempted to flip her hair and walk by him but she didnt want to antagonize people if she didnt have to. Theyd be upset enough as it was when she split that boulder. His sentiment was common, especially among warriors, when it came to Marionettes like her or even Jiangshi in small bodies. Her form was static, she couldnt build muscle like an Oni or Kitsune or Raijin. But she could focus more mana into her metal limbs, her wooden skin, and her joints to increase her strength and durability. In fact, she could feel she was only tapping the barest potential of what her body was capable of even now.

By the time she reached the end of the line, there was already a cacophony of shouts and weapon impacts against stones intermingled with distant cheers as weaker stones were split. But at the basalt boulder, the only sounds were roars of effort and the ring of ineffectual impacts. A cheer went up when she was just about to get her turn when the Raijin that had mocked her used his lightning infused katana to break a finger sized chip off the boulder, marking the most significant damage so far.

Finally it was Yuzus turn and laughter followed her as she swung her sword off her shoulder to land heavily point down in the ground, balanced by her grip on the crossguard that was just about at eye level with her. Calls to get the doll out of here rang out but she ignored them. With a shout that drowned them out, she slammed her sword straight down into the earth until it stopped with a tremor at the guard, making those around her wobble. She pulled it out with a slight hop, her sword now coated in jagged stone. She stepped up to the basalt boulder, the shMki of it a palpable aura. She lifted her sword easily above her head.

Keisoku HMkai! 

Her sword came down with a resonating crack, the earth and air vibrating with the force of it. With a groan of protest, the basalt boulder slowly split apart to the stunned silence of the crowd.

--
Translators Note: 

ShMki - Miasma

Kita no ShMheki - Northern Barrier

Keisoku HMkai - Measured Collapse

Ezochi - The center northern island of Kirara

requirementsHtml: Any class mastered. Proficiency in any two-handed melee weapon.
requirementsText: Any class mastered. Proficiency in any two-handed melee weapon.
guideHtml:
The Earthcleaving style is a class that's focused on busting through heavily armored and low evasion targets. It lets you use your potentially high focus and transfer that into hitting enemies against which you'd normally struggle.

Your basic combo starts with Tectonic Overhead, which is a heavy attack that has a high chance to miss against enemies without low evasion. Only use it against enemies with heavy armor or similar lowered evasion. This combo's into Strata Cut, which is effectively guaranteed half pierce damage. Finally, measured collapse is a full pierce heavy of which you can know in advance whether it will hit or not. If it does, it is one of the best abilities you can use.

Weight of the Mountain is a simple ability that lets you convert focus into damage when you're hitting something that has low enough evasion that you don't need your focus to hit it.

Finally, measured collapse is a full pierce heavy of which you can know in advance whether it will hit or not. If it does, it is one of the best abilities you can use.

Weight of the Mountain is a simple ability that lets you convert focus into damage when you're hitting something that has low enough evasion that you don't need your focus to hit it.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus.

keyAbilityRef.name: Earth Weapon
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Earth Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare you chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

ability1Ref.name: Strata Cut
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Precise attack against the target. 

Combo: If used after Tectonic Overhead, the mana cost is reduced by 1.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Precise attack against the target.

Combo: If used after Tectonic Overhead, the mana cost is reduced by 1.

ability2Ref.name: Tectonic Overhead
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a slow, powerful overhead swing and make a Heavy attack against the target. Their evasion (but not dodge) is doubled for this attack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a slow, powerful overhead swing and make a Heavy attack against the target. Their evasion (but not dodge) is doubled for this attack.

ability3Ref.name: Weight of the Mountain
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You prepare to strike with enhanced power.

Your next attack with a two-handed melee weapon this turn loses the keywords: Lock On and Sure Hit. In addition, you do not gain accuracy from your Focus on the enhanced attack. The attack also cannot benefit from Offstep and similar effects that guarantee the attack hits.

Instead, the attack deals extra damage equal to the accuracy lost from not gaining Focus due to Weight of the Mountain. If the attack is a heavy or precise attack, it deals extra damage equal to double the accuracy lost instead. 

If the attack made with this effect already did not benefit from focus, this ability has no effect.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You prepare to strike with enhanced power.

Your next attack with a two-handed melee weapon this turn loses the keywords: Lock On and Sure Hit. In addition, you do not gain accuracy from your Focus on the enhanced attack. The attack also cannot benefit from Offstep and similar effects that guarantee the attack hits.

Instead, the attack deals extra damage equal to the accuracy lost from not gaining Focus due to Weight of the Mountain. If the attack is a heavy or precise attack, it deals extra damage equal to double the accuracy lost instead.

If the attack made with this effect already did not benefit from focus, this ability has no effect.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Measured Collapse
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You make a Heavy attack against the target. This attack is always considered to have rolled a 2 for accuracy instead of rolling the d20.

Against creatures that are large size, gain a +2 bonus. If they are huge size, gain a +4 bonus.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You make a Heavy attack against the target. This attack is always considered to have rolled a 2 for accuracy instead of rolling the d20.

Against creatures that are large size, gain a +2 bonus. If they are huge size, gain a +4 bonus.

name: Electromancer
requirementsHtml: Any class mastered or have Lightning element mastered.
requirementsText: Any class mastered or have Lightning element mastered.
guideHtml:
Electromancers excel in fighting against enemies in water. Their superconductor ability applies two powerful keywords all lightning attacks for free. This applies even to attacks made with channeling weapons, not just the Electromancer abilities.

Zap is an ability with a high chance to apply static. Static is the main debuff of the electromancer and what gives them the controller status. It does not damage the person who has it, but it forces them to position carefully to not hit their allies with it. Your allies are immune to the effect, so you don't need to worry about the enemies hitting them with your static. Static is most effective if combined with roots, such as a netting flask or against enemies already engaged in melee combat, where moving to an isolated spot becomes more difficult.

Bolting is a long range strike with a small benefit of potentially ignoring cover. The nature of the attack means it cannot be used indoors however. When not using it as a snipe attack, it's most useful for being a Heavy attack that also applies Static.

Forked Lightning is their only way to apply static without using mana. It requires there to be two targets, but static is useless without at least two targets anyway. The static value is lower than on the other attacks is lower to compensate for this lack of mana cost.

Chain Lightning can decimate entire groups, provided that their RP has been burned beforehand and you don't get unlucky with rolls. Only use it when at least two people have 0 RP or otherwise cannot react to your attack, otherwise it's not worth the cost.
