ability1Ref.name: Plow the fields
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You strike the ground with magical power and make a Light attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide line up to range. The terrain within this range becomes difficult terrain until the start of your next turn.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You strike the ground with magical power and make a Light attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide line up to range. The terrain within this range becomes difficult terrain until the start of your next turn.

ability2Ref.name: Wither the Stalk
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You strike an enemy with an attack that saps their strength. You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target cannot add their Power for damage to their next attack.

Does not work against Boss enemies.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You strike an enemy with an attack that saps their strength. You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target cannot add their Power for damage to their next attack.

Does not work against Boss enemies.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Scythe only
ability2Ref.requirementText: Scythe only

ability3Ref.name: Pull the weeds
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You destroy one of your planted seeds to give yourself +5 crafting points for this session.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You destroy one of your planted seeds to give yourself +5 crafting points for this session.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Must be in a crafting session. Only applies to a farming crafting session.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Must be in a crafting session. Only applies to a farming crafting session.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Scarecrow's Gaze
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You summon the appearance of whatever someone fears the most. Every enemy within range must make a Save against your potency. On failure, they may not walk closer to you until the start of your next turn unless they have no valid targets to attack without doing so.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You summon the appearance of whatever someone fears the most. Every enemy within range must make a Save against your potency. On failure, they may not walk closer to you until the start of your next turn unless they have no valid targets to attack without doing so.

name: Fencer
guideHtml:
The fencer is a highly mobile 1v1 specialist. Their fancy footwork allows them to reposition on the battlefield and away from other attackers, keeping them focused on their target.

Parry is their most important ability. It costs 1 mana, but the RP cost is easily refunded if you use it against light attacks or against someone weaker than you. Because it effectively is a damage check with your focus instead of power, you should be careful in using it against high power enemies, especially if they use a heavy weapon. A heavy attack from those is likely to break through your parry and a dodge might be the better option.

Riposte is the follow up to a parry. It costs 1 RP, so it does eat into your defenses but it is an otherwise free Heavy attack. Just keep your RP in mind before using it.

Lunge helps fencer on fighting multiple enemies a bit. If an enemy is barely out of range, you can use your Lunge for extra range than fancy footwork to get in range. It can also be useful against rogueish characters who think they are safe after an evasive maneuver. Simply activate lunge and hit them from 10ft away.

Precise Thrust is an attack that's best used against heavily armored targets. Dodging is usually the preferred answer to a Heavy attack, so this may not see much use except against the most armored of enemies.

FAQ:

Q: Does Parry still apply guard after?
A: No, Parry is treated as a Block, so it does not add guard afterwards. It would also block crits, and does not work against Full Pierce attacks.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Deception, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness, Cunning or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 Focus.

keyAbilityRef.name: Fancy Footwork
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain proficiency with Dueling Weapons. While wielding a dueling weapon, you also gain the light blades' Nimble ability, even if not wielding or proficient with light blades.

ability1Ref.name: Parry
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
Roll your Heavy Weapon Damage, but add your Focus instead of Power to the roll and treat that as your Block against the attack targeting you. If Parry reduces the damage to 0, you regain 1 RP (cannot go higher than your max RP). On hit effects and maces do not gain their effect if you reduce the damage to 0.

When using Parry against an attack, that attack cannot land a critical hit against you.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
Roll your Heavy Weapon Damage, but add your Focus instead of Power to the roll and treat that as your Block against the attack targeting you. If Parry reduces the damage to 0, you regain 1 RP (cannot go higher than your max RP). On hit effects and maces do not gain their effect if you reduce the damage to 0.

When using Parry against an attack, that attack cannot land a critical hit against you.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Target of a melee attack, wielding a One-Handed melee weapon.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Target of a melee attack, wielding a One-Handed melee weapon.

ability2Ref.name: Lunge
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: The attack range of your next basic Light, Heavy or Precise Attack with a melee weapon is doubled.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: The attack range of your next basic Light, Heavy or Precise Attack with a melee weapon is doubled.

ability3Ref.name: Riposte
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target that you used your Parry ability against.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target that you used your Parry ability against.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Precise Thrust
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.

This attack does not disable the dueling weapon ability.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.

This attack does not disable the dueling weapon ability.

name: Fighter
descriptionHtml:
Fighters come from many backgrounds. They can be adventurers, soldiers or even just travelers who've decided to master the arts of combat. They wield weapons with expert skill, making them the backbone of any adventuring party.

What sets them apart from ordinary soldiers is their ability to perceive and control mana within their bodies. This keen understanding gives them an edge—allowing them to move at high speeds and deliver powerful blows. Fighters are a force to be reckoned with on the battlefield and welcome allies.
descriptionText:
Fighters come from many backgrounds. They can be adventurers, soldiers or even just travelers who've decided to master the arts of combat. They wield weapons with expert skill, making them the backbone of any adventuring party.

What sets them apart from ordinary soldiers is their ability to perceive and control mana within their bodies. This keen understanding gives them an edge—allowing them to move at high speeds and deliver powerful blows. Fighters are a force to be reckoned with on the battlefield and welcome allies.
guideHtml:
Fighters are versatile front liners that are able to defend against attacks effectively while also exerting pressure on the enemy.  A good fighter is expected to draw the enemy to them and to create space for the rest of their allies to unleash their full potential.  

Charge can be used to gain position in a fight without sacrificing attack. When moving in in the first round of a combat, you normally have to sacrifice attacks for the movement. Charge helps mitigate this, allowing the fighter to take point and still do damage.

Slam is only a small knockback, but the AP cost of the attack is low and it has no other cost. It can be used to create space for an ally to safely escape, but a fighter can also use it at the end of their attack sequence. This way the target will need to spend 1 AP on movement again to get in range, lowering their attacks.

Defensive Flexibility gives high RP economy, but it requires a bit of thinking ahead. It has to be used after you take one of the two basic reactions, and youll have to predict what your opponent will do next or force them to do suboptimal attacks. In long fights, consider what attacks you can safely tank without the use of RP to allow for more defensive flexibility uses in the future. Every RP you can spend that leads into defensive flexibility is effectively doubled.

Power Strike is an attack that can deal a lot of damage, but is not very mana-efficient. It should almost always only be used against a target with no RP left. Fighters themselves don't have anything else that uses mana, so it's worth using for new characters. As you unlock more classes and get other abilities that use mana, Power Strike will probably not see much use anymore.
guideText:
Fighters are versatile front liners that are able to defend against attacks effectively while also exerting pressure on the enemy.  A good fighter is expected to draw the enemy to them and to create space for the rest of their allies to unleash their full potential.

Charge can be used to gain position in a fight without sacrificing attack. When moving in in the first round of a combat, you normally have to sacrifice attacks for the movement. Charge helps mitigate this, allowing the fighter to take point and still do damage.

Slam is only a small knockback, but the AP cost of the attack is low and it has no other cost. It can be used to create space for an ally to safely escape, but a fighter can also use it at the end of their attack sequence. This way the target will need to spend 1 AP on movement again to get in range, lowering their attacks.

Defensive Flexibility gives high RP economy, but it requires a bit of thinking ahead. It has to be used after you take one of the two basic reactions, and youll have to predict what your opponent will do next or force them to do suboptimal attacks. In long fights, consider what attacks you can safely tank without the use of RP to allow for more defensive flexibility uses in the future. Every RP you can spend that leads into defensive flexibility is effectively doubled.

Power Strike is an attack that can deal a lot of damage, but is not very mana-efficient. It should almost always only be used against a target with no RP left. Fighters themselves don't have anything else that uses mana, so it's worth using for new characters. As you unlock more classes and get other abilities that use mana, Power Strike will probably not see much use anymore.
skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness.
soul: You gain +1 to Power, Focus, Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Fighter's Journey
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain proficiency in 5 common weapons groups.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You gain proficiency in all light armor, medium armor and normal shields.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You gain +5 HP.

ability1Ref.name: Charge
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may spend 2 or 4 AP on this ability and move as though you had spent that AP on movement. 

After you finish moving, you may spend half of the AP you used on this ability on Light, Heavy or Precise Melee Attacks.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You may spend 2 or 4 AP on this ability and move as though you had spent that AP on movement.

After you finish moving, you may spend half of the AP you used on this ability on Light, Heavy or Precise Melee Attacks.

ability2Ref.name: Power Strike
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the enemy.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the enemy.

ability3Ref.name: Slam
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You attack a single target for Light Damage. On damage, the target is moved 5ft away from you if they are a Grunt or if your Power is higher than theirs, in addition the target must be no more than one size larger than you.

You may spend 1 Mana to ignore the Power requirement.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You attack a single target for Light Damage. On damage, the target is moved 5ft away from you if they are a Grunt or if your Power is higher than theirs, in addition the target must be no more than one size larger than you.

You may spend 1 Mana to ignore the Power requirement.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Defensive Flexibility
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You react to the triggering attack with the opposite reaction you used to meet the requirement of this ability. (IE if you used Dodge as your last reaction, you use the Block reaction for this).

The first time each encounter your RP goes below half your maximum RP, you regain the Circuit benefit on this.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You react to the triggering attack with the opposite reaction you used to meet the requirement of this ability. (IE if you used Dodge as your last reaction, you use the Block reaction for this).

The first time each encounter your RP goes below half your maximum RP, you regain the Circuit benefit on this.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Must be the target of an attack and must have reacted to the previous attack against you with the Dodge or Block action.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Must be the target of an attack and must have reacted to the previous attack against you with the Dodge or Block action.

name: Five Harmonies Style
descriptionHtml:
The first Suryan monastery was built on the highest mountaintops in Kirara, the ones closest to the sun. There the monks sought its guidance and found enlightenment through the 5 core tenets that they called the harmonies.

The Harmony of Form teaches that the body is a vessel of light. The body must remain clear and disciplined for radiance to pass through it. Through mastery of the body, the first harmony is achieved. Perfect form, movement without wasted motions.

The Harmony of Intention transforms will into compassion. True strength is not to destroy, but the choice to preserve. It is the power to channel your inner radiance into healing energy to aid those in need around you.

The Harmony of Sensation awakens sensation through contact. Every touch becomes a dialogue in the world. Once a person feels without judgement, they experience the world honestly and begin to awaken to truth.

The Harmony of Perception refines this awareness into clarity. Once mastered, this perception blooms into pure intuition and a fighter is able to extend their influence on the world.

The final teaching is the Harmony of Consciousness. It only can be understood once the other harmonies become one. It is the dissolution of separation. The body, thought, senses and motions all become a single blooming sun of radiance. A single moment of temporary enlightenment.

While the teachings were philosophical in nature at the start, masters of the Five Harmonies have converted these teachings into a formidable fighting style.

Move with clarity, Choose without hesitation, Feel without fear, See without illusion, Become without self.

guideHtml:
The 5 harmonies style is a healing class with low range and mobility on their heals, but high versatility in how they use them.

The core part of the class is the Luminous Form Stance. It is required to use all the other abilities in the class and gives you extra range to help with the fact that you can't willingly move. Leaving the stance means you lose all stacks, so consider very carefully if you have to leave it or not. The stance does not end if you make ranged attacks, so that may be a better option and you can wait for your allies to move to you for healing. You also only build stacks with abilities, so abilities like Leg Sweep should be considered for stack building.

Pure Intentions is your primary healing ability. It has relatively short range, but it is low AP cost and very versatile in how much you heal. If an ally needs only a small amount of healing you could spend only 1 or 2 stacks, while for an ally that is near death you could choose t o spend all 5 stacks for a big burst heal.

First Sensation is a simple ability that helps you stack faster. Noteworthy is that the first stack it gives is on hit, not on damage. It also has a combo effect, so stance into first sensation is a good way to instantly gain some stacks.

Flowing Perception is your one mobility tool that lets you move while remaining in stance. Useful for both repositioning to heal and for moving around the fight.

Blooming Consciousness is your secret art. It heals for a large amount in an aoe around you being both relatively mana efficient for the healing done and not costing too much AP for an AoE heal. It's only weakness is its limited range combined with the unmoving nature of your stance and relying on getting max stacks. If your team is grouped up, you can Flowing Perception to someone for a stack and positioning before you use it.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Negotiation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Reason or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus.

keyAbilityRef.name: Five Harmonies
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Luminous Form Stance.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Luminous Form Stance
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You enter the Luminous Form Stance and create the Threads of Light. These manifest spiritual cloth, spectral arms or something similar that enhances your reach.

While in this stance, you gain an additional 10ft range on your unarmed attacks and your threatened range becomes 5-15ft. Whenever you deal damage to an enemy with an unarmed strike ability (not basic attacks), you gain 1 stack of Harmony up to a maximum of 5. You cannot gain more than 1 Harmony per use of ability.

When you leave this stance all stacks are lost.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance or you willingly move, with the exception of movement abilities with the unarmed strike keyword. Abilities from allies that allow you to move, such as Gravitional Ki, count as willing movement.

ability1Ref.name: Mantra: Pure Intentions
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You wrap your Threads of Light around the chosen ally and channel your Harmony into them.

You may spend up to 5 stacks of Harmony and 1 mana per stack you consume to heal an ally within range for 3+Focus for each stack.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You wrap your Threads of Light around the chosen ally and channel your Harmony into them.

You may spend up to 5 stacks of Harmony and 1 mana per stack you consume to heal an ally within range for 3+Focus for each stack.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must have at least 1 stack of Harmony.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must have at least 1 stack of Harmony.

ability2Ref.name: Mudra: First Sensation
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You deliver the first motion, a graceful strike that awakens awareness in both you and your foe and you make a Light attack against the target. On hit, you gain a Harmony stack (In addition to the one you might get if this deals damage.)

Combo: When used after entering Luminous Form Stance, the mana cost is reduced by 1.

ability2Ref.requirementText: Must be in Luminous Form Stance

ability3Ref.name: Mantra: Flowing Perception
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You extend your Threads of Light and make an unarmed light attack against a target within range. On damage, you may pull yourself towards the target and sidestep to the nearest square within 5ft of them.

You may also use this on ally. If you do, this always hits and deals no damage. It still gives you a stack of Harmony.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Dharani: Blooming Conciousness
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You release all gathered harmony as petals of radiant light that spiral outward from your body.

You consume all Harmony stacks and 1 mana per stack to heal all allies within range for (6 + Your focus) / 2 for each stack consumed.

If you do not have enough mana for all stacks, this ability instead consumes stacks equal to the mana you can spend.

ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Must be in Luminous Form Stance and have 5 stacks of Harmony.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Must be in Luminous Form Stance and have 5 stacks of Harmony.

name: Flame Sentinel
guideHtml:
Flame Sentinels support their allies primarily through offense. Flame Weapon grants a small power boost to a weapon of an ally and lets them deal fire damage, which may be important against some enemies. Controlled Blaze will not prevent your allies from setting things on fire, so be careful when giving it out when flameable material is around.

Flame Shield allows you to do damage after an ally takes damage. It costs 1 RP, but its fully unavoidable and full pierce damage. If you hang out in the back and don't have much use for your RP, this can be an excellent way to spend it.

Flame Infusion is a situational ability. Against certain enemies it's extremely powerful and against others, its a detriment.

Flame Wall is a strong zoning tool, able to cut off a large portion of a fight. You can also use it as a damage tool, dropping it right on top of enemies to force them to use an RP. If they choose not to do so, or if they have no more RP to spent, then it is effectively a Sure Hit attack once their turn starts.

keyAbilityRef.name: Controlled Blaze
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When you deal Fire Damage, you may choose not to ignite flammable objects.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You gain Fire Mastery.

ability1Ref.name: Flame Infusion
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You surround a willing target in searing flames, changing their typing to Fire II. As a Fire II type, they take half damage from Fire and Frost Damage or attacks with the Fire or Frost keywords unless that attack deals true damage. They take 50% more damage from Water Damage or attacks with the Water keyword. While the ward lasts, they also gain immunity to Burning. The ward lasts until the end of the encounter or until their type is overwritten by a more powerful type.

As a Fire II type, they take half damage from Fire and Frost Damage or attacks with the Fire or Frost keywords unless that attack deals true damage. They take 50% more damage from Water Damage or attacks with the Water keyword. While the ward lasts, they also gain immunity to Burning. The ward lasts until the end of the encounter or until their type is overwritten by a more powerful type.

ability2Ref.name: Flame Shield
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: The attacker immediately takes Light Fire Damage.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: The attacker immediately takes Light Fire Damage.

ability3Ref.name: Flame Weapon
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You touch one willing ally’s or your own weapon and enchant it with Fire. Until the end of the encounter, the weapon deals Fire Damage and grants a +1 Power Enchantment.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You touch one willing ally’s or your own weapon and enchant it with Fire. Until the end of the encounter, the weapon deals Fire Damage and grants a +1 Power Enchantment.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Flame Wall
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You create a wall of Fire that is 30ft long, 5ft wide and 30ft high. This wall must be a straight line. Anyone passing through the wall or starting their turn in it takes Heavy Fire Damage. The wall lasts for 10 minutes or until canceled. Those in the wall when it is casted, may spend 1 RP to sidestep out of the wall. A person can only take this damage once per round.

Those in the wall when it is casted, may spend 1 RP to sidestep out of the wall. A person can only take this damage once per round.

name: Flash Star Blade Style
descriptionHtml: A blade style originating from Raijin swordmasters.  A notable practicioner of Flash Star Blade Style is Rin Tatsugami of Madeline.
descriptionText: A blade style originating from Raijin swordmasters.  A notable practicioner of Flash Star Blade Style is Rin Tatsugami of Madeline.
requirementsHtml: Any class mastered. Proficiency in one of light swords, longsword, katana or dueling weapon.
requirementsText: Any class mastered. Proficiency in one of light swords, longsword, katana or dueling weapon.
guideHtml:
The Flash Star Style is a sword style originating from the Raijin that requires precise positioning. Photon Line moves you exactly 25ft, not up to 25ft. This can make it difficult to use against a single target, since you'll often dash past the target and be unable to attack them without moving. Using it as a retreating option is a valid choice as you force the enemy to move to attack you.

If you do manage to line up the perfect Photon Line on the offense, you can combo it into Thunderbolt Slash. Thunderbolt Slash is 3 light attacks for the cost of 2 AP. With its mana cost reduced by the combo effect, it lines up perfectly for a 4 AP turn that doesn't cost any mana.

Starlight Crescent Reversal is a safe mana dump each turn, allowing for a cheap Light attack.

The Secret Art: Twin Nova Pursuit is an engagement and isolation tool. You are able to move a target 25ft or 50ft, depending on how they react, which can let you get a 1v1 when you remove an enemy from a melee brawl. It can also be used to engage and knock an enemy into your own allies, allowing them to pile on without having to move and most likely dragging that enemy out of range of any support abilities.

keyAbilityRef.name: Lightning Weapon
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Lightning Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare your chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

ability1Ref.name: Flash Star Style: Photon Line
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You sidestep exactly 25ft in a straight line in any direction and can move over gaps of terrain as long as you end on solid ground. You make a Light attack against all targets that are in your path.  If only one target is in your path, make a Heavy attack instead.

If you were flying at least 10ft above the ground when using this ability, you do not need to end on solid ground.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You sidestep exactly 25ft in a straight line in any direction and can move over gaps of terrain as long as you end on solid ground. You make a Light attack against all targets that are in your path.  If only one target is in your path, make a Heavy attack instead.

If you were flying at least 10ft above the ground when using this ability, you do not need to end on solid ground.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Can move 25ft in a straight line. Light sword, longsword, katana, dueling weapon only.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Can move 25ft in a straight line. Light sword, longsword, katana, dueling weapon only.

ability2Ref.name: Flash Star Style: Thunderbolt Slash
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You slash the target in a thunderbolt pattern, making a 3-hit attack.  Make 3 separate Light attacks against the target.  

Combo: If used immediately after Flash Star Style: Photon Line, mana cost reduced by 1.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You slash the target in a thunderbolt pattern, making a 3-hit attack.  Make 3 separate Light attacks against the target.

Combo: If used immediately after Flash Star Style: Photon Line, mana cost reduced by 1.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Light sword, Longsword, Katana, dueling weapon only.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Light sword, Longsword, Katana, dueling weapon only.

ability3Ref.name: Flash Star Style: Starlight Crescent Reversal
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You take advantage of the slow windup of the enemy’s attack, countering with a Light attack of your own. You may still Dodge or Block the triggering attack after this counter.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You take advantage of the slow windup of the enemy’s attack, countering with a Light attack of your own. You may still Dodge or Block the triggering attack after this counter.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: You are targeted by a heavy attack. Light sword, Longsword, Katana, dueling weapon only.
ability3Ref.requirementText: You are targeted by a heavy attack. Light sword, Longsword, Katana, dueling weapon only.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Flash Star Style Secret Art: Twin Nova Pursuit
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You teleport to the target and then knock them 25ft in any horizontal direction.  You then teleport in a straight line to them again and make a Heavy attack.  If the attack is responded to with any RP action, knock them straight back 25ft again, teleport in a straight line to them and make another Heavy attack.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You teleport to the target and then knock them 25ft in any horizontal direction.  You then teleport in a straight line to them again and make a Heavy attack.  If the attack is responded to with any RP action, knock them straight back 25ft again, teleport in a straight line to them and make another Heavy attack.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Light sword, Longsword, Katana, dueling weapon only.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Light sword, Longsword, Katana, dueling weapon only.

name: Flowing Wind Style
descriptionHtml:
The Flowing Wind Style was founded by Yun Fei of the Azure Cloud Sect, a cultivator and the first leader of the sect. She taught that freedom without motion is illusion, and motion without freedom is merely compulsion. To cultivate the wind is to accept that remaining unchanged is itself a form of confinement. These teachings shape the core beliefs behind the style and likewise the style encourages its students to accept those tenets.

Motion is continuity. A flowing thing may be slowed, turned aside or dispersed but it does not stop unless forced. Students are taught that every movement is an act of cultivation. To move is to remain free.

Freedom is choice and choice is power. Without the strength to resist being forced into an action, a person can never be truly free. Those that follow the Flowing Wind Style seek strength for that reason, to remain free until the very end.

She taught that freedom without motion is illusion, and motion without freedom is merely compulsion. To cultivate the wind is to accept that remaining unchanged is itself a form of confinement. These teachings shape the core beliefs behind the style and likewise the style encourages its students to accept those tenets.

Motion is continuity. A flowing thing may be slowed, turned aside or dispersed but it does not stop unless forced. Students are taught that every movement is an act of cultivation. To move is to remain free.

Freedom is choice and choice is power. Without the strength to resist being forced into an action, a person can never be truly free. Those that follow the Flowing Wind Style seek strength for that reason, to remain free until the very end.
requirementsHtml: Forager, Fighter, Rogue, Martial Artist, Cultivator or Adventurer mastered.
requirementsText: Forager, Fighter, Rogue, Martial Artist, Cultivator or Adventurer mastered.
guideHtml:
The Flowing Wind Style is a damage dealing class with some focus on mobility.

Cloudstep is your initial ability and it lets you move a short distance without provoking. While the distance is very short at the start, it's good for repositioning in melee and it gets buffed when combined with the Plume-Treading Art passive, which boosts your movement speed. When you get enough extra movement, the halved movement speed from Cloudstep matters less. It also lets you walk on air to reach flying targets without needing to fly yourself.

All this movement lets you build up stacks you can then proc with Flowing Wind Strike, which can deal up to +10 extra damage based off your stacks. 

You can also use your stacks to gain a bonus to your Dodge with Driftwind Evasion, which may let you avoid an attack you couldn't normally dodge at the cost of giving up damage.

Your Secret Art: Vanishing Thousand Steps lets you move freely across the battlefield for 1 turn. It can be great for both starting a fight by instantly reaching their backline or for catching runners towards the end of a fight.

keyAbilityRef.name: Cloudstep
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Cloudstep.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Cloudstep
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may Sidestep up to half your speed. This movement ignores difficult terrain.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may Sidestep up to half your speed. This movement ignores difficult terrain.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Must be the first movement action in your turn.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: Must be the first movement action in your turn.

ability1Ref.name: Plume-Treading Art
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
After you use Cloudstep, you treat air as regular walkable terrain until the end of your turn. At the end of your turn, if still standing on air and not Flying, you float back down to the ground without taking fall damage.

In addition, after each AP spent on the Move action or an ability that lets you move, you gain 1 Windflow up to a maximum of 6. Each stack of Windflow gives a +5 Windflow bonus to your movement speed. These stacks last until the end of the encounter or until you end your turn without having moved.

Cloudstep also always gives you 1 stack, up to your normal cap.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
After you use Cloudstep, you treat air as regular walkable terrain until the end of your turn. At the end of your turn, if still standing on air and not Flying, you float back down to the ground without taking fall damage.

In addition, after each AP spent on the Move action or an ability that lets you move, you gain 1 Windflow up to a maximum of 6. Each stack of Windflow gives a +5 Windflow bonus to your movement speed. These stacks last until the end of the encounter or until you end your turn without having moved.

Cloudstep also always gives you 1 stack, up to your normal cap.

ability2Ref.name: Flowing Wind Strike
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target that deals additional damage equal to double your Windflow stacks. Afterwards, you lose all Windflow stacks.

Afterwards, you may Sidestep 5ft though this movement does not give a Windflow stack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Light attack against the target that deals additional damage equal to double your Windflow stacks. Afterwards, you lose all Windflow stacks.

Afterwards, you may Sidestep 5ft though this movement does not give a Windflow stack.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Must have moved at least 10ft before using this.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Must have moved at least 10ft before using this.

ability3Ref.name: Driftwind Evasion
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You take the Dodge action against the attack and gain a Windflow bonus to your Dodge equal to your Windflow stacks.

Afterwards, you lose all Windflow stacks.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You take the Dodge action against the attack and gain a Windflow bonus to your Dodge equal to your Windflow stacks.

Afterwards, you lose all Windflow stacks.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Vanishing Thousand Steps
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You may sidestep to a target within range and make a Heavy attack against them. 

Then, until the end of the turn, you may Sidestep to any enemy you can see as part of a melee attack you are about to make. You gain Windflow stacks as if you spent AP on the movement equal to the AP spent on the attack.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You may sidestep to a target within range and make a Heavy attack against them.

Then, until the end of the turn, you may Sidestep to any enemy you can see as part of a melee attack you are about to make. You gain Windflow stacks as if you spent AP on the movement equal to the AP spent on the attack.

name: Forager
descriptionHtml:
From the lively activity of the deep forest to the serene stillness of the snowy tundra, a Forager was most at home wandering places no one has traveled through in decades (if ever). To take only what was needed, to preserve the beauty of what was; that was the motto of many Foragers. An ideal that caused natural tension between Foragers and Farmers but even the most steadfast Forager would admit that their ways wouldn’t be able to support the growing population of Lyr.

A true Forager could be recognized by their unique gathering tool: giant scissors. Named so due to their general construction of two handles with paired blades connected to a pivot point, the giant scissors were reinforced survival multi-tools. Designed for the specific needs of each Forager, the scissors often had blades large enough to cut a tree in half (or a man) and most came with sharpened tips that could be used like a spear when the tool was closed. Serrated or sharpened outer edges could allow for sawing and chopping wood, while the handles would sometimes have hooks to aid in pulling things down into easy reach. A difficult tool to master just for harvesting, those Foragers that trained to fight with them were unpredictable dervishes that utilized their unconventional weapon’s quirks to their advantage.

With a deep connection to the natural world around them and experience born from time in the wilderness, Foragers were never truly in danger of running out of even the most basic of supplies. Be it food and water or torches, ropes, and even shelters, a Forager could often find or fashion some manner of natural replacement. With a sharp eye for details honed by spotting tiny gatherable herbs and mushrooms (and identifying their properties at a glance), their versatility in supplies, and even their strange combat style, any adventure or expedition would do well to consider bringing a Forager along.

guideHtml:
The forager is a gatherer and utility class. Their primary use in combat is to use Lifebeat Sense to gather information on the HP status of enemies, as well as making them easier to be critically hit. This does cost mana, so be careful about overusing this.

Their second combat ability is vivisect, which is an instakill ability with severeal requirements to it. While it may look hard to use because of that, in practice you can get this off pretty reliably. Simply use Lifebeat Sense on a grunt you know is RP exhausted, and you can immediately Vivisect them after. The combo isn't cheap action or mana wise, but it cannot miss which is nice.

For gathering, you have Verdant Instinct and Divining Petalfall. These are best used together. An early Verdant Instinct can give you the information you need, then you can replace one with a 5 if needed which allows you to guarantee a Divining Petalfall for Lucky Points.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Foraging. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Medicine, Survival or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Reason or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Toughness or Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Forager's Path
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain proficiency in Giant Scissors and proficiency in your choice between Axes or Hori. You may use axes for harvesting foraging outcrops.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You gain the ability to harvest Tier 1 Foraging Outcrops.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You gain the ability to use natural item as replacements for an Adventurers Kit and Torches such as:  using the sun/moss as a compass, using vines as rope etc.  The material must be available (GM’s discretion)
keyAbilityRef.benefit4: You gain the ability to create Wood Blocks as an Errand.  See item: Carpenter Materials. You may use an IP to do this 4 times instead.

ability1Ref.name: Verdant Instinct
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: Make a gathering check and apply it to NP. After rolling, roll two more dice that will be used as your next 2 strike dice values. You may reorder them or replace one with a 5.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: Make a gathering check and apply it to NP. After rolling, roll two more dice that will be used as your next 2 strike dice values. You may reorder them or replace one with a 5.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must be in a gathering session.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must be in a gathering session.

ability2Ref.name: Lifebeat Sense
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You can assess the approximate HP percentage of the target until the end of combat.  (The DM should enable health bars for the unit, or you can ask for an approximate percentage). In addition, the target is afflicted by Expose 1, reducing the target’s crit threshold by 1. (20 to crit becomes 19.) Expose is removed once the target is critically hit (regardless of if they defend or not)
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You can assess the approximate HP percentage of the target until the end of combat.  (The DM should enable health bars for the unit, or you can ask for an approximate percentage). In addition, the target is afflicted by Expose 1, reducing the target’s crit threshold by 1. (20 to crit becomes 19.) Expose is removed once the target is critically hit (regardless of if they defend or not)

ability3Ref.name: Divining Petalfall
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: Make a gathering check with a +5 bonus. If the final dice value is even, add it to Node Points. If it’s odd, add it to Lucky Points.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: Make a gathering check with a +5 bonus. If the final dice value is even, add it to Node Points. If it’s odd, add it to Lucky Points.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Must be in a gathering session.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Must be in a gathering session.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Vivisect
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You cut the target in half with your scissors or chainsaw, killing them instantly.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You cut the target in half with your scissors or chainsaw, killing them instantly.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Giant Scissors or Chainsaw only. Target is affected by Expose and is a grunt.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Giant Scissors or Chainsaw only. Target is affected by Expose and is a grunt.

name: Forgemaster
descriptionHtml: Crafters who supply the world's weapons.
descriptionText: Crafters who supply the world's weapons.
guideHtml:
The forgemaster retains their crafting focus and is able to craft higher quality items. With Inspired Finish you can add a large boost to your craft at the very end. However, you can't do anything else after that so you might have to take a risk and apply the bonuses early, then hope you roll high enough.

Forged Knowledge II now work on the weapons of allies as well, though you still need to have made their weapon. it also maximizes all the damage dice, which can create a sizeable damage increase on a bad damage roll.

Secret Art: Shatter is either a Sure Hit heavy attack that deals good damage, or if they block it you destroy their weapon. Powerful against enemies that uses weapons, but it won't have much use against non-humanoids.

FAQ:

Q: How does inspired finish interact with a Westrian Style tool?
A: All 3 dice become a 5.

skills: You gain +5 skill points in Blacksmithing.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Power, Focus or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Focused Weapons
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Forgemaster Core Crafting Abilities.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You can craft using Mithril and Orihalcum.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Forgemaster Core Crafting
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Blacksmith’s Modification B
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an B-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon or Crafting mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
Blacksmith’s Modification B
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description:
You add an B-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon or Crafting mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

ability1Ref.name: Inspired Finish
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: Make a crafting check. The dice is upgraded to 3d10 and you still add your crafting bonus to this. The crafting immediately ends after using this ability and you may not spend Crafting Points to add artisan bonuses, alloys or similar things.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: Make a crafting check. The dice is upgraded to 3d10 and you still add your crafting bonus to this. The crafting immediately ends after using this ability and you may not spend Crafting Points to add artisan bonuses, alloys or similar things.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: This is your last crafting dice. Costs 1 Crafting Dice.
ability1Ref.requirementText: This is your last crafting dice. Costs 1 Crafting Dice.

ability2Ref.name: Forged Knowledge II
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: Your bond with the weapon allows you to infuse it with mana at a crucial point. Use when determining attack damage, maximize all weapon dice for that attack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: Your bond with the weapon allows you to infuse it with mana at a crucial point. Use when determining attack damage, maximize all weapon dice for that attack.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: You or an ally attacked with a weapon you crafted.
ability2Ref.requirementText: You or an ally attacked with a weapon you crafted.

ability3Ref.name: Expertise Study (Blacksmithing)
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You gain 5 Expertise Points in 3 different blacksmithing crafts. You also gain wielding proficiency in one smithing melee weapon of your choice. You may select specialized weapons with this.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You gain 5 Expertise Points in 3 different blacksmithing crafts. You also gain wielding proficiency in one smithing melee weapon of your choice. You may select specialized weapons with this.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Shatter
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy Attack against the target using a Blacksmith’s Hammer. If the target chooses to Block the attack, they gain 100 damage reduction against it. If they do, choose one equipped non-divine arms weapon in their hand that has equal or less crafted enhancements than your hammer and destroy that item, giving it the Broken condition.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Heavy Attack against the target using a Blacksmith’s Hammer. If the target chooses to Block the attack, they gain 100 damage reduction against it. If they do, choose one equipped non-divine arms weapon in their hand that has equal or less crafted enhancements than your hammer and destroy that item, giving it the Broken condition.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Attack must be made with a Blacksmith's Hammer.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Attack must be made with a Blacksmith's Hammer.

name: Geomancer
descriptionHtml: A magic user that focuses on manipulating the earth around them.
descriptionText: A magic user that focuses on manipulating the earth around them.
requirementsText: Any class mastered or Earth mastered.
guideHtml:
A Geomancer is a controller that uses shapes the battlefield to their benefit. Fissure is their main control spell that creates a large hole in the ground that enemies will have to get out of. This can be powerful when combined with other tools such as a Flame Oil Flask. People at the edges may step out of the fissure by spending an RP, but then you have the option of hitting them with a Seismic Shove. 

Seismic Shove is a unique forced movement ability in that it lets you push an enemy in any direction, instead of only backwards. If you hit an enemy with it, you can toss them into the Fissure they just avoided.

Mud Wall is another area control spell that you can use. You can spend AP on it to either build cover for yourself and your allies, or create a small patch of difficult terrain for the enemies. When used a reaction, you can use the cover creating part of it to turn a potential hit into a miss or use it to make an enemy be unable to reach your allies. You can also use it in combination with the Fissure ability to create a sudden wall for when an enemy attempts to jump out of the hole.

Secret Art: Earthquake is a wide hitting spell that does low damage, but knocks enemies prone. Its main strength is the extremely long range on it and the bonus damage it does against magically created objects. The last part may not come up often, but it will be devastating when it does. Be aware that it will also destroy your own mud walls.

Finally, Tremorsense is a simple spell that lets you temporarily become aware of creatures. Useful for spotting out potential assassins, but it can also let you do things like position a fissure through a wall.

skills: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Tremorsense
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Earth Mastery.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use Tremorsense

associatedAbilityRef.name: Tremorsense
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You become aware of the presence and exact location of any creature within range that is touching the ground until the end of your turn.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to increase the range by an additional 40ft per mana spent.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You become aware of the presence and exact location of any creature within range that is touching the ground until the end of your turn.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to increase the range by an additional 40ft per mana spent.

ability1Ref.name: Mud Wall
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You create a wall 4ft high, 1ft thick and up to 20ft long that serves as low cover. Anybody attempting to get over the wall treats it as difficult terrain. Walls must be placed on solid ground and cannot be stacked on top of eachother.

Each 5ft of wall has 60 HP and dissolves if it reaches 0. It otherwise lasts until the end of the encounter.

You may spend 1 RP instead of 2 AP on this ability instead.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You create a wall 4ft high, 1ft thick and up to 20ft long that serves as low cover. Anybody attempting to get over the wall treats it as difficult terrain. Walls must be placed on solid ground and cannot be stacked on top of eachother.

Each 5ft of wall has 60 HP and dissolves if it reaches 0. It otherwise lasts until the end of the encounter.

You may spend 1 RP instead of 2 AP on this ability instead.

ability2Ref.name: Fissure
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You slam the ground and create a 15ft wide fissure up to the target point. Those at the edges of the spell’s range may spend 1 RP to Sidestep 5ft out of the effect. All caught in the effect fall down 20ft into the fissure and take falling damage as normal.

The spell requires natural ground to be below the touching surface to function. It may work on the bottom floor of a house, but will fail if used higher up or in some dungeons.

ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Special
ability2Ref.requirementText: Special

ability3Ref.name: Seismic Shove
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light attack that deals Earth damage against the target. On damage, you may push the target 5ft in any direction.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light attack that deals Earth damage against the target. On damage, you may push the target 5ft in any direction.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Earthquake
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light attack that deals Earth damage against all enemies in range that are touching the ground. On damage, they fall prone.

You also make a Light attack that deals Earth damage against all magically created walls and similar inanimate objects created by magic. You deal 10x damage against those targets.

You make a Light attack that deals Earth damage against all enemies in range that are touching the ground. On damage, they fall prone.

You also make a Light attack that deals Earth damage against all magically created walls and similar inanimate objects created by magic. You deal 10x damage against those targets.

name: Geomaster
descriptionHtml:
The caravan is stopped. The bridge over the ravine is gone, scrap and splinter of it remaining on either side of the more than one hundred twenty foot gap in the earth. Did it collapse from the weather or an earthquake? Or was it deliberately destroyed by Northi sappers, flaunting their new air superiority of Sorthens logistical lines? It didnt matter, the bridge was gone and the evacuation toward Marlone was stopped dead in its tracks. Teyo LuSolana scowled at the gap, the earth shivering with each impatient tap of her foot.

The leaders of the caravan were arguing over alternative routes or taking the time to rebuild the bridge. They were assuming General ArGolan would be able to hold off the might of the combined Northi-Westria forces. That they had time for alternative routes or bridge building. Teyo knew if they stayed here, they would be caught against this ravine and slaughtered. And she had been tasked by the General to get this caravan safely to Marlone.

Were crossing, she declared loudly, interrupting the debate.

One of the men nervously looked at his compatriots and then reluctantly stepped forward. With all due respect, Lady LuSolana, the decision hasnt been made yet. Well take your input into consideration as a vote for a bridge, but--

Teyo scoffed and turned away, walking toward the edge of the ravine. No, were crossing now. Weve wasted enough time.

She ignored the indignant officials shouting at her as she plants her feet at the point where stone meets open air. Mana surged through her body, her blood singing, begging to release its shackles. She held that urge back and crossed her arms imperiously. Then, with a stomp of her foot, the earth heaved beneath her and she rode a bridge of earth across the ravine where it anchored solidly with the opposite wall as if it had always been.

The new stone bridge is twenty feet wide and gently arched for support. With a few twists of her feet and guidance from her hands, support pillars rose from the ravine floor to shore up the structure of the bridge and ensure it wouldnt buckle under the load. Finally, Teyo let a shuddering breath escape her lips but didnt let the exhaustion of shaping that much earth show. She just stands to the side of the new bridge as the awestruck evacuees trundle past her in wagons and carts. She only followed once the last of the refugees were across and with a violent twist of her feet, the entire bridge collapsed in a roar of falling stone.

They were safe, for now, and free to get to the ships that would take them to Marlone in the Bay.

guideHtml:
Geomaster is primarily a controller class like the geomancer.

Earthen Divide lets you split up the battlefield by just creating a massive wall. Powerful even outdoors to block ranged attacks, but especially if inside a cave or dungeon of some sort where there might not be a way to get around the wall and enemies have to force their way through.

Wall Ride is a simple mobility tool that lets you reposition after using Mud Wall or Earthen Divide. If you do a very long Earthen Divide, Wall Ride can let you move a super long distance for free.

Pillar of Earth is a manaless construction tool with the limit of having to place it below someone. Apart from creating cover, you can use it to either push up an enemy or an ally to get them out of attack of opportunity range.

Spike Field is a nasty field hazard that will quickly chew through the HP of any grunt or even heroic enemy that simply goes through it at full speed. Be aware that it does affect allies as well.

Finally, Second Tremor gives you increased controlling power against heroics. The only downside of it is that you may struggle to reliably activate it often without the support of allies, but it can be devastating if combined with reliable sources of prone such as leg sweep or wire sweep. As a pure geomancer, you'll most likely activate this purely off earthquake and fissures by yourself.

Wall Ride is a simple mobility tool that lets you reposition after using Mud Wall or Earthen Divide. If you do a very long Earthen Divide, Wall Ride can let you move a super long distance for free.

Pillar of Earth is a manaless construction tool with the limit of having to place it below someone. Apart from creating cover, you can use it to either push up an enemy or an ally to get them out of attack of opportunity range.

Spike Field is a nasty field hazard that will quickly chew through the HP of any grunt or even heroic enemy that simply goes through it at full speed. Be aware that it does affect allies as well.

Finally, Second Tremor gives you increased controlling power against heroics. The only downside of it is that you may struggle to reliably activate it often without the support of allies, but it can be devastating if combined with reliable sources of prone such as leg sweep or wire sweep. As a pure geomancer, you'll most likely activate this purely off earthquake and fissures by yourself.
skills: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Wall Ride
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When you create a wall on your turn using your Geomancer or Geomaster abilities, you may move along the wall as it is created. You can jump off at any point of the wall creation, ending in an empty square next to the wall.

ability1Ref.name: Earthen Divide
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You create a wall up to 20ft high, 5ft wide and 20ft long in a straight line. Each Segment of the wall has HP equal to 20 + Focus.

Overcharge: You may spend X mana to increase the length by 20 x Xft.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You create a wall up to 20ft high, 5ft wide and 20ft long in a straight line. Each Segment of the wall has HP equal to 20 + Focus.

Overcharge: You may spend X mana to increase the length by 20 x Xft.

ability2Ref.name: Pillar of Earth
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You create a 5x5, 20ft high pillar below someone. The target may spend 1 RP to step out of the way.

Each block of the pillar has HP equal to 20+Focus and the entire pillar collapses if one block is removed from it.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You create a 5x5, 20ft high pillar below someone. The target may spend 1 RP to step out of the way.

Each block of the pillar has HP equal to 20+Focus and the entire pillar collapses if one block is removed from it.

ability3Ref.name: Spike Field
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You create a 20x20ft field of spikes. Any creature moving more than 5ft per movement action across it takes full pierce damage equal to your Focus for each 5ft they move across the field.

If the movement through it was forced movement, you only take the damage once.

Lasts until the start of your next turn.

Upkeep: 1 mana, extends the duration of the spike field by 1 turn.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You create a 20x20ft field of spikes. Any creature moving more than 5ft per movement action across it takes full pierce damage equal to your Focus for each 5ft they move across the field.

If the movement through it was forced movement, you only take the damage once.

Lasts until the start of your next turn.

Upkeep: 1 mana, extends the duration of the spike field by 1 turn.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Second Tremor
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: The enemy falls prone and takes Light damage.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: The enemy falls prone and takes Light damage.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: An enemy stands up from prone or takes falling damage without falling prone.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: An enemy stands up from prone or takes falling damage without falling prone.

name: Grandmaster
descriptionHtml:
Isabelle Un Lavi scowled when her healed boots crunched against the wooden debris of the front door as she stepped outside. The big bruiser of a bandit lay sprawled where shed tossed him through the door. Well, she hadnt. Stone grinding against wood announced the arrival of her very effective bodyguard. The intricately carved visage of a Westrian Knight came to a rest at the foot of the stairs, the sunlight gleaming off the white marble stone. She took the time to straighten her sleeves and brush her hair back with a flick of her wrist.

Gentlemen, this is the single most foolish attempt at robbery I have ever seen, she said to the half dozen other bandits watching her warily (one was trying to wake up the big bruiser of a leader without much luck). And normally, I would be delighted to entertain you until the sheriff arrives to collect you. Her tail flicked in irritation. But unfortunately, youve gone and destroyed a perfectly wonderful door on my masters house and that I cannot abide. No, youll just have to pay for that.

This is stupid! Its just the maid and an enchanted statue! one of the bandits shouted, either the second in command or the loudmouth that wished he was. Whoever he was, he was inspiring enough to get them motivated to commit to the robbery again and the new leader was charging right at her, spear held like a lance.

Pawn to E4, she said. The sound of stone grinding was all the warning the loudmouth had before a stone statue of a Westrian Squire grew from the ground in front of him. His spear chipped a chunk off the pawns shoulder but the pawn buried its stone sword through the bandits torso. It carelessly dropped the corpse and took a step forward, visibly morphing into a regal Westrian Priest, white stone robes pristine and an ornate, heavy stone mace in its hands.

Faced with two enchanted statues, one unconscious leader, and one dead loudmouth, the remaining bandits seemed to think it was time to make a run for it.

I dont think so, Isabelle said, gesturing for her Bishop and Knight to move. The sounds of stone slamming against flesh and the screams of their fellows drove the bandits that had slipped by her creations to make a full sprint for the gates. With a twist of her wrist and a snap of her fingers, stone rose up out of the ground between them and safety. The stone face of Saint Rebekah stared down at them as they stumbled to a halt.

Im afraid this is Checkmate, gentlemen.

guideHtml:
The grandmaster is ranged striker that builds up their collection of pieces over the course of the battle that they use to attack enemies.

Summon Piece is your first ability and the only way to generate the pieces you need for your other ability. A pawn is the least versatile, but the only piece that doesn't require mana. What you summon will depend on what you are facing, as it may be easier to promote your pieces against heavy tank enemies or bosses who are more likely to just tank a heavy. If you need to set up a piece to swap to with castling, then you have no choice but to summon a rook.

The most important ability for dealing damage is Move Piece, which moves a piece you control as if it was a chess piece. At 1 AP 1 mana for heavy, it's quite good at dealing damage though it comes with the condition that your pieces must be in position to make use of it.

Castling is a simple swap ability. You can use it to both get yourself out of danger or to position a rook into a spot it can attack from.

Check can be used to force an enemy to move or accept taking an additional heavy attack. Against enemies that can't move for free, you're at worst trading 1 AP for 1 AP. Even if an enemy moves out of your check, the free move piece lets you set up for a real Move Piece right after.

Checkmate is your secret art and an ability that gains more value the longer a fight goes. Every piece that's in position is 1 heavy attack, so the maximum amount of heavy attacks is equal to the amount of turns in the battle assuming you've spent every turn using one.

skills: You gain +5 to Deception, Linguistics, Magic, Appraise, History, Artifice, Insight, Art, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Puppet Grandmaster
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You can summon puppets under your control using Summon Piece. While these puppets follow movement rules as described in the abilities and are always Medium size, they can appear as whatever you wish, decided when you take this class.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: As an EP action, you may change the appearance of your pieces.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Summon Piece
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You summon a Pawn puppet under your control. It (and other puppets from this class) have HP equal to 20 + Focus, no evasion and no guard. These puppets are not living targets and are generally treated as structures rather than creatures.

They last until the end of the encounter.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to summon the following puppets instead:

1 Mana: Knight or Bishop

2 Mana: Rook

3 Mana: Queen
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You summon a Pawn puppet under your control. It (and other puppets from this class) have HP equal to 20 + Focus, no evasion and no guard. These puppets are not living targets and are generally treated as structures rather than creatures.

They last until the end of the encounter.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to summon the following puppets instead:

1 Mana: Knight or Bishop

2 Mana: Rook

3 Mana: Queen

ability1Ref.name: Move Piece
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You move a puppet piece you can see that's under your control. The way it moves depends on the type of piece. When a piece moves, it's a Sidestep.

Pawn: Moves up to 2 squares orthogonally or 1 square diagonally in a straight line.

Knight: Moves 1 square orthogonally, then 1 square diagonal in the same direction it moved in orthogonally (like a knight in chess). Can pass through allies and enemies.

Bishop: Moves up to 8 squares diagonally in a straight line. 

Rook: Moves up to 8 squares orthogonally in a straight line.

Queen: Moves up to 8 squares orthogonally or diagonally in a straight line.

Except for the Knight, chess pieces cannot move through allies and stop their movement when they hit an object or creature. If this happens, it makes a Heavy attack that deals Physical(Bludgeoning) damage against that target with your stats. A knight that makes an attack and does not kill the target remains in its original spot.

On damage, a piece is promoted into a higher rank (Pawn -> Bishop/Knight -> Rook -> Queen). A queen cannot promote.

On Killing Blow, a piece occupies the square of the thing it attacked.

On Killing Blow, a piece occupies the square of the thing it attacked.

ability2Ref.name: Castling
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You swap positions with a Rook puppet piece you control.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You swap positions with a Rook puppet piece you control.

ability3Ref.name: Check
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You move a puppet piece in such a way (following Move Piece rules) that it can attack an enemy if it moves again. It cannot attack with this movement.

At the start of your next turn, you may move that piece as if you used Move Piece without spending AP/Mana or it counting as an ability use.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You move a puppet piece in such a way (following Move Piece rules) that it can attack an enemy if it moves again. It cannot attack with this movement.

At the start of your next turn, you may move that piece as if you used Move Piece without spending AP/Mana or it counting as an ability use.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Checkmate
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may move all your pieces as if you used Move Piece on them.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may move all your pieces as if you used Move Piece on them.

name: Granite Warden
descriptionHtml:
The whistle of the cannonball-like projectile shrieked through the air only to be intercepted by a slab of stone erupting from the ground. The projectile and wall shattered and Taran burst through the debris and dust, his massive form like a wall in its own right as he brought his axe down in a devastating strike against one of the many Kratos swarming toward them, the shockwave shattering more than the one hed directly struck.

Keep those walls coming, Bethan! Were almost there!

Bethan barely had the breath to sprint to keep up with her husband, each of her steps bringing an echo of the battlefield and everything touching the ground. The large Fiend behind the small army of lesser Fiends sucked up another one of its minions, the creature screaming as it was chewed and molded into another projectile. It fired again at Taran but she didnt have to intervene as he deflected the cannonball with a fist infused with stone.

A stomp of her dainty foot caused a fissure to spring open along Tarans flank, swallowing a couple Kratos but mostly preventing the large group from jumping him right as he finally reached the Fiend that was spawning them. She slid her foot, scraping against the rock and she lifted her hand. Another wall shot up between Taran and the Fiend, catching a heavy claw blow that would have caught her husband full in the side. That left him with every opportunity to bring his axe down in a mana fueled blow.

Ten minutes later, they were standing among the evaporating remains of the Fiends and the rubble of her protective walls, cracks in the earth, and stone projectiles shed created during the fight.

That was closer than I like, Taran said tiredly as she tied a bandage around his arm. 

We handled it, she said, reaching out to take his face in her small hands. No one in the village was hurt, no homes were damaged.

I know. But this is the third attack this month. There must be a Dungeon nearby, somewhere.

Thats a problem for adventurers, she insisted. Youre not dragging me off to clear a Dungeon. We have kids, Taran.

I know&but&

Twenty years ago, we wouldnt have hesitated, I know. Bethan shifted his face until he was looking directly at her. But we have something more important than our old glory days, now. Well send a message to Mothergreen and Northi to get some help with this when we get back home.

Taran smiled and scooped her up into his arms and stood, drawing a laugh out of her. What did I ever do to deserve such a wonderful wife?

Because youre a wonderful husband. Also, the muscles didnt hurt.

guideHtml:
Granite Warden is a support class that excels in buffing the defense on your allies.

Earth Armor increases the guard of an ally's armor, effectively making it 1 tier higher than it is. Note that you can't cast it on yourself.

Earth Infusion is a situational spell like all infusions. It reduces the damage from Lightning and Earth, which are both decent damage types to mitigate if you face an enemy that uses them.

Steady Ground is primarily a reaction tool to prevent an ally from getting knocked prone or if they would be pushed out of position. It does cost an RP, so it may not be worth usign every time.

Earth Shield is your primary tool in combat allowing you to exchange your RP to give someone else an RP against the most common attacks. It also gives a sizeable amount of Block if you have high focus, which can help low guard allies against attacks they can't dodge.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Tremorsense
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Earth Mastery.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use Tremorsense

associatedAbilityRef.name: Tremorsense
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You become aware of the presence and exact location of any creature within range that is touching the ground until the end of your turn.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to increase the range by an additional 40ft per mana spent.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You become aware of the presence and exact location of any creature within range that is touching the ground until the end of your turn.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to increase the range by an additional 40ft per mana spent.

ability1Ref.name: Steady Ground
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
The target gains Immovable until the start of their next turn.

You may also use this for 2 AP instead.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
The target gains Immovable until the start of their next turn.

You may also use this for 2 AP instead.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: The target must be touching the ground.
ability1Ref.requirementText: The target must be touching the ground.

ability2Ref.name: Earth Armor
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You touch a willing ally’s armor and enchant it with earthen power. Until the end of the encounter, it grants a +1 guard and +4 block bonus.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You touch a willing ally’s armor and enchant it with earthen power. Until the end of the encounter, it grants a +1 guard and +4 block bonus.

ability3Ref.name: Earth Infusion
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You surround a willing target in mud and rocks, changing their typing to Earth II. 

As an Earth II type, they take half damage from Lightning and Earth damage or attacks with the Lightning or Earth keyword unless that attack deals true damage. They take 50% more damage from Wind damage or attacks with the Wind keyword.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You surround a willing target in mud and rocks, changing their typing to Earth II.

As an Earth II type, they take half damage from Lightning and Earth damage or attacks with the Lightning or Earth keyword unless that attack deals true damage. They take 50% more damage from Wind damage or attacks with the Wind keyword.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Earth Shield
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You grant the target 1 RP they must spend on the Block action against the triggering attack. They gain a bonus to their Block for this action equal to your focus x 2.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You grant the target 1 RP they must spend on the Block action against the triggering attack. They gain a bonus to their Block for this action equal to your focus x 2.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: An ally within range is the target of a melee attack and/or physical attack.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: An ally within range is the target of a melee attack and/or physical attack.

name: Gravitect
descriptionHtml:
Storm clouds swirled unnaturally overhead as if drawn to a singular point directly over the ruins of the temple. Seren waited, her robes and tail tugging in the swirling winds. The adventurers finally stepped out of the intact section of the temple, a ramshackle group that proudly displayed the colors of the Dragon Claw Alliance in some way or another: a scarf, a patch, a tunic, an armband. They were a standard seeming group with a heavy frontline and ranged support, well suited for the variety of dangers found when traveling within the Holy Astra Line.

Its over, Seren! Your reign of terror ends today! the leader shouted, a rabbitfolk man in heavy plate.

All of your followers are dead, theres nowhere to run! growled the hulking bulk of a bearfolk as he flexed his clawed fists.

The other two, a rogue and a priestess, stayed silent, watching her warily.

That is quite the thing to say in such a holy place, she said calmly. Here I am, attempting to restore the world to its proper balance, righting the wrongs placed at the feet of the Holy Mother, Heira. And you can only scream and shout and kill. Ungrateful. Unacceptable.

She raised her hand. You are in the presence of a High Priestess of Heira! I am Seren Rhys and you! Will! KNEEL!

Her hand dropped and the world dropped with it. Cracks spread across the worn stone beneath the DCA partys feet and they each fell to their knees at the sheer weight crushing them down, even the mighty bearfolk. 

You have spat on Her Grace for the last time!

With a roar, the adventurers pushed themselves to their feet and charged at her. Gravity shifted and warped at her command, stone flew and shattered, and spells flew. The sanctity of the ancient temple was disgraced by purified and unpurified blood alike. And she would make them pay for every transgression!

requirementsHtml: Mage mastered and one tier 2 class mastered.
requirementsText: Mage mastered and one tier 2 class mastered.
guideHtml:
The Gravitect is a controller class with a large focus on moving allies and enemies.

Gravitational Push is your basic tool for this working on both allies and enemies and not allowing for a save of any kind. You can pull an ally out of danger or push an enemy away from them, pull an enemy closer to your allies to gang up on them.

Singularity Collapse is an AoE pull that helps you set up for future AoE attacks such as Fireball, Fissure etc.

Weight of the World is a power anti-air and anti-jump tool that cancels movement and punishes something for trying to leave the ground in your range.

Black Hole is a relatively cheap Secret Art that will likely affect most of the map and be a constant annoyance. It may also cause problems for your own allies by moving them out of range, so be aware of that.

Gravity Master key ability primary use is to let you work around sources of immovable. Since most of your stuff revolves around forcefully moving people, it lets you spend a mana to still be able to affect targets that go temporarily immune to it.
