Gravitational Push is your basic tool for this working on both allies and enemies and not allowing for a save of any kind. You can pull an ally out of danger or push an enemy away from them, pull an enemy closer to your allies to gang up on them.

Singularity Collapse is an AoE pull that helps you set up for future AoE attacks such as Fireball, Fissure etc.

Weight of the World is a power anti-air and anti-jump tool that cancels movement and punishes something for trying to leave the ground in your range.

Black Hole is a relatively cheap Secret Art that will likely affect most of the map and be a constant annoyance. It may also cause problems for your own allies by moving them out of range, so be aware of that.

Gravity Master key ability primary use is to let you work around sources of immovable. Since most of your stuff revolves around forcefully moving people, it lets you spend a mana to still be able to affect targets that go temporarily immune to it.
skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Appraise, Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Gravity Master
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When you push or pull an enemy on your turn, you may spend 1 mana to increase this distance by 10ft. You may also spend 1 mana to instead ignore the effects of 1 source of Immovable on a target you’re attempting to push, unless that target is permanently Immovable.

ability1Ref.name: Gravitational Push
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You push the target 20ft or pull it 20ft towards you.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You push the target 20ft or pull it 20ft towards you.

ability2Ref.name: Singularity Collapse
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You target a 25x25 square and all creatures within that square must make a save or be pulled 10ft towards the center.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You target a 25x25 square and all creatures within that square must make a save or be pulled 10ft towards the center.

ability3Ref.name: Weight of the World
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You increase the target’s gravity, reducing their elevation by 40ft. This may interrupt movement and if you reduce their elevation down to 0, you slam them into the ground dealing fall damage for the distance they failed to move and knocking them prone.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You increase the target’s gravity, reducing their elevation by 40ft. This may interrupt movement and if you reduce their elevation down to 0, you slam them into the ground dealing fall damage for the distance they failed to move and knocking them prone.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Target enemy is flying or jumping.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Target enemy is flying or jumping.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Black Hole
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You create a Black Hole at a target location within range. When you cast this and when you pay upkeep, all enemies within 120ft of the black hole must make a save or get pulled 20ft towards it.

All enemies within 120ft of the black hole that are moving away from it are considered slowed for that movement.

Upkeep: 1 Mana. Instead of paying upkeep, you may detonate the Black Hole making a Heavy attack against all enemies within 15ft of the black hole.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You create a Black Hole at a target location within range. When you cast this and when you pay upkeep, all enemies within 120ft of the black hole must make a save or get pulled 20ft towards it.

All enemies within 120ft of the black hole that are moving away from it are considered slowed for that movement.

Upkeep: 1 Mana. Instead of paying upkeep, you may detonate the Black Hole making a Heavy attack against all enemies within 15ft of the black hole.

name: Graviturge
descriptionHtml:
The sound of combat was all around her but seemed muted and distant. Kumiko pushed herself painfully to her feet, one arm hanging limp at her side and dripping blood steadily onto the stone beneath her feet. The blade of her katana shook slightly as she brought it up into a poor reflection of a proper stance. Opposite her, staring at her without emotion and unblinking eyes, was the Jiangshi cultist leader. Kumiko grit her teeth just looking at the cultist that was wearing her mothers face.

I expected more out of your Team, the cultist said flatly, stepping forward in the slow, deliberate advance shed used the entire fight. Her giant axe dragged along behind her, carving a furrow in the stone with its sheer weight.

Kumikos Team was still fighting, engaging the other cultists and akuma they had summoned. All of them were Ascendant rank and they were being pushed back by the sheer weight of numbers in this attack. They were one of the fifty Teams spread across Ezochi to contain any akuma incursions. She had already signaled to them that they had a major incursion and the lack of any response likely meant there were similar attacks happening elsewhere. Hopefully, none as strong as what was hitting Team 44.

You think youre winning here? Kumiko demanded with more bravado than she felt.

No, Ive won here.

That was the only warning before the cultist leader rushed forward, axe swinging from on high in a crushing blow that she knew she couldnt avoid or block. Kumiko stared defiantly, unwilling to blink or look away from her end.

Which is why she caught the air bend like a distorted mirror so much so that for a moment, she could see the cultists back reflected above her. Then the air cracked and the leader was crushed into the stone. A second, louder crack slammed into the ground next to her and a petite, dark haired Oni stood calmly with her hands pressed together like she was holding a trapped bug.

Well done holding the line, she said. The earth shook and the horrifying sound of dozens of bones snapping at once filled the air. The Oni, the Dark Radiance Natsuki herself, clapped her hands and then swept them out toward the others fighting, hurling cultists and akuma away like play things. Of the cultist leader, only a crumpled mess of an axe and blood stained stone remained. Pull Team 44 back, Ill handle this incursion from here.

requirementsHtml: Mage or Sorcerer mastered and one tier 2 class mastered.
requirementsText: Mage or Sorcerer mastered and one tier 2 class mastered.
guideHtml:
The Graviturge is a straightforward striker class that excels vs larger targets.

Mass Index gives you a small damage boost against large and huge creatures. Gravity Crush is a short range spell of which the main goal is to let you activate this damage boost as basic attack replacements.

Compression is a simple 1 AP heavy attack that gets a small discount against large targets.

Density Break is your 1 AP filler move for the average rotation of 2 AP heavy, compression, 1 AP spare. It benefits from Mass Index as well and will likely outperform a light against boss type enemies.

Localized Collapse is technically an AoE, but is more effective when used against single targets assuming you have decent Focus and won't do much against smaller ones. Because it's not a spell, it cannot benefit from Mass Index.

Absolute Compression is your secret art and one you can kind of toss out whenever. Heroics will likely dodge this and spend the extra RP from trick attack and while bosses can tank it, double heavy full pierce will be good damage regardless.

heart: You gain +1 to Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Mass Index
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: Your spells with the Gravity keyword deal extra damage to the target based on its size. They deal an additional 1 damage to large creatures and an additional 3 to Huge creatures.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use Gravity Crush.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Gravity Crush
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Physical (Crushing) damage.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Physical (Crushing) damage.

ability1Ref.name: Compression
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Heavy attack against the target that deals Physical (Crushing) damage.

If the target is Large Size or bigger, the mana cost of this is reduced by 1.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Heavy attack against the target that deals Physical (Crushing) damage.

If the target is Large Size or bigger, the mana cost of this is reduced by 1.

ability2Ref.name: Density Break
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Precise attack that deals damage equal to the target’s Toughness.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Precise attack that deals damage equal to the target’s Toughness.

ability3Ref.name: Localized Collapse
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You target a 15x15ft square. All in the area take damage equal to your Focus / 2. If a creature occupies multiple squares in your target square, they take damage for each square they occupy.

A creature caught in it may spend 1 RP to avoid the effect.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You target a 15x15ft square. All in the area take damage equal to your Focus / 2. If a creature occupies multiple squares in your target square, they take damage for each square they occupy.

A creature caught in it may spend 1 RP to avoid the effect.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Absolute Compression
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make an attack against the target that deals Double Heavy Physical(Crushing) damage.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make an attack against the target that deals Double Heavy Physical(Crushing) damage.

name: Great Berserker
descriptionHtml: Offensive powerhouses that overwhelm enemies with sheer force.
descriptionText: Offensive powerhouses that overwhelm enemies with sheer force.
guideHtml:
Great Berserker are high risk, offensive powerhouses.

Single-Minded Fury makes them focus on that 1 person, gaining a ton of bonuses and immunities, but being effectively useless against other enemies until that target is dead. Using it early or against the wrong target can make you ineffective in the fight. For example, a flying enemy may be unreachable for several turns in which you'd be able to do little.

Simply running away is not much of an option however. Unrelenting assault prevents them from escaping through any means other than teleporting.

Smash through the pain gives great berserkers a bit of survivability, though at the cost of using that RP to do more damage. It's pretty efficient and it stacks with your normal guard bonus.

Victory or Death is a double-edged sword. Doing maximum damage is obviously good, but if you fail to finish off the enemy or enemies, then you will take a ton of damage on the turn after. It is best used when needing to finish off a high priority target or near the end of a fight. Because of the difference in damage rolls sizes, it is best used solely with heavy attacks. There's little benefit to maximum damage light attacks in most cases.

skills: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Perception, Survival, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness.
soul: You gain +1 Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Giant Strength
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: While in Rage Stance, you may choose to have your size be treated as one size bigger than it actually is. You get an additional +5 bonus to Fitness checks during Rage stance regardless.

ability1Ref.name: Unrelenting Assault
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may use this ability when an enemy willingly moves out of your melee weapon range to make a Heavy attack against them. You can use this even if they took the disengage action or a similar action that would not make them provoke attacks of opportunity. If the target uses a teleport type move, then you cannot use this ability, unless they Teleport into your weapon range.

This is treated as an opportunity attack.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You may use this ability when an enemy willingly moves out of your melee weapon range to make a Heavy attack against them. You can use this even if they took the disengage action or a similar action that would not make them provoke attacks of opportunity. If the target uses a teleport type move, then you cannot use this ability, unless they Teleport into your weapon range.

This is treated as an opportunity attack.

ability2Ref.name: Single-Minded Fury
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You choose a single enemy as the target of your Single-Minded Fury. Your movement speed is doubled when moving towards your target plus you gain immunity to Slowed and difficult terrain. You also gain immunity to any mind-affecting effect that would prevent you from chasing or attacking your target. You gain a 1d4 bonus to accuracy rolls against that target, but suffer a 2d4 penalty to accuracy rolls against any other target. This effect lasts until the end of the encounter or until the target falls to 0 HP or lower.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You choose a single enemy as the target of your Single-Minded Fury. Your movement speed is doubled when moving towards your target plus you gain immunity to Slowed and difficult terrain. You also gain immunity to any mind-affecting effect that would prevent you from chasing or attacking your target. You gain a 1d4 bonus to accuracy rolls against that target, but suffer a 2d4 penalty to accuracy rolls against any other target. This effect lasts until the end of the encounter or until the target falls to 0 HP or lower.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: In Rage Stance, ability is not already active.
ability2Ref.requirementText: In Rage Stance, ability is not already active.

ability3Ref.name: Smash through the pain
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You gain Smash through the pain, which reduces the damage taken by the next attack by an amount equal to 3 + Power
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You gain Smash through the pain, which reduces the damage taken by the next attack by an amount equal to 3 + Power

name: Great Knight
descriptionHtml:
First fielded by Northi not long after Hussars were becoming common on the battlefield, the Great Knight represented the quintessential heavy cavalry. With knight and mount both clad in heavy armor, the Great Knight was made to be a mobile barrier. Able to interpose their armored bulk for their allies and even project that protection into an aura around them, Great Knights were able to push through even the most heavily contested battlefields for their allies. And while there were countless stories throughout the ages of heavily armored knights turning the tides of battle, there were none as debated as the story of Sir Owain Morgan and Dame Madeline Benoit.

As told and accepted by Northi historians, Sir Owain Morgan was a dogfolk Great Knight without peer. Honorable beyond reproach and an unstoppable juggernaut on the battlefield, he had a list of successful battles attributed to him that stretched the imagination. In fact, detractors of Sir Owain’s tale would often point out that he must have surely known teleportation and time travel magics to be at so many battles at once. What could not be disputed was that Sir Owain certainly existed, his name recorded in the annals of the Bastion Knights. But his most debated feat was one that involved a Westrian knight, Dame Madeline Benoit, and the proliferation of Mage Knights into Northi’s military.

While there were many versions of the tale, the most accepted one in Northi followed a series of reasonably set in stone facts. During one of the many border skirmishes common between Northi and Westria as they vied for control over the Nuren Plains, it was said that Sir Owain led a counter attack against a Westrian skirmishing force. He led his men to victory and successfully captured many knights to ransom back to Westria, including Dame Madeline Benoit. While most of the knights and men at arms were released back to their homes, Dame Madeline was kept for one very important reason: to teach Northi the art of the Mage Knight. Historians for the Era of Knights could often be incited into argument around the idea as there were as many accounts for how Northi gained access to the skills of Westria’s holy knights as there were historians to argue about them.

But what was almost universally accepted as the truth was that Sir Owain, disgusted with his peers and superiors' lack of any decency to release Dame Madeline back to her family, took it upon himself to free the woman. There was little historical accounting of where they went or what they did but there were legends of a pair of knights wandering Lyr and helping those in need. And while those actions had never definitively been attributed to Sir Owain and Dame Madeline, what couldn’t be denied was when they both turned back up in solid historical record. 

They would found Morgan Ranch in what would later become Dacquoise, near the future site of the city of Madeline. It was there that the retired knights, happily married for an unclear amount of time, had begun taking on students and ranch hands to teach the art of horsemanship, knighthood, and magic. While the ranch itself did not last into modern times, the house itself was maintained and the legacy of chivalry was upheld by the descendants of those students. Collectively, and informally, they called themselves the Knights of Morgan and dedicated themselves to helping those in need whenever and wherever they were found.

guideHtml:
The great knight is a mounted tank class that excels in fights with many different engagements.

Intercept is your main tool in getting around the battlefield, allowing you to ride to an allies aid. If you move to an ally far away enough, you can even use Trample and do some damage with it. Note that your movement is not a sidestep, you do provoke attacks of opportunity with it. 

Rescue & Drop lets you save an ally from a bad situation and ride them out to safety. You can also drop them immediately to another square nearby to break them free from being surrounded or to let them move away without provoking.

Your Secret Art: Moving Castle is an aura that reduces damage by 3 in a small aura around you. This can be massive when fighting against a group of non-heroic enemies, reducing the damage your allies take by a large amount. You can also move for free once per turn to reposition and potentially help allies in two completely different positions at the same time.

Shared Defense is a simple tool that helps you protect your mount from AoE attacks.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Animal Husbandry, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Heavy Cavalry
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Medium Armor and Heavy Armor proficiency.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: Every enemy damaged by your Trample effects become Challenged by you until the start of your next turn.

ability1Ref.name: Rescue & Drop
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You pick up a willing ally and put them on your mount. They travel with you on the back of your mount until either you or they spend 0 AP or 1 RP to place them back on the ground. An ally that is being rescued can not take any other actions until they are dropped.

Only 1 ally can be rescued at a time.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You pick up a willing ally and put them on your mount. They travel with you on the back of your mount until either you or they spend 0 AP or 1 RP to place them back on the ground. An ally that is being rescued can not take any other actions until they are dropped.

Only 1 ally can be rescued at a time.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must be mounted.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must be mounted.

ability2Ref.name: Intercept
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You move towards an ally, Sidestepping them 5ft in a direction of their choice and occupying their square. The triggering attack is made against you and you automatically take the Block action against it.

Mounted move rules apply to your movement for this action.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You move towards an ally, Sidestepping them 5ft in a direction of their choice and occupying their square. The triggering attack is made against you and you automatically take the Block action against it.

Mounted move rules apply to your movement for this action.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Must be mounted and an ally is the target of an attack.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Must be mounted and an ally is the target of an attack.

ability3Ref.name: Shared Defense
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: Your mount (the last mount you were riding on this arc) may use your defenses instead of its own while within range. In addition, you may spend RP for it while dismounted if your mount is in range.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: Your mount (the last mount you were riding on this arc) may use your defenses instead of its own while within range. In addition, you may spend RP for it while dismounted if your mount is in range.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Must be dismounted.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Must be dismounted.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Moving Castle
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You project a defensive aura up to 15ft around you, granting all allies (excluding yourself) the Bulwark buff. While under the effects of Bulwark, allies take 3 less damage.

While projecting this aura, you may spend 0 RP to sidestep up to your movement. After you do so, you may not do so again until the start of your next turn.

Upkeep: You must spend 1 mana at the start of your turn to continue this effect.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You project a defensive aura up to 15ft around you, granting all allies (excluding yourself) the Bulwark buff. While under the effects of Bulwark, allies take 3 less damage.

While projecting this aura, you may spend 0 RP to sidestep up to your movement. After you do so, you may not do so again until the start of your next turn.

Upkeep: You must spend 1 mana at the start of your turn to continue this effect.

name: Guardian
guideHtml:
The guardian is a defender that wants to reduce damage through high toughness and heavy armor. They get access to this heavy armor, though the drawbacks of it are noticeable too. Too much guard may be overkill and it may be better to stick to medium or light armor in some cases.

They control what the enemy can do by using Provoke and Threatening Aura. Provoke is a 1 turn Challenge for no AP cost, while threatening aura does cost 2 AP, but you automatically challenge everything around you. Challenge makes enemies provoke attacks of opportunity when they hit anybody except you, so you do need to keep in melee range of the enemies. Challenging Aura is especially good when you have other melee fighters that are squishy next to you. A berserker will greatly appreciate being hard to target.

To help with being a threat with your attacks of opportunity, the controlled opportunities key ability gives you a small bonus to them. Thanks to it, guardians are more free to ignore focus and power and focus on defensive stats.

Hold the line lets a guardian convert AP to RP at a good rate, at the cost of some mana. In a long fight, frequent use of this will let you keep your RP until you really need it.

Last Stand is an ability that should only be used when you know you're going to face many attacks during the upcoming round. The RP cost is heavy, but it can easily be RP efficient. You don't need to use mana on this for it to be worth it, but it's worth considering if you are likely to face a bunch of heavy attacks.

Challenging Aura is especially good when you have other melee fighters that are squishy next to you. A berserker will greatly appreciate being hard to target.

To help with being a threat with your attacks of opportunity, the controlled opportunities key ability gives you a small bonus to them. Thanks to it, guardians are more free to ignore focus and power and focus on defensive stats.

Hold the line lets a guardian convert AP to RP at a good rate, at the cost of some mana. In a long fight, frequent use of this will let you keep your RP until you really need it.

Last Stand is an ability that should only be used when you know you're going to face many attacks during the upcoming round. The RP cost is heavy, but it can easily be RP efficient. You don't need to use mana on this for it to be worth it, but it's worth considering if you are likely to face a bunch of heavy attacks.

keyAbilityRef.name: Controlled Opportunities
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain Heavy Armor proficiency.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: When making an attack of opportunity, you gain a +2 bonus to the accuracy and damage roll.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Last Stand
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: Until the start of your next turn, you may use the Block action for 0 RP and you gain additional Guard equal to the mana spent on this ability. Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again in this encounter.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: Until the start of your next turn, you may use the Block action for 0 RP and you gain additional Guard equal to the mana spent on this ability. Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again in this encounter.

name: Gun Paladin
descriptionHtml:
John was a paladin who loved guns. Like his fellow paladins, he had been trained in the ways of sword and shield.

One day, he and his friends were forced to gather at a border village where a fiend had reportedly appeared. The reports were true. In front of them stood a towering mass of shadows and tentacles that had already murdered the entire population. But of course, they could not simply retreat or it may attack other villages.

His commander gave the order and dozens of paladins charged forward to subdue the creature. But not John. He looked towards the men and women who were running to their death, then towards the pistols he carried at his side. They were expected to fight in melee, but why would Kari's light discriminate against a weapon?

He sheathed his sword and shield and drew his pistol. He ignored his commander's shouting and aimed the weapon at the monster. Brilliant holy light fired from the barrel and as it struck the monster, his allies' attacks became empowered. Without hesitation, he threw his spare pistol to other paladins nearby. Together, their shots annihilated the creature and he became known as the first gun paladin.

guideHtml:
The gun paladin is a support striker class that lets your allies attack for you.

Judgement Shot is a light attack that allows an ally to spend their extra RP on making a heavy attack. Even them making a light attack can be better if your offensive stats are worse.

Twin Verdict lets you convert your extra RP into extra offensive power. It combo's decently well with snap shot in giving you access to another heavy in a turn.

Divine Barrage lets you and several allies all light up a single target. While it costs a bit of mana, it can do a lot of damage to a squishy target.

Sanctity Aura is a situational aura effect. It may let your allies do extra damage or bypass resistances when you let them make attacks.

keyAbilityRef.name: Holy Weapon

keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may choose to have your weapon emit light as if it was a lantern. You can enable or disable this effect for 1 AP.

ability1Ref.name: Judgement Shot
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target.

Afterwards, an ally other than you within 30ft of you may make a Light attack against the target. They may spend 1 RP to make this a Heavy or Precise attack instead.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:

ability2Ref.name: Twin Verdict
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make two Heavy attacks against the target.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make two Heavy attacks against the target.

ability3Ref.name: Sanctity Aura
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You project an aura 30ft around you that blesses your allies.

When an ally makes an attack as part of one of your Gun Paladin abilities, they may choose to make the damage type of that attack the same as your Holy Weapon damage type.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:

You make a Light attack against the target and instruct others to follow your lead.

X allies other than you within 30ft of you may also make a Light attack against the same target.

name: Gunfu
descriptionHtml: A skilled gunner and martial artist that combines guns and martial arts into a deadly mix.
descriptionText: A skilled gunner and martial artist that combines guns and martial arts into a deadly mix.
requirementsHtml: Cultivator or Martial Artist mastered. Gunslinger mastered.
requirementsText: Cultivator or Martial Artist mastered. Gunslinger mastered.
guideHtml:
Those who practice Gunfu are able to fight well from both range as well as in melee due to not suffering any penalties when firing in melee combat. They are able to weaken the enemy from range, then go in for the kill once they’ve come closer.

Slide & Shoot is their gap closer that can be used to slip past the frontline to target the backline. It also starts their combo and should always be followed up with an unarmed attack for maximum value.

Pistol Whip is counted as an unarmed attack and is thus a natural follow up that now costs 0 AP. The debuff it applies will help reduce the amount of attacks you face.

After that, you can finish it up with a Point Blank Shot. It is not particularly effective against heavy armor, but it punishes dodgy characters extremely hard.

Their Secret Art Whirling Death is an excellent clean up tool for grunt type enemies. Ideally it should be used when multiple enemies are already weakened. This will allow for multiple activations of its Killing Blow mechanic when you finish off the weak enemies and can keep shooting.

keyAbilityRef.name: Gun Martial Arts
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You can fire your pistols while in melee range of an enemy without provoking attacks of opportunity.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You can make unarmed attacks even if carrying a pistol in both hands. If wielding a weapon other than a pistol, this no longer applies. When wielding only pistols and no other weapons, your unarmed attacks benefit from your pistol material bonuses.

ability1Ref.name: Slide & Shoot
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You slide forward up to 20ft towards your target. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity and you may pass through enemy space while moving. At the same time, you may make a Light Attack with your pistol against the target at any point during this movement.

Your next ability gains Combo: If this attack is an unarmed attack, its AP cost is reduced by 1.

Your next ability gains Combo: If this attack is an unarmed attack, its AP cost is reduced by 1.

ability1Ref.requirementText: Pistol only.

ability2Ref.name: Point Blank Shot
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy Attack against the target.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy Attack against the target.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Pistol only.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Pistol only.

ability3Ref.name: Pistol Whip
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light Attack with a pistol in your hand. On damage, the target becomes Unbalanced.

This attack benefits from alloys and material bonuses on your pistol (but not from any on your unarmed strike).
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Light Attack with a pistol in your hand. On damage, the target becomes Unbalanced.

This attack benefits from alloys and material bonuses on your pistol (but not from any on your unarmed strike).
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Must be holding a pistol.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Must be holding a pistol.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Whirling Death
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You enter the Whirling Death stance, spinning wildly and shooting at enemies within range. While in this Stance you may spend 2 AP to make a basic Light Attack with a pistol against all enemies within 20ft.

When you kill an enemy or make them enter the downed state from with this attack, you may repeat the attack.

The Stance lasts until you enter a different Stance or until you make an attack that is not tied to this ability.

name: Gunslinger
guideHtml:
The gunslinger takes control of the fight from the start with their Quickdraw ability. It's only a light attack, but it's a free attack that can't be reacted to, so it's free early damage. Because it's an encounter start ability, it is one of the few things that lets you act before enemy with ‘First Shot’ initiative bonus can take their action.

Ricochet is a simple attack that can be used to bypass light cover. If an enemy is using light cover, there's little reason not to use it.

Warning Shot costs a fair bit of mana, but gaining Trick Attack can help you secure hits. Grunts are unable to react to them, so you can snipe one hiding behind cover with a warning shot + ricochet combo.

For their Secret Art: Fan the hammer you'll probably want to bring a spare pistol. It is a relatively close range ability and the high AP cost makes it hard to move and shoot if you don't get extra AP from somewhere, but it can do a large amount of damage. A single reaction will affect all 4 shots, so its something only to be used against a target that has exhausted their RP.

If an enemy reacts to this ability with a Dodge or Block type reaction, that reaction is valid for all 4 attacks.

name: Harrier
descriptionHtml:
Eirlys Saoirsen crouched low, fingers brushing against a crushed leaf. A single discrepancy in a literal forest of leaves. A single mistake by her prey. She stood and continued following the trail, ghosting through the brush and vines without a trace or hindrance. It was an eternal job to patrol the border of the Great Forest, the boundary of Easter. Normally, the magic of the Fae prevented intruders from making it more than half a mile into the Forest before they became so confused that they wandered back out. But sometimes, poachers would hire an unscrupulous Fae (there were more of those than she liked) to guide them through the country wide bounded field.

Eirlys had been tracking this group for three days, her anger growing at each sign of a hunted glitter stag or crudely harvested glow moss. But she was close, finally, and she could put an end to this. She froze as a branch snapped in the near distance ahead of her and she could just make out brash voices and the sounds of a camp being prepared. She paused, pulling out her crossbow and silently readying it with a bolt. Then she stalked forward, silent and leaving no trail to tack her by.

The poachers were gathering fallen branches for a fire, two catfolk chimera and a human. Their guide, a stuffy looking high fae, was leaning against a tree, radiating barely disguised disgust. Eirlys stalked closer slowly as the camp came together until she was only twenty feet away from the guide. Once in position, she didn’t hesitate and the snap of her crossbow firing put everyone on alert. Well, so did the cry of agony from the guide as her bolt punched through his left calf, pinning it to the tree. She thrust out her hand, vines flying from her outstretched arm and wrapping the surprised Fae to the tree, pinning his arms. Then she sprinted from cover, to slap a piece of sticky bark over his eyes to prevent him from teleporting free of his bindings. 

The poachers were scrambling for their weapons, swords and axes. They had already unstrung their bows for the night so she had the advantage in range. And her crossbow had already reset itself, waiting for a bolt, the partially living weapon her steady companion for longer than most civilizations had lasted. Her second shot of the night pinned one of the catfolk to the ground by his boot, her third knocking another head over heels as he tried to close the distance through the rough terrain of the brush.

She faded in and out of visibility, moving impossibly quickly through the surrounding forest, bolts picking away at them from safety. The sun was just setting by the time the last poacher fell still and she was finally able to relax. Well…Eirlys looked at the struggling, screaming Fae guide and walked over to him. She stuck another sticky bark over his mouth, careful to keep his nose free for breathing. With his screaming muffled, she would really be able to relax. She could turn him in to the Council in Sylvan later.

guideHtml:
The harrier is a ranged controller class that prefers using a crossbow.

Pinning Shot will be your most powerful control option, allowing you to potentially root a target which is a powerful debuff. The target can spend 2 AP to break free, but this is still an AP win for you and you and your allies will benefit from its reduced dodge until it does.

To setup for your pinning shot, you have Knockback Shot allowing you to knock an enemy close to or into a wall.

To help with reliability, you have Rapid Correction which not only allows you to turn a miss against the base evasion into a potential hit, it can also allow you to react after a target dodges the attack to turn it into an attack that goes past their dodge.

Flushing Shot is naturally used against enemies in cover to bait them out of it. Even if they choose not to move, you just get a Sure Hit attack against them which is just good.

Finally, Discern Immunities is a simple ability that will prevent you from using abilities that won't work against an enemy. It does share the Quick keyword with Status Assessment, so be aware you can't use both in the same turn without spending an extra PA.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus.

keyAbilityRef.name: Discern Immunities
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Discern Immunities.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Discern Immunities
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You look at a single target and glean what its resistances are until the end of the encounter. When you are about to use an ability with a negative effect that would be passively negated by the target, you become aware of this and may cancel your action.

Does not apply if the target has an active ability that can remove it, such as a paladin’s Paragon of Virtue for example.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You look at a single target and glean what its resistances are until the end of the encounter. When you are about to use an ability with a negative effect that would be passively negated by the target, you become aware of this and may cancel your action.

Does not apply if the target has an active ability that can remove it, such as a paladin’s Paragon of Virtue for example.

ability1Ref.name: Knockback Shot
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target is pushed back 20ft.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target is pushed back 20ft.

ability2Ref.name: Pinning Shot
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Light attack against the target with your weapon. On damage, if there is a wall or similar object directly behind the target, they become pinned against it. A pinned target is considered Rooted until they spend 2 AP to remove the arrow pinning them.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Light attack against the target with your weapon. On damage, if there is a wall or similar object directly behind the target, they become pinned against it. A pinned target is considered Rooted until they spend 2 AP to remove the arrow pinning them.

ability2Ref.requirementText: Bow or Crossbow only.

ability3Ref.name: Rapid Correction
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You turn your Light attack into a Precise attack.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You turn your Light attack into a Precise attack.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: You miss with a ranged weapon Light attack.
ability3Ref.requirementText: You miss with a ranged weapon Light attack.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Flushing Shot
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy attack against the target. The target may choose to sidestep out of cover from your attack to automatically avoid the attack. Otherwise, the attack gains Sure Hit.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Heavy attack against the target. The target may choose to sidestep out of cover from your attack to automatically avoid the attack. Otherwise, the attack gains Sure Hit.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Target must be in Low or High cover.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Target must be in Low or High cover.

name: High Priest
descriptionHtml: Powerful divine agents who bring peace and comfort to their allies.
descriptionText: Powerful divine agents who bring peace and comfort to their allies.
guideHtml:
The high priest has strong out of combat healing thanks to their Tranquil Prayers. If possible, you will want to delay healing near the end of a fight and save your mana to use it after combat ends.

Cure Touch II is a massive heal with an equally big mana cost. It's very powerful when an ally is low on hit points, but it may leave you out of options in the future.

Purification Circle is an AoE debuff removal. It is relatively cheap AP and Mana cost wise if you're hitting more than 1 person and it can remove different debuffs from different people.

Radiant Halo is another spell with a high mana cost. It heals all allies, but the range may be limiting if the party is split up in a fight. You can also cast it by using RP, which can be a lifesaver when you need to top of your allies after a powerful AoE attack.

Finally, Walking Church is a secret art that effectively makes a target immune to damage for one turn. There will always be a use for this ability in a fight, so save a mana and RP for it until you need it.

heart: You gain +1 to Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to either Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Tranquil Prayers
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: Your Acolyte, Priest and High Priest Healing abilities heal for 2x value when used out of combat.

ability1Ref.name: Cure Touch II
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You Heal the target for 22 + (4 x Power)
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You Heal the target for 22 + (4 x Power)

ability2Ref.name: Purification Circle
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You remove all stacks of single debuff from all allies in a 20ft radius circle from target point.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You remove all stacks of single debuff from all allies in a 20ft radius circle from target point.

ability3Ref.name: Radiant Halo
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You Heal all allied targets in a 15ft radius from the target point for 11 + (2 x Power).

You may use 1 RP instead of 2 AP for this ability. When using RP on this ability, you may use it even when it is not your turn.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You Heal all allied targets in a 15ft radius from the target point for 11 + (2 x Power).

You may use 1 RP instead of 2 AP for this ability. When using RP on this ability, you may use it even when it is not your turn.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Walking Church
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: Blesses the target, granting them the status of a church. Until the end of your next turn, the target gains Damage Reduction 1000 against all damage types.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: Blesses the target, granting them the status of a church. Until the end of your next turn, the target gains Damage Reduction 1000 against all damage types.

name: Highlander
requirementsHtml: Adventurer, Fighter, Farmer, Blacksmith or Miner mastered.
requirementsText: Adventurer, Fighter, Farmer, Blacksmith or Miner mastered.
guideHtml:
The highlander is a reliable combatant that uses heavy melee weapons. Their aftershocks ability ensures that even if they miss their heavy attacks, they still deal some damage to the target and enemies around them.

Steady Stance is cheap to activate and protects against most knockbacks and prone knocking abilities, assuming you've leveled Power a good amount. It can be used as a reaction, so you don't need to activate it until you are hit by one of those types of abilities.

Cleave is good at larger groups of enemies, its interaction with Aftershock almost guarantees you'll do some good chip damage to the enemies around the target.

Meteor Cleave helps you get in range vs enemies that slightly outrange you, such as enemies with polearms. It also combos nicely into overpower, letting you do a double heavy even when not in range at the start of your turn.

Overpower can demolish enemies with much lower power than you, but it may not do much if their power is similar to yours. Evaluate how much power your opponent has before using it.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness.
soul: You gain +1 to Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Aftershocks
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Aftershock.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: When using a Fighter, Highlander or Reaver ability that compares Power against a Boss type enemy, you may treat your Power as 2 higher for that purpose. (Does not affect the dmg roll etc)

associatedAbilityRef.name: Aftershock
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You attack with such force that you create aftershocks when you miss your attack, dealing Half Pierce Physical Damage equal to your Power to the target and all enemies within 5ft of it. 

If the target Dodged the triggering attack, this damage is reduced to half your Power instead.

If your attack targets multiple enemies, this only applies if you miss all enemies.

You attack with such force that you create aftershocks when you miss your attack, dealing Half Pierce Physical Damage equal to your Power to the target and all enemies within 5ft of it.

If the target Dodged the triggering attack, this damage is reduced to half your Power instead.

associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: You missed a Heavy melee attack with a Two-Handed Melee Weapon.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: You missed a Heavy melee attack with a Two-Handed Melee Weapon.

ability1Ref.name: Steady Stance
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You enter a Stance in which you become Immovable unless the enemy has more Power than you. If they do, you may pay an additional mana to retain the effect against them for that turn if they would prone you or knock you back.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You enter a Stance in which you become Immovable unless the enemy has more Power than you. If they do, you may pay an additional mana to retain the effect against them for that turn if they would prone you or knock you back.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance.

ability2Ref.name: Meteor Cleave
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You sidestep up to 10ft towards the target and make a melee Heavy attack against them. 

On damage, if your power is equal or higher than the target's, they are knocked Prone.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You sidestep up to 10ft towards the target and make a melee Heavy attack against them.

On damage, if your power is equal or higher than the target's, they are knocked Prone.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Two-Handed melee weapon only.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Two-Handed melee weapon only.

ability3Ref.name: Cleave
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Heavy attack against the target. On damage, you deal Half Pierce Physical damage equal to your Power to all other enemies within 5ft of the target.

If you miss the attack and use Aftershock in reaction to this missing, Aftershock mana cost is reduced by 1 for that use.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Overpower
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. For every point of Power you have over the target, you deal 2 additional damage.

Combo: If used after Meteor Cleave, the AP cost is reduced by 1.

name: Hoarfrost
descriptionHtml:
The Hoarfrosts are Cryomancers who’ve mastered the chilling power of ice, tracing their origins back to a legendary mage named Mirane. She was the first to truly perfect the art of Cryomancy during her journey through the eastern Tundra. Her story took a dramatic turn when she faced off against a powerful Fiend. In that intense battle, Mirane developed her ultimate spell, the Eternal Mirane Cage. This incredible spell froze the Fiend in place for ten long years, giving an army of adventurers the time they needed to arrive and finally take it down.

Mirane’s magic didn’t just fade into history—it’s been passed down through the generations to future Hoarfrosts. Those who follow in her footsteps wear a snowflake emblem, just like Mirane did, and many of them embark on their own journeys to the Tundra that now bears her name.
descriptionText:
The Hoarfrosts are Cryomancers who’ve mastered the chilling power of ice, tracing their origins back to a legendary mage named Mirane. She was the first to truly perfect the art of Cryomancy during her journey through the eastern Tundra. Her story took a dramatic turn when she faced off against a powerful Fiend. In that intense battle, Mirane developed her ultimate spell, the Eternal Mirane Cage. This incredible spell froze the Fiend in place for ten long years, giving an army of adventurers the time they needed to arrive and finally take it down.

Mirane’s magic didn’t just fade into history—it’s been passed down through the generations to future Hoarfrosts. Those who follow in her footsteps wear a snowflake emblem, just like Mirane did, and many of them embark on their own journeys to the Tundra that now bears her name.
guideHtml:
Eternal Mirane Cage is a legendary ability, but it’s not something you’ll use all the time. It’s perfect for when you need to subdue enemies who just won’t give up, making Hoarfrosts a great choice for missions where taking down targets alive is the goal.

Frost Driver is an excellent follow-up move that turns a slow into a full-on root, setting up your team for devastating AoE attacks and utility plays. Just be aware of its limitation against flying enemies—it won’t be as effective there.

White Thunder is a clever setup move that transforms your Glacial Walls into potential bombs. It forces enemies to think twice before getting too close, giving you control over the battlefield.

When things get dicey, Glacial Bastion is your panic button, but it’s not something to use without thought. While it shields you, it can also give enemies the chance to position themselves around you, potentially trapping you. If you see them making a move for a big strike, use White Thunder while encased, since you can’t defend yourself while you’re in the ice.

Lastly, the Hoarfrost’s Secret Art, Whiteout, is a powerful tool that reduces accuracy for everyone on the battlefield and can be activated right at the start of an encounter. It’s especially useful if you’re ambushed or under heavy pressure, buying your team some defensive turns to regroup and reposition. Just keep in mind that enemies with Frost Immunity or Ice Cold are unaffected by it, just like you.

heart: You gain +1 to Reason.

keyAbilityRef.name: Eternal Mirane Cage
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Eternal Mirane Cage.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Eternal Mirane Cage
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: Instead of killing the target, they are reduced to negative their Max HP +1. The target is encased in Magical Ice for 10 years, rooted on the location and cannot be moved.  The Ice never melts despite temperature and can only be dispelled by Higher Order Magic (such as a Divine Arms), or by you. The Spell effect ends if you die. You can extend the duration of the Frost by 10 years for every 2 mana spent.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: Instead of killing the target, they are reduced to negative their Max HP +1. The target is encased in Magical Ice for 10 years, rooted on the location and cannot be moved.  The Ice never melts despite temperature and can only be dispelled by Higher Order Magic (such as a Divine Arms), or by you. The Spell effect ends if you die. You can extend the duration of the Frost by 10 years for every 2 mana spent.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Use when an ally attack would kill a target and target is not an ally.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: Use when an ally attack would kill a target and target is not an ally.

ability1Ref.name: Frost Driver
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You send ice spikes along the ground towards a single target, making a Heavy attack that deals Frost Damage. This cannot hit a target higher than 5ft above the ground. On hit, the target is Slowed until the end of their next turn.  If the target is already Slowed, they instead become Rooted, making them unable to move until the end of their next turn.

Combo: When used after Icicle Nail or White Thunder hits, reduce the mana cost to 0.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You send ice spikes along the ground towards a single target, making a Heavy attack that deals Frost Damage. This cannot hit a target higher than 5ft above the ground. On hit, the target is Slowed until the end of their next turn.  If the target is already Slowed, they instead become Rooted, making them unable to move until the end of their next turn.

Combo: When used after Icicle Nail or White Thunder hits, reduce the mana cost to 0.

ability2Ref.name: White Thunder
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You target a Glacial Wall in range and fire a bolt of Frost energy that requires line of sight. On impact, the wall is destroyed and explodes into blades of ice, attacking all enemies within 5ft of the destroyed wall with a Precise attack that deals Frost damage. On damage, the target is Slowed until the end of their next turn.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You target a Glacial Wall in range and fire a bolt of Frost energy that requires line of sight. On impact, the wall is destroyed and explodes into blades of ice, attacking all enemies within 5ft of the destroyed wall with a Precise attack that deals Frost damage. On damage, the target is Slowed until the end of their next turn.

ability3Ref.name: Glacial Bastion
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You encase yourself in a Glacial Wall that fills your space until the start of your next turn, making you untargetable for attacks during the duration. This Glacial Wall has HP equal to 20 + Focus, any remaining damage is transferred to you (mitigated by your armor).  While encased, you can cast White Thunder on your encased Ice for 1 RP which removes Glacial Bastion. Other than White Thunder, you cannot take any other actions while under the effect of Glacial Bastion.   

This Spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You encase yourself in a Glacial Wall that fills your space until the start of your next turn, making you untargetable for attacks during the duration. This Glacial Wall has HP equal to 20 + Focus, any remaining damage is transferred to you (mitigated by your armor).  While encased, you can cast White Thunder on your encased Ice for 1 RP which removes Glacial Bastion. Other than White Thunder, you cannot take any other actions while under the effect of Glacial Bastion.

This Spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Whiteout
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You create a sudden snowstorm that obscures sight, creating difficult battle conditions. 

Attack accuracy for all affected units is reduced by your Focus and sight range is reduced to 60ft. A creature can only target something within their sight range.

You and creatures with Frost Immunity are not affected by this ability.

You may activate this ability as an Encounter Start action during the Encounter Start phase of an encounter. If you do, you do not pay the RP or initial mana cost.

Upkeep: You must pay 2 mana at the start of your turn or the effect ends at the end of that turn.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText:

Attack accuracy for all affected units is reduced by your Focus and sight range is reduced to 60ft. A creature can only target something within their sight range.

name: Hussar
descriptionHtml:
The romantic image of cavalry often brought to mind knights in gleaming plate armor, resplendent in heraldry, astride a powerful steed. Lances, spears, and longswords held aloft, ready to ride nobly into the fray. Ready to rescue princesses, slay dragons, and dispatch dishonorable knaves. While many knights struggle to live up to that image, the origin of those lofty traits could be found in the Hussars.

Named after the light cavalry unit raised from the nobles and knights of Macaron in Dacquoise, the Macaron Hussars set a standard for both chivalry and battlefield prowess that has remained largely unchallenged even into the modern day. While the individual knights may have their betters in swordsmanship or as riders, the combined unit has been considered the premier elite light cavalry force since its creation across all of Lyr. Sworn directly to the King of Dacquoise, the honor within the ranks of the Macaron Hussars is such that there has never been a recorded or rumored instance of betrayal of their oaths, even during the rare instances of attempted usurpation of the Line of the King. They have never failed to follow the rightful King or Heir of Dacquoise.

Their metal was tested in recent memory at the Battle of Nuren Crossing where the combined forces of the Nuren Guard and a small adventuring party held a critical bridge crossing against Northi invasion during the Four Swords War. The Northi commanders, intent to utilize Dacquoise’s roads to move rapidly into a flanking position against Mothergreen, attempted to take the northernmost bridge crossing of the Nuren river. When they were unable to capture the bridge with their scouts, the Northi force was pinned against the river by Lamian elements of the Nuren Guard. Before they were able to withdraw and regroup, the hammer of the Macaron Hussars smashed them against the Lamian anvil, having used a risky ford miles downstream. With their support elements gutted, supplies captured, and further Dacquoise reinforcements incoming, the invading force surrendered and were permitted to withdraw back to Northi.

Hussars around the world have strived to live up to the gallantry and reputation of their namesakes, always chasing that seemingly distant goal. And while more renegade knights have taken up the skills of mobility and devastatingly powerful charges that the Hussars are known for, many still believe that the embodiment of the knight as demonstrated by the Macaron Hussars was a goal worth striving for.

guideHtml:
The hussar is a mounted class that overwhelms the enemy with devastating charges.

Cavalry charge is a movement ability that boosts your trample. It has no additional cost over normal movement, so you should always use this if you're planning to trample enemies over moving normally. Any non-heroic enemy with your trample also becomes Shaken, which basically makes them useless on their next turn.

Momentum is an ability that you can use after you've moved in range of the enemy with your charging attack, as it can gain a large damage boost. Another option is to strike at a non-heroic enemy that used their RP to dodge your trample. They'll now have no RP left and your momentum boosted attack is likely to one shot them, especially if using a lance.

Break Away is the ability you use to move away from the enemy to prepare for another trampling charge attack. The mana cost is relatively high, so ideally you don't want to be in a position where you have to use it.

Secret Art: Override is a powerful secret art that relies heavily on enemies being in a line to get maximum value out of. Be careful when using this against rogues, their evasive maneuvers ability can force you to end your override early by creating an empty space.

keyAbilityRef.name: Terrifying Charge
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When dealing damage with the trample effect or with your Secret Art: Override, any non-heroic target you damage becomes Shaken until the end of their next turn.

ability1Ref.name: Cavalry Charge
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may spend up to 4 AP on this ability and move as though you had spent that AP on movement. You automatically gain the Trample effect as like the Trample ability once you have moved 20ft.

For every 10ft moved, the attack you make as part of the Trample effect gains +1 bonus damage, up to a maximum of +10.

You may spend up to 4 AP on this ability and move as though you had spent that AP on movement. You automatically gain the Trample effect as like the Trample ability once you have moved 20ft.

ability2Ref.name: Break Away
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You disengage and may move up to half your speed away from the nearest enemy.

ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Must be mounted.

ability3Ref.name: Momentum

ability3Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy attack against the target. For every 10ft moved during your last action this turn, this attack gains +1 damage up to a maximum of +10 damage. Abilities with the Teleport keyword do not apply for this.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Override
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy attack against all enemies in a line and move through them. You keep moving through enemies until you reach an empty spot you can stand in. If there is no valid end position due to an ally occupying the spot or terrain (such as a wall) being in the way, you cannot use this ability.

If there is no valid end position due to an ally occupying the spot or terrain (such as a wall) being in the way, you cannot use this ability.

name: Hydromancer
descriptionHtml: Hydromancers are specialist water mages who can fit into almost any party.  They're ‘fluid’ if you will.  (Placeholder)
descriptionText: Hydromancers are specialist water mages who can fit into almost any party.  They're ‘fluid’ if you will.  (Placeholder)
requirementsText: Any class mastered or Water mastered.
guideHtml:
Hydromancers are versatile specialist mages who can do a little bit of everything.  They are reliant on their ability key ability Blue Tide to create domains of water from which they can cast spells from.  This poses a challenge, as you must set up preparations in advance in order be effective.  In exchange for this limitation, Hydromancers are incredibly versatile and can deal damage, support, control and even heal.  Their ultimate ability, Refreshing Stream, is a rare ability that allows you to heal RP, a critical defensive resource.  In addition, Hydromancers can use RP to cast many of their abilities, giving them the ability to turn the tides mid turn.

Setting up Blue Tide early is critical to take early space.  You’ll want to move with your team and continue to use Blue Tide to create more zones that you can fight and cast from.  If you find yourself in a battle near or on top of water, you’re in luck as you won’t need to cast Blue Tide as often.  Aqua Drill can be used as a normal heavy attack, but also has the additional purpose of spreading water into areas that do not have any.  You can use Aqua Drill as a combo with other abilities to increase your casting range of water spells.

Crashing Foam Wave is a versatile ability that can be used to push enemies away, as well as set up allies for more powerful attacks, or to pull enemies away from danger.  Since forced movement doesn’t trigger attacks of opportunity, you can bounce allies away with it.

Cure Water is a basic healing spell but its quite strong.  Its mana cost is quite high, so use caution with casting it often.

Agility builds favor Hydromancers, as having high RP for your abilities is recommended.

heart: You gain +1 to Reason, Presence or Awareness
soul: You gain +1 to Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Mage of the Tides
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You can use Blue Tide.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You gain Elemental Mastery: Water
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You gain the ability to walk on water and skate across its surface—every 10ft of water surface movement only costs 5ft of movement.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Blue Tide
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You create a 15ft radius zone of 1ft of water in a circle around yourself. This zone of water remains stationary at its original position, even after you move.

This zone lasts until the end of the encounter and automatically replenishes itself when Aqua Drill is used inside of it. You may have multiple of these zones active at once.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You create a 15ft radius zone of 1ft of water in a circle around yourself. This zone of water remains stationary at its original position, even after you move.

This zone lasts until the end of the encounter and automatically replenishes itself when Aqua Drill is used inside of it. You may have multiple of these zones active at once.

ability1Ref.name: Aqua Drill
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You infuse water with your mana firing a spiraling blast of high pressure water, making a heavy attack that deals Water damage against a target within range. You can use either the RP or AP cost for the ability.  The water is used as a projectile and spills anywhere within 5ft of the target at your choice.

If you use an RP for this ability, the mana cost increases by 1.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You infuse water with your mana firing a spiraling blast of high pressure water, making a heavy attack that deals Water damage against a target within range. You can use either the RP or AP cost for the ability.  The water is used as a projectile and spills anywhere within 5ft of the target at your choice.

If you use an RP for this ability, the mana cost increases by 1.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: You are touching an amount of water equal to a normal sized bucket of water.
ability1Ref.requirementText: You are touching an amount of water equal to a normal sized bucket of water.

ability2Ref.name: Crashing Foam Wave
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: Creates a wave of water originating from a point of water within range.  Shoves an adjacent target of size large or smaller 10ft away from the point of origin.  If the target is on top of the water, you can choose to shove the target in any direction.  You can use either AP or RP for the cost of this ability.

If the target is on top of the water, you can choose to shove the target in any direction.  You can use either AP or RP for the cost of this ability.

ability3Ref.name: Cure Water
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You cast this ability on water within range, healing a target within 30ft for 11 + Power x 2. You can use either AP or RP for the cost of this ability.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You cast this ability on water within range, healing a target within 30ft for 11 + Power x 2. You can use either AP or RP for the cost of this ability.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Refreshing Stream
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You cast this ability on water within range, recovering fatigue and granting a target within 15ft of that point 1 RP.  The target cannot go over their max RP from this.

name: Iai Style
requirementsText: Any class mastered. Proficient with Katana.
guideHtml:
Masters of the Iaido style typically start the battle in one of two ways: They open with an Iai strike or wait for the enemy to strike, then counter with Uke Nagashi. Iaido centers around Iaijutsu and the sheathed state. Their most important abilities are locked behind this sheathed state and unless you fulfill conditions, its a once per battle thing.

Iai Strike is a powerful attack that not only has lock-on, it's also only 1 AP. Unless you are using a weapon other than a katana and mixing weapon fighting styles, this will most likely be your first action in combat. Because using it loses the sheathed state, it can not be used again unless you use Kiri-tsuke.

Kiri-tsuke is a heavy attack with the benefit of resetting your sheathed state when you score a kill with it. Depending on the battle, this may be an easy or hard thing to do, but you should always look for the opportunity for the reset.

Chiburi Noto is used at the end of the encounter and buffs you for your next one by maximizing your Iai strike damage. The benefit of this can be good, but if you are low on HP or mana it may be wise to use one of the basic options instead.

Uke Nagashi is a powerful counter move that has the condition that you must still be in the sheathed state. Because making any attack with a katana removes the sheathed state, this means you can not attack before using Uke Nagashi, unless you use a different type of weapon. This is a valid option. You can choose to attack with a normal sword, then drop it and prepare for your counter. If you'd rather only use a katana and still want to use this, then you can instead play very aggressive with your positioning. Use all your AP to move up, then when the enemy wants to attack you, unleash the counter. You can also choose to delay and hope the enemy moves up and attacks you.

keyAbilityRef.name: Iaijutsu
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: If you become the target of an attack while you're surprised, you may still react to it and any special properties of an attack that require you to be unaware of the attack (such as Killshot) do not apply to it.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may sheathe and draw katanas for 0 AP once per turn per katana.
keyAbilityRef.benefit3: You start each battle in the 'Iai' state, which is used for Iai abilities. Making an attack with a katana removes the Iai state for that weapon. Using a skill that requires the Iai state requires the weapon to be sheathed and will remove the Iai state for ALL your weapons, unless specified otherwise.

ability1Ref.name: Iai Strike
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You draw your weapon and attack with lightning speed, making a Heavy Attack. You lose the Sheathed state afterwards.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You draw your weapon and attack with lightning speed, making a Heavy Attack. You lose the Sheathed state afterwards.

ability2Ref.name: Kiri-tsuke
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On Killing Blow, you regain the Iai state.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On Killing Blow, you regain the Iai state.

ability3Ref.name: Chiburi Noto
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You clean the blood off your katana and sheath the blade. If your next Iai strike with this weapon during this arc is a hit, its Roll Damage is maximized.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You clean the blood off your katana and sheath the blade. If your next Iai strike with this weapon during this arc is a hit, its Roll Damage is maximized.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Uke Nagashi
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: Roll Heavy Damage and treat that as your Block against the attack that’s targeting you. If you reduce the damage to 0, the attacker becomes Unbalanced. On hit effects and maces do not gain their effect if you reduce the damage to 0. After the attack is resolved, you may immediately make an Iai Strike against the attacker for 0 RP. This Iai Strike gains the Sure Hit keyword against an Unbalanced target.

name: Idol
descriptionHtml:
Kyouko took a deep breath to settle her nerves and had to stop her hands from smoothing her skirt down again. Her boots crunched softly against the frosted moss, the heels sinking in a bit before she adjusted her stride.

Dont worry, Kyouko-chan, we wont let any harm come to you, Nalin said, his voice a deep rumble as he held out her wand. It looked like a tiny toy in his huge, bullfolk hands and she gave him a bright smile, her twin tails curling. He patted her gently on the head and then hefted his heavy shield and mace.

I know. You all have protected me for so long, now I can help you! she said earnestly.

Henry flashed her a peace sign with a grin and Marco looked away to fiddle with his staff. Marco always got embarrassed so easily but Kyouko knew he loved listening to her practicing her songs every night. Seren pushed her way through the brush ahead of them, her face brightening as she jogged over to the group, her cat ears perked up happily and her tail twitching back and forth. She leaned in for a quick hug before getting down business.

Its definitely an enemy group, she said. Five members with a full mix of front liners, mid range, and supporters. Theyll be tricky to beat but they dont have Kyouko.

True! Henry agreed. Cmon, lets teach these guys that they cant just do what they want here.

The point of intercept Seren had scouted was just ahead, a nice clearing that would give them plenty of area to move in. Kyouko took her position behind her friends and waited. It wasnt long until an eclectic group stumbled into the clearing with none of the matching uniforms her friends had. The newcomers noticed them instantly and they immediately pulled out weapons.

Why do we always run into cultists? one of them complained. And why do they always have hostages?!

Hostages? Kyouko looked around. Her group hadnt taken any hostages. And she resented being called a cultist! 

Still, just like she had practiced, she began the first steps of her performance, her mana spreading across the clearing in sparkles and phantom music.

In the shadow of the world I play~

guideHtml:
The idol class is one of the easiest classes in the game. 

You have 3 buffing songs, one that boosts accuracy, one that boosts damage and one that boosts evasion. Simply use the song that your party needs you to use based on the enemy you're fighting. In most cases, you'll likely up using This is our moment!

Stay here with me is an AoE healing song that can keep your party up over a long fight with decent efficiency if you're healing multiple people.

Mashup is the one way to get more songs active as an Idol without going into other classes. Due to the way songs work, you can only have 1 of them active normally but Mashup lets you ignore this and get your songs up faster for a small mana cost.

name: Jewel Idol
descriptionHtml:
Melody tumbled across the cracked, scorched ground and rolled to her feet moments before she would have crashed into the remains of a burning tree. The shockwave had knocked them all over and she coughed, waving her hand through the smoke to try to get some clear air or at least see if her friends were alright. Fire roared to her right, illuminating the area in a fierce glow briefly before the fireball detonated, showering her with dirt and scorched wood even as far away as she was.

This was impossible! They had been so close! Theyd had the perfect plan! Silverd weapons, a tengu fiend slayer, and all the information the Dragons Claw Alliance had on this monster. Shed known it wouldnt be easy, no fiend slaying mission was ever easy, but they had been prepared for this!

An arcane blast cut through the smoke and impacted the fiend, the silvery tinge to the magic causing the monster to shriek. A heavily armored shadow burst through the smoke, his huge hand missing a gauntlet as he helped her to her feet.

You alright, Melody? Bishop asked, his voice rough from the smoke. Hed come from the direction of the fireball earlier and judging by how his shield was still shimmering with heat, hed taken it head on. The idiot.

She coughed once and then pulled the water bottle attached to her outfits belt to take a quick swig. That cleared up her throat nicely.

Im fine, Im fine! Tired of this smoke, though!

Good! And well put a stop to that soon enough. Mimi says it's on its last legs. Can you still give support?

Melody secured her water bottle and brushed at her uniform, straightening the pleats of her skirt and getting as much ash and dirt off as she could manage. Ill keep going until my voice breaks, she said with a grin, giving him her signature pose. She could tell he rolled his eyes behind his helmet.

Well, whatever you have to do, he said fondly, clapping her on the shoulder. Well make sure Aislyn gets you the good stuff when we get back.

Her eyes sparkled at the thought. Aislyn hoarded the best chocolates and that was just what the doctor ordered for dealing with this fiend. Bishop nodded and then rushed toward the sound of fighting and screeching.

Melody took a deep breath, the mana building in her throat protecting her from the smoke and ash.

Three, two, one--Full Combo~!

guideHtml:
The Jewel Idol is a supportive idol that focuses on letting your allies hit and rewarding them from doing so.

Your key ability gives you increased range to your songs (the listed ranges are without the increase) and lets you double the effect One Beat, One Heart for a small mana cost.

Most of the class sort of revolves around your Secret Art Full Combo. It gives a massive +4/+8 damage bonus to all allies, as long as they keep hitting every attack. Therefore, you want to make sure to use your allies that they do so.

To help with this, you have Never Miss The Beat. 2 Mana is quite expensive, but it does give a sizeable boost to accuracy provided your ally did not dump focus. Once you know the evasion of your enemy, you should be able to judge whether this would actually help or not.

To prevent enemies dodging and ruining the combo, you have Offbeat Recovery. It can be used once for each ally's turn and while it does nerf the damage of the attack, it lets you keep the combo and against dodgy enemies even do good chip damage to them after they block.

Until My Voice Breaks lets you spend your (maximum) HP to help offset some of the high mana costs you'll be having. Naturally if you're being focused, you don't want to be spending your HP but if you are left untouched it lets you contribute even after your mana runs dry.
