They may also see from your position rather than their own and they may calculate their ranged weapon range (but not LoS) from your position instead of their own. This new range may not exceed twice their normal range.

ability2Ref.name: Pathfinder
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may move up to your speed and you ignore difficult terrain penalties for this movement. Until the start of your next turn, all terrain within 20ft of you is not considered difficult terrain for your allies.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may move up to your speed and you ignore difficult terrain penalties for this movement. Until the start of your next turn, all terrain within 20ft of you is not considered difficult terrain for your allies.

ability3Ref.name: Hide in bush
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You take the Hide action and are considered to be in cover for the purposes of remaining stealthed. This lasts until you take any action or speak.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You take the Hide action and are considered to be in cover for the purposes of remaining stealthed. This lasts until you take any action or speak.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Must be in natural terrain.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Must be in natural terrain.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Ambush Shot
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy attack that does maximum damage instead of rolling. This attack does not break your stealth.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Heavy attack that does maximum damage instead of rolling. This attack does not break your stealth.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Enemy must be unaware of your presence. Can only be used on the first turn of an encounter and must be your first attack.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementText: Enemy must be unaware of your presence. Can only be used on the first turn of an encounter and must be your first attack.

name: Searing Lotus Style
descriptionHtml:
Aine watched as injured soldiers ran past her through a narrow pass. She hadnt expected things to be easy when she set out with her comrades from Glassworks, answering the surprising call for help from the Demons, but the sheer weight of numbers the combined Northi-Westrian forces brought to bear was mind boggling. They had lost the battle, that was clear and obvious, but her units objective had been complete. The relic was safely smuggled out of the now burning city while they had held the rear guard.

She didnt know what the relic was, she didnt know why it was so important, she didnt know why half her comrades were dead and the rest injured, but she had to hope that it was something that would turn the tide of the war.

Keep going! she shouted after the few remaining Salamanders and Demons that had hesitated when she hadnt followed them. She turned fully toward the approaching humans and brought her longsword down with a sharp motion. Fire bloomed along the blade, so hot that it began to sear the grass nearby and caused the humans to come to a wary halt at the sheer heat of it. The tip of her blade scratched a molten line in the stone between her and the enemy.

If you cross that, I'll be forced to kill you, she warned, her voice holding an echo of a wildfire. 

Their response was just laughter and a cautious approach, swordsmen fanning out and a pair of riflemen hurrying to load their weapons. She let out a scorching breath and let Fire become a reality in the world around her. The air shimmered and the stone slagged into molten puddles around her. Scorching wind flew around her, the sudden heat displacing air aggressively and sending her hair dancing. She took a defensive stance and waited for the first man to gain his nerve. Their courage was bolstered by more men coming up the pass, ready to push the chase for her withdrawing men.

The first man to cross into her domain barely had time to scream before he was scorched to ash and bone. Those that tried to bypass her found that she had placed herself too neatly in the pass opening and they had to dance back with severe burns. Rifles range out and she was forced to deflect away one shot with her sword while the others were wildly off target, the heat haze throwing off their aim.

She couldnt hold them forever and she knew she would die here. But she would burn as much time and as many men as she could, the flame of hope in her heart that the artifact would all be worth it, that it would bring an end to the war.

Come, then! Ill burn you all to ash!

guideHtml:
The Searing Lotus Style is a striker and tank hybrid class.

Trial by Fire is a simple attack that applies Challenged to the target, but it punishes you if you miss. If you have other ways of applying challenge to the target and need to land it, you might not want to take the risk. 

Scorching Reprisal is a heavy attack of opportunity that just gives you another free one after. Naturally it's only worth using if you think an enemy will provoke another AoO.

Molten Domain is a tanking domain that forces enemies around you to stand and fight you or catch on fire. Against multiple enemies, this can rack up a lot of burning quickly. 

Infernal Bastion is a block that can force the attacker to waste an AP or do some damage to them. If you time it at their last attack of a turn, they'll likely be unable to remove the burn.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Reason or Presence.

keyAbilityRef.name: Fire Weapon
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Fire Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare your chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

ability1Ref.name: Trial By Fire
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, they are challenged by you until the start of your next turn.

 If this does not deal damage, they cannot be challenged by you until the start of your next turn.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, they are challenged by you until the start of your next turn.

 If this does not deal damage, they cannot be challenged by you until the start of your next turn.

ability2Ref.name: Scorching Reprisal
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy attack of opportunity against the target. Your next basic attack of opportunity against that target before the start of your next turn costs 0 RP.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy attack of opportunity against the target. Your next basic attack of opportunity against that target before the start of your next turn costs 0 RP.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: An enemy provokes an attack of opportunity.
ability2Ref.requirementText: An enemy provokes an attack of opportunity.

ability3Ref.name: Molten Domain
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
Until the start of your next turn, the ground 5ft around you becomes like burning ground.

Any enemy willingly leaving or moving through this area other than entering it becomes Burning X, where X is your Focus x 3.

The domain burns away other friendly damage aura effects while in effect. Effects that would normally trigger upon ending the turn in it (such as Poison Spore) don't trigger if someone ends their turn in the Molten Domain.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Infernal Bastion
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You surround yourself with flames and take the Block action against the attack. If you reduce damage of this attack to 0, inflict Burning X on the target where X is your focus x 3.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You surround yourself with flames and take the Block action against the attack. If you reduce damage of this attack to 0, inflict Burning X on the target where X is your focus x 3.
ultimateAbilityRef.requirementHtml: You are targeted by a melee attack.

name: Shadow Thief
guideHtml:
The shadow thief is a utility class that works best during the night. Every single ability they have has higher than normal mana costs during the day, but lower than average during the night. Ideally, you only want to fight during the night though of course this isn't always an option.

Shadowstep at night is better than normal movement. Not only do you move further or the AP cost, with it being a teleport you ignore attacks of opportunity, difficult terrain and other hassles. During the day, it's a worse Fae Flash. Still usable if you need to teleport, but not worth the mana cost in a lot of cases.

Vanish during the night is a 1 RP hide action. This has some use cases, allowing you to make more attack while taking advantage of the stealth bonus. The use case by itself doesn't change much during the day, as the RP cost is a bigger limiting factor than the mana cost in most cases. 

Shadow Cloak not only gives you a stealth bonus, it also lets you stealth even when you don't have cover or concealment. This makes the Vanish ability a lot more useful, allowing you to stealth in the middle of nowhere, although it requires a high stealth skill bonus. It's a one time cost that lasts until the end of the encounter, so it's still usable even during the day. It's also just good for sneaking into places, especially when combined with Shade Form.

Shade Form is a primarily out of combat exploration tool, though it has some use in combat too by being able to move through enemy spaces. It is almost unusable during the day due to its upkeep cost though.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Stealth, Deception, Roguecraft, Perception, Insight, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Cunning.
soul: You gain +1 to Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Nightstalker
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You gain 60ft darkvision, allowing you to see perfectly in the dark even without a source of light. If you already have darkvision, the range of it improves by 60ft.

ability1Ref.name: Shadow Step
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You may Teleport up to twice your movement speed. During the night, the mana cost of this is reduced by 2.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You may Teleport up to twice your movement speed. During the night, the mana cost of this is reduced by 2.

ability2Ref.name: Shade Form
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You become like a shade becoming near weightless and gaining the ability to pass through spaces occupied by other creatures. You can also slip through small cracks in walls or doors, as long as they are at least 1inch wide and tall. Upkeep: You must pay 2 mana at the start of each turn or this ability ends. During the night, the mana cost and Upkeep of this is reduced by 2.

You can also slip through small cracks in walls or doors, as long as they are at least 1inch wide and tall. Upkeep: You must pay 2 mana at the start of each turn or this ability ends. During the night, the mana cost and Upkeep of this is reduced by 2.

ability3Ref.name: Vanish
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You gain the Hiding state from anyone you have cover or concealment from.

During the night, the mana cost of this is reduced by 1.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You gain the Hiding state from anyone you have cover or concealment from.

During the night, the mana cost of this is reduced by 1.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Shadow Cloak
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You cloak yourself in Shadows, giving you a +5 bonus on Stealth checks for 1 hour or until end of encounter. It also allows you to hide even when not behind cover or concealment. During the night, the mana cost of this is reduced by 2.

name: Shadowbringer
skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Stealth, Deception, Roguecraft, Perception, Insight, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Cunning.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Agility.

keyAbilityRef.name: Shared Darkness
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: The range of your darkvision increases by 60ft.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use Darkvision.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Darkvision
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You touch an ally and share your Darkvision with them until the end of the encounter, letting them see up 60ft in the darkness.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You touch an ally and share your Darkvision with them until the end of the encounter, letting them see up 60ft in the darkness.

ability1Ref.name: Hide in Shadows
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You jump into a target’s shadow. If you were hidden before using this ability, you remain hidden inside the shadow. Else, the target or any other enemy may attack the shadow as if they were attacking you. If you take damage, you are forced out. While you’re inside the shadow, you travel with the target when it moves, including if it teleports, but cannot take actions other than exiting it.

You may exit the shadow willingly for 0 RP. When you exit it, if you were hidden, your next basic attack that turn against the target whose shadow you were hiding in gains Full Pierce.

ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Must be day.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Must be day.

ability2Ref.name: Darkness
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You conjure a 15x15ft area of pure darkness within range. The darkness blocks line of sight. Targets inside of or within 5ft of the Darkness can only see up to 5ft into or out of Darkness. In addition, any targets within the Darkness that do not have Darkvision or a similar ability that lets them see in darkness are considered Blinded 2 (nightvision does not apply).

This lasts for up to 10 minutes and you may end this effect for 0 AP.

The mana cost of this ability is reduced by 2 during the night.

ability3Ref.name: Sable Grace
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You take the Dodge action against the triggering attack. You remove Lock-On from this attack and treat your Dodge as 99 against it.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You take the Dodge action against the triggering attack. You remove Lock-On from this attack and treat your Dodge as 99 against it.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: You become the target of an attack. Can only be used during the night.

name: Shield Paladin
requirementsHtml: Human, proficient with shields and medium or heavy armor.
requirementsText: Human, proficient with shields and medium or heavy armor.
guideHtml:
The shield paladin is a tank that focuses on blocking and temporary HP. 

Through their Aura of Protection, they allow their allies to block against attacks even if their Block is normally too low to be usable. This does cost mana and it requires your ally to block, so communication with your allies is key.

With Holy Shield, you can convert AP into defense, gaining both a high Block potentially allowing you to block even heavy attacks and a decent check of temporary HP. You do give up damage by using too much AP on this, so it is not always the best option to dump all your AP into it.

Divine Smite is a more offensive way to gain temporary hit points. It does require landing the heavy attack, but it can be used both on yourself or an ally.

Shield paladins can also heal themselves or ally through their Lay on Hands ability. It's efficient both mana and AP wise, with the only drawback being that it only has a touch range. It does not provoke attacks of opportunity however, so you don't need to worry about disengaging from the fight before healing.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Holy Weapon
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Holy Damage instead of its normal damage type.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may choose to have your weapon emit light as if it was a lantern. You can enable or disable this effect for 1 AP.

ability1Ref.name: Holy Shield
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
Your gain Holy Shield, which gives your next Block action gains a +2 guard bonus (not doubled again). If you reduce the damage you take with your next Block action to 0, you gain 3 temporary HP.

Overcharge: You may spend up to 3 additional AP on this ability. Each extra AP spent increases the Guard bonus and temporary HP gained by 3.

Your gain Holy Shield, which gives your next Block action gains a +2 guard bonus (not doubled again). If you reduce the damage you take with your next Block action to 0, you gain 3 temporary HP.

Overcharge: You may spend up to 3 additional AP on this ability. Each extra AP spent increases the Guard bonus and temporary HP gained by 3.

ability2Ref.name: Aura of Protection
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You create a 30ft radius Aura of Protection around yourself. While this aura is active, when an ally within 30ft of you takes the Block action you may spend 1 Mana to replace their Block Value with your own. In addition, any abilities with the Aid keyword have their range increased by 20ft while inside the Aura.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You create a 30ft radius Aura of Protection around yourself. While this aura is active, when an ally within 30ft of you takes the Block action you may spend 1 Mana to replace their Block Value with your own. In addition, any abilities with the Aid keyword have their range increased by 20ft while inside the Aura.

ability3Ref.name: Divine Smite II
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack that deals Holy Damage against the target. On damage, you may give yourself or an ally within 30ft temporary HP equal your 5+Power until the end of the encounter.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack that deals Holy Damage against the target. On damage, you may give yourself or an ally within 30ft temporary HP equal your 5+Power until the end of the encounter.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Lay on Hands
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You Heal the target for 4 + Power. Overcharge: You may spend an additional mana to remove a negative effect from the target. Injuries and Mortal Wounds cannot be removed with this.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You Heal the target for 4 + Power. Overcharge: You may spend an additional mana to remove a negative effect from the target. Injuries and Mortal Wounds cannot be removed with this.

name: Shield Saint
descriptionHtml:
The sworn shieldbrother or sister of a Sword Saint. Like its twin saint, the Shield Saint originally hails from Kirara where it fights together with their chosen partner.

The Shield Saint is trained in defensive techniques whose purpose is to allow for their partner to focus purely on offense while the Shield Saint absorbs the hits. At first these techniques were merely physical, but over time the nature has evolved into a more spiritual connection. The bond between a Shield Saint and their partner links their very souls together and neither can be bonded again until one of them passes away. Because of this, the partner a Shield Saint chooses is very important and in Kirara most pairs are selected based on compatibility at an early age. It is not uncommon for a Sword Saint and Shield Saint pair to be romantically involved with each other.
descriptionText:
The sworn shieldbrother or sister of a Sword Saint. Like its twin saint, the Shield Saint originally hails from Kirara where it fights together with their chosen partner.

The Shield Saint is trained in defensive techniques whose purpose is to allow for their partner to focus purely on offense while the Shield Saint absorbs the hits. At first these techniques were merely physical, but over time the nature has evolved into a more spiritual connection. The bond between a Shield Saint and their partner links their very souls together and neither can be bonded again until one of them passes away. Because of this, the partner a Shield Saint chooses is very important and in Kirara most pairs are selected based on compatibility at an early age. It is not uncommon for a Sword Saint and Shield Saint pair to be romantically involved with each other.
guideHtml:
As a Shield Saint, your primary goal is to protect your soulmate from harm.

Your main tool for doing so is Faithful Shield, which lets you turn AP into free RP for the purpose of keeping your soulmate safe. Shelter still has a range restriction, so this works best if your soulmate is also a melee character.

Quick Assist combines several feature of Bodyguard into one and adds some offense on top of it. Keep in mind the range is rather short and like with Faithful Shield, your life is easier with a melee soulmate as you are more likely to get the mana cost reduction.

The secret art Dynamic Duo combines defense with offense. You Challenge all enemies and can use the bodyguard's At your side to teleport across the battlefield to threaten enemies you didn't before. Where normally your attacks might be a bit weak, because your soulmate is making them it will punish enemies ignoring your Challenge that much harder. It also has a low mana and AP cost, so you can use it pretty much every fight.

Finally, Lifesense is a utility tool that can be used to keep track of the status of your soulmate should you split up. It has no mana cost, so you can keep it up indefinitely if you want to.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception, Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Agility or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Soul Bond
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When you acquire this ability, choose 1 willing person. That person is treated as your Soulmate and several of the class abilities only work on your Soulmate. This cannot be changed unless your Soulmate dies.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: Your Soulmate is always treated as your VIP for any bodyguard abilities. You may still designate another VIP as normal.

ability1Ref.name: Lifesense
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You know the direction your Soulmate is in and if you’re within 300 feet of them, you know their exact location.

You also know their current HP, Mana and RP.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You know the direction your Soulmate is in and if you’re within 300 feet of them, you know their exact location.

You also know their current HP, Mana and RP.

ability2Ref.name: Faithful Shield
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may use Shelter once at 0 RP cost if the target is your Soulmate. This effect lasts until the start of your next turn.

Overcharge: You may spend 4 AP on this ability to instead gain 2 free uses of Shelter.

These free uses ignore the normal limitation of using an ability only once per turn.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You may use Shelter once at 0 RP cost if the target is your Soulmate. This effect lasts until the start of your next turn.

Overcharge: You may spend 4 AP on this ability to instead gain 2 free uses of Shelter.

These free uses ignore the normal limitation of using an ability only once per turn.

ability3Ref.name: Quick Assist
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You teleport next to your soulmate and force the triggering attack to resolve against you, gaining the benefit of the Block action against it.

Afterwards, if your Soulmate is in range of the attacker, they may make an Attack of Opportunity against it.

If you were already next to your Soulmate before you used this ability, the mana cost is reduced by 1.

ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Your soulmate is the target of an attack and is within the range of this ability.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Your soulmate is the target of an attack and is within the range of this ability.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Dynamic Duo
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You challenge all enemies in range until the start of your next turn. Any time you would be allowed to make an Attack of Opportunity, your Soulmate may make it instead. They may Sidestep up to their speed before making each Attack of Opportunity.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You challenge all enemies in range until the start of your next turn. Any time you would be allowed to make an Attack of Opportunity, your Soulmate may make it instead. They may Sidestep up to their speed before making each Attack of Opportunity.

name: Shieldwarden
descriptionHtml: Shielding mages
descriptionText: Shielding mages
requirementsText: Acolyte, Mage or Mage Knight mastered.
guideHtml:
Shieldwardens are a healing class that walk a tight line between being too cautious and being too confident. Those too cautious will burn through their mana quickly, while those who wait too long before shielding may find their allies already on the floor where their shields will do little to get them back up. They lack traditional combat healing, but make up for it with superior numbers on their shields.

Power Word: Shield is their standard protection spell. You’ll want to cast it on your tank or the target most likely to get hit in the early rounds of combat.

Power Word: Barrier is also an option for an early cast, but the mana cost may feel high if the temporary HP doesn’t get used. Since it can be cast while using RP, it may be better to wait until the party is targeted by AoE attacks before using it.

Power Word: Reinforce is a manaless way to give your allies more shields, with the condition that you probably already placed shields on them beforehand. It also works with temporary HP not applied by you, so there’s some possible powerful synergies there.

Secret Art: Lifesaver is something you’ll only use as a last resort. Unless someone is low on hit points, it will not provide great value for its mana cost. But if you combine it with the above mentioned reinforce, an ally can fight at near death for a long period of time.

Recover Power makes it less likely you waste your mana by shielding yourself near the end of the combat, but it only works on yourself.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Magic, Religion, Medicine, Perception, Negotiation or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Power or Toughness.

keyAbilityRef.name: Recover Power
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: Any temporary HP on you at the end of an encounter is converted into healing. You regain HP equal to half the value of temporary HP you have remaining.

ability1Ref.name: Power Word: Shield
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: The target gains temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to 11 + (2 x Power).
ability1Ref.descriptionText: The target gains temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to 11 + (2 x Power).

ability2Ref.name: Power Word: Barrier
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
Up to 5 target allies within range (including yourself) gain temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to 10 + Power.

You may pay either the AP or RP cost for this ability, you don't need to pay both.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
Up to 5 target allies within range (including yourself) gain temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to 10 + Power.

You may pay either the AP or RP cost for this ability, you don't need to pay both.

ability3Ref.name: Power Word: Reinforce
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
The target gains temporary HP equal to 2 x their current temporary HP. 

After the attack is done resolving, the target's temporary HP is set to the value it was before this ability was used unless the target's current temporary HP is lower than that.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
The target gains temporary HP equal to 2 x their current temporary HP.

After the attack is done resolving, the target's temporary HP is set to the value it was before this ability was used unless the target's current temporary HP is lower than that.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: An ally is the target of an attack.
ability3Ref.requirementText: An ally is the target of an attack.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Lifesaver
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: The target gains temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to their missing HP.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: The target gains temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to their missing HP.

name: Shinigami Eyes
requirementsHtml: Any class mastered, must have the Touched by Death breakthrough. Must have at least 1 mystic eye slot open.
requirementsText: Any class mastered, must have the Touched by Death breakthrough. Must have at least 1 mystic eye slot open.
guideHtml:
The Shinigami Eyes are a mystic eye ability. When you activate your death perception, you immediately see the HP % of all enemies within sight. It may cost a mana, but it gives information a lot faster than relying on Status Assessment skills.

With the True Death passive ability, you immediately become a counter to any sort of healer. There's nothing to heal when their maximum HP is reduced by the same amount as their HP.

Line of Death is a light attack that punishes armored targets that think they can ignore light attacks. It won't do much extra against targets without a high guard.

Point of Death does consistent damage against enemies below half HP. Even against targets without high guard, it may still be worth using sometimes for its maximum damage property when you need to secure a kill and can't risk rolling low on damage.

Secret Art: Seventeen Dissection is a secret art that does not cost any mana, making it usable in every fight. Against heroic enemies, it is just a guaranteed 17 damage. It can be useful if they are still sitting on RP but are low on HP. If they are out of RP, then just attacking normally may do more damage. Against any type of grunt, it is just an instant kill no matter. Useful against grunts that have a lot of hit points.

heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning or Awareness.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Death Perception
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Death Perception.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: One of your eyes becomes a Shinigami Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Death Perception
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You can assess the approximate HP percentage of all living units within range until the end of combat. (The DM should enable health bars for the unit). Only affects unit in your line of sight when you use this ability, if more enemies join the fight later they are not affected until you use this ability again.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You can assess the approximate HP percentage of all living units within range until the end of combat. (The DM should enable health bars for the unit). Only affects unit in your line of sight when you use this ability, if more enemies join the fight later they are not affected until you use this ability again.

ability2Ref.name: Line of Death
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Light Attack against the target. Overcharge: You may spend 1 additional mana and AP to make another Light Attack against the same target.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Light Attack against the target. Overcharge: You may spend 1 additional mana and AP to make another Light Attack against the same target.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Slashing weapons only.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Slashing weapons only.

ability3Ref.name: Point of Death
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. This attack deals maximum damage instead of rolling for the damage.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. This attack deals maximum damage instead of rolling for the damage.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Target is below half HP of their original max HP (Before True Death reductions).
ability3Ref.requirementText: Target is below half HP of their original max HP (Before True Death reductions).

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Seventeen Dissection
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You deal 17 true damage to the target. If this damage lowers the target’s HP to 0 or lower, they immediately die.
If the target of this attack is a non-heroic unit, they instantly die regardless of their remaining HP.

name: Sky Knight
descriptionHtml:
As was common with most innovations that made radical leaps, the history of the Sky Knights was steeped in warfare. Like the development of the Cavalier, learning to take to the sky on the backs of winged mounts stemmed from a need to counter a threat. Aerial Mages from Sorthen had long since, and in some cases continue, dominated the skies as scouts, couriers, and as untouchable bombardment specialists. With no modern airships to deter them and no Aeromancers to threaten them, both Northi and Westria searched for a solution. Nearly simultaneously, the two countries developed their own methods.

From the mountains and hills near Mount Merlin, Northi tamed the wild pegasi. Harpy hunters were the first to capture and tame the wild flying horses and harpy run ranches continue to produce the highest quality mounts with specialized breeds tailored for various tasks. A pegasus’s agility and speed in the air let the chimeran knights of Northi put up a challenge to Sorthen aerial units in terms of scouting, but their limited endurance and fragile wings made them less suited as couriers and frontline combatants. 

Westria took a nuisance that plagued their southeastern border along the mountains near Jav and turned it into a solution. Wyverns roosting in the mountains and forests were regularly dealt with by adventurers and knights but some enterprising (and foolhardy) beast tamers decided to attempt to tame newly hatched wyverns as companions. With some false starts, they succeeded and it wasn’t long before an interest in training them as mounts emerged. Slower and less nimble in the air than their pegasi counterparts, a wyvern’s thick scales and devastating Reginleiv made them much more suitable as flying heavy cavalry.

To this day, there is little overlap in the Sky Knight forces of Northi and Westria. While each side failed to deploy units that could directly counter Aerial Mages, their arrival on the battlefield removed Sorthen’s complete dominance of the air. Modern Sky Knight adventurers utilize whatever type of mount that suits their style (or that they can afford) and continue to fill their historical roles as scouts, couriers, and aerial cavalry.

From the mountains and hills near Mount Merlin, Northi tamed the wild pegasi. Harpy hunters were the first to capture and tame the wild flying horses and harpy run ranches continue to produce the highest quality mounts with specialized breeds tailored for various tasks. A pegasus’s agility and speed in the air let the chimeran knights of Northi put up a challenge to Sorthen aerial units in terms of scouting, but their limited endurance and fragile wings made them less suited as couriers and frontline combatants.

Westria took a nuisance that plagued their southeastern border along the mountains near Jav and turned it into a solution. With some false starts, they succeeded and it wasn’t long before an interest in training them as mounts emerged. Slower and less nimble in the air than their pegasi counterparts, a wyvern’s thick scales and devastating Reginleiv made them much more suitable as flying heavy cavalry.

To this day, there is little overlap in the Sky Knight forces of Northi and Westria. While each side failed to deploy units that could directly counter Aerial Mages, their arrival on the battlefield removed Sorthen’s complete dominance of the air. Modern Sky Knight adventurers utilize whatever type of mount that suits their style (or that they can afford) and continue to fill their historical roles as scouts, couriers, and aerial cavalry.
guideHtml:
The sky knight is a class that focuses on flying mounts. It is vulnerable to ranged attacks, but it has excellent target access. Despite being a tier 2 class, it may not be accessible for everyone due to the high cost of pegasi and wyverns. A pegasus is faster, but wyverns are a lot more durable and with some offensive power.

Air Stability helps manage the durability issues of the pegasus, allowing you to re-direct attacks like normal. The flight rules of you losing altitude still apply though. Aerial Loop can also help with this. It can be used if either you or your mount become the target and it affects both of you, useful for avoiding AoE attacks.

Aerial Crashdown is your main tool of attacking the backline, moving you to the target and allowing your mount to make a heavy attack for just 2 AP. Be careful of getting stuck on the ground against melee enemies however, as you can't canter of your mounts attack.

If you don't want to commit to an engagement, you can use Flyby attack. This, like Aerial Crashdown, also works with Trample, though getting trample value is harder with a flying mount.

Your Secret Art: Mythical Awakening varies based on the type of flying mount you use. The pegasus one can be used both offensively to just deal damage, but also defensively if you're scared of being forced to the ground by arrows. You can just bellorophon to attack and move low enough that you won't get shot down. The wyvern's reginliev is best used on the same elevation as the enemy, as it will be hard to hit many targets with the line attack otherwise.

skills: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Animal Husbandry, Riding, Flight, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Power

keyAbilityRef.name: Air Stability
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: The cavalier’s Rider & Mount As One ability applies even while flying. Any damage you take while flying on a flying mount will cause you to lose altitude as if your mount took damage.

ability1Ref.name: Aerial Crashdown
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You and your mount crash down on the target. You move towards the target and your mount makes a Heavy attack against it. On damage, the target is knocked prone.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: You and your mount crash down on the target. You move towards the target and your mount makes a Heavy attack against it. On damage, the target is knocked prone.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: You are mounted on a flying mount and the target is at least 20ft below you.
ability1Ref.requirementText: You are mounted on a flying mount and the target is at least 20ft below you.

ability2Ref.name: Aerial Loop
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You and your mount take the Dodge action and gain a +5 bonus to evasion against the attack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You and your mount take the Dodge action and gain a +5 bonus to evasion against the attack.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: You are mounted on a flying mount and you or your mount are the target of a ranged attack
ability2Ref.requirementText: You are mounted on a flying mount and you or your mount are the target of a ranged attack

ability3Ref.name: Flyby Attack
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: You sidestep up to your speed. At any point during this movement, you may spend 1 AP to make a Light attack with a weapon or spend 2 AP to make a Heavy attack with a weapon. After this attack, you may change the direction in which you spend the rest of your movement.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: You sidestep up to your speed. At any point during this movement, you may spend 1 AP to make a Light attack with a weapon or spend 2 AP to make a Heavy attack with a weapon. After this attack, you may change the direction in which you spend the rest of your movement.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: Must be mounted on a flying mount.
ability3Ref.requirementText: Must be mounted on a flying mount.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Mythical Awakening
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You channel your power into your mount, allowing it to unleash a devastating attack based on what type of mount it is.

Pegasus: Bellerophon: You Sidestep towards a target within 60ft and make a Heavy attack against it. This attack has Lock-on and costs 1 RP and 2 Mana

Wyvern: Reginliev: You make a Heavy attack against all enemies in a 5ft wide line up to a point within 60ft. You also make a Heavy attack against all enemies within 10ft of the selected point, though enemies can also take an attack from this ability once. This attack costs 2 AP and 2 Mana.

Dragonfly: Darting Mirage: You move at incredible speed and create several illusionary afterimages in your wake. Until the start of your next turn, every attack against you or your mount that is not a critical hit or does not have the Sure Hit keyword  automatically misses. This is an Illusion effect. This ability costs 0 AP and 4 Mana.

All these attacks are made with your mount’s stats instead of your own.

name: Sky Talon Style
descriptionHtml:
The various clans of Harpies and Tengus live in harmony together on Mt Merlin. This was not always the case.

A long time ago, the two races were in a violent territorial dispute over the mountain. The tengu held control over it when the harpies moved in. The tengu were worried about the threat of these sudden newcomers, while the harpies considered Mt Merlin part of Northis territory and thus their land by right.

The tengu had both the numbers and defenders advantage and quickly moved to forcefully expel the invaders after the brief set of negotiations broke down. However, they had lacked knowledge of the Harpys innate mastery of the wind. Their downbursts would snipe tengus out of the sky without any recourse and the first battle ended in a crushing defeat for the crows.

Seizing the momentum, the harpies pushed forward and suppressed the tengus, who were unable to use their usual flying combat strategies. Hope seemed lost. Until, Dawn Waits Above stepped out of her solitary training from the highest peaks of the mountains.

Dawn gifted onto her fellow tengus a martial arts style that let them harness the power of the wind themselves. They managed to fly faster and land extremely quickly to avoid the dangerous blasts of wind coming from the sky. Once they closed the gap, their wind-infused fists knocked the harpies out of the sky themselves where they were beaten into submission.

With the new techniques, the tides of the battle shifted into a stalemate. With neither side having an advantage and enough blood already spilled, the two races agreed to stop fighting. The tengus settled on the higher peaks, always being able to look down on the harpies and keep watch over them.

Even after that short war, the Sky Talon Style remains a commonly used martial arts style to this day. The tengus of Mt Merlin will never forget how vulnerable they were, while other aspiring martial artists see the style as a way to mimic the strength of the flying races.

Fall not like a bird losing flight, but like a blade from heaven. Dawn Waits Above, 3rd scroll of the Sky Talon Style.
descriptionText:
The various clans of Harpies and Tengus live in harmony together on Mt Merlin. This was not always the case.

A long time ago, the two races were in a violent territorial dispute over the mountain. The tengu held control over it when the harpies moved in. The tengu were worried about the threat of these sudden newcomers, while the harpies considered Mt Merlin part of Northis territory and thus their land by right.

The tengu had both the numbers and defenders advantage and quickly moved to forcefully expel the invaders after the brief set of negotiations broke down. However, they had lacked knowledge of the Harpys innate mastery of the wind. Their downbursts would snipe tengus out of the sky without any recourse and the first battle ended in a crushing defeat for the crows.

Seizing the momentum, the harpies pushed forward and suppressed the tengus, who were unable to use their usual flying combat strategies. Hope seemed lost. Until, Dawn Waits Above stepped out of her solitary training from the highest peaks of the mountains.

Dawn gifted onto her fellow tengus a martial arts style that let them harness the power of the wind themselves. They managed to fly faster and land extremely quickly to avoid the dangerous blasts of wind coming from the sky. Once they closed the gap, their wind-infused fists knocked the harpies out of the sky themselves where they were beaten into submission.

With the new techniques, the tides of the battle shifted into a stalemate. With neither side having an advantage and enough blood already spilled, the two races agreed to stop fighting. The tengus settled on the higher peaks, always being able to look down on the harpies and keep watch over them.

Even after that short war, the Sky Talon Style remains a commonly used martial arts style to this day. The tengus of Mt Merlin will never forget how vulnerable they were, while other aspiring martial artists see the style as a way to mimic the strength of the flying races.

Fall not like a bird losing flight, but like a blade from heaven. Dawn Waits Above, 3rd scroll of the Sky Talon Style.

keyAbilityRef.name: Boundless Wind
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Boundless Wind Stance.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Boundless Wind Stance
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You enter the Wind Stance, an unarmed style focusing around aerial combat. While in this stance, you may Hover and Fly up to your speed. If you already have a natural Flying speed or the Flight racial ability, your flying speed is increased  by 10ft.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance or make an attack with anything other than a melee attack or an ability with the Unarmed Strike keyword.

ability1Ref.name: Master of the Empty Sky

ability1Ref.descriptionText: When flying, you gain a +2 Cover bonus to your Evasion and Dodge against ranged attacks.

ability2Ref.name: Heavenfall Descent
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You crash to the ground without taking falling damage and make a Light attack against all enemies within 5ft of you.

May target a point on the floor as long as it’s within 20ft of you horizontally.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You crash to the ground without taking falling damage and make a Light attack against all enemies within 5ft of you.

May target a point on the floor as long as it’s within 20ft of you horizontally.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Must be airborne and between 20ft and 120ft above the ground.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Must be airborne and between 20ft and 120ft above the ground.

ability3Ref.name: Swift Wind Palm
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target is knocked down 30ft or until they hit the floor (on top of any other height loss from being hit while flying) and you may Sidestep next to them afterwards. 

A target knocked down on to the ground with this ability is knocked prone and takes falling damage as normal. They do not take the normal extra damage as result of being knocked into the floor.
ability3Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target is knocked down 30ft or until they hit the floor (on top of any other height loss from being hit while flying) and you may Sidestep next to them afterwards.

A target knocked down on to the ground with this ability is knocked prone and takes falling damage as normal. They do not take the normal extra damage as result of being knocked into the floor.
ability3Ref.requirementHtml: You and the target must be airborne
ability3Ref.requirementText: You and the target must be airborne

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Cloud Piercing Claw
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, they are knocked airborne 20ft until the end of your turn (if this attack is made outside of your turn, they drop immediately). They are NOT knocked prone from this, but do take fall damage like normal.

You may Sidestep up to 25ft into the air after making this attack, regardless of if you did damage with it.

Combo: If used after entering Boundless Wind Stance, the mana cost of this ability is reduced by 1.

name: Sniper
guideHtml:
The sniper uses sniper rifles to fire at enemies from far beyond a range in which they can counter. It will be rare to get these extreme range fight, but you should always look to play as far back as possible.

Ideally, you want to move as little as possible to get the accuracy bonus from Snipe. Positioning well early in the fight will keep paying off as it goes on.

Penetrating Shot allows you to remain stationary without having to care about the cover the enemy has. Check with the GM if their cover can be penetrated before using it.

With Concealed Shot, you are able to keep entering stealth after firing make your attacks impossible to react to. The RP cost may not be an issue either as long as you can stay far enough away from the fighting that you do not take counterattacks.

Aimed Shot is a lock-on Heavy attack that can punish dodge users. Stealthed attacks can do the same, but you won't alway have that option.

Killshot is the opening move of the sniper, provided you are able to remain hidden from the enemies before the fight starts. Normally high AP abilities do not gain the benefit of being a hidden attacker, but Killshot is an exception to this if used in the first round. This lets a sniper do huge damage right from the start. You can combine it with the ranger's guided aim ability for an almost certain hit that cannot be reacted to in any way.

ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. This attack penetrates through thin layers of cover such as wood planks (GM’s decision), negating the bonus it would give.

You may make a Heavy Attack against the target that deals double Heavy Damage instead.

If fired in the first round of combat while all enemies are unaware of you, the target has to spend an additional RP to react to your attack. This stacks with the Trick Attack keyword, but is ineffective against abilities that negate surprise or stealth attacks such as Instinct.

name: Sorcerer
descriptionHtml:
Why cant I just use a wand? This is hard!

Kori flexed her fingers as the magic she was trying to channel into a magical blast fizzled underneath her skin. Her tutor, Mister Corwin, tutted as he watched her stoically. But most importantly, he didnt hand her her wand. Her long ears twitched and flattened back, mirroring the annoyance on her face.

Mister Corwin!

You are the Crown Princess of Ithelia, Princess Itia. The magical traditions and strengths of your family run in your blood. This power and these spells are your heritage and the source of pride and defense of the kingdom.

Kori continued to pout. Mister Corwin sighed lightly.

If you can channel this magic without a focus today, Ill inform the Queen of how well youre doing in your studies. Im sure shell reward you for your diligence.

Koris pout vanished in an instant, thoughts of treats and sweets swirling through her mind. With a shake of her head that caused her ears to flop about, she turned her attention back to the target dummy a few paces away.

Remember, focus not just on your own magic, but the magic in the air and the stone beneath your feet. Open yourself up to the flow of worldy magic and become a conduit&

She tuned out Mister Corwins lecture, taking a breath as she reached out with the part of her that was real but unreal at the same time. She breathed in, a phantom breath mirroring her, filling her with an energy that made her blood feel fizzy. She brought her hand up and breathed out steadily, picturing the magic pulling in from the air and floor beneath her feet, into her core, and up her arm.

Taro! 

Her skin tingled and a prismatic sheen covered her arm for a moment before it leapt from her outstretched hand in a bolt, hitting the target dummy with a resounding crash that knocked it over and left a scorched divot on the shoulder.

I did it! Kori spun around with a wide grin, her dress fluttering around her ankles. 

You did, Princess. Well done. Now, do it again, faster.

guideHtml:
The sorcerer is a simple spellcasting striker class.

Your magic blast is a close range spell. It's not really more effective than using a wand, but it can be used by other abilities that use spells for elemental damage access.

Arcane Rapidfire is a spell that gives you some extra light attacks in exchange for mana. At circuit costs, it's pretty efficient damage but the ability shares the circuit with Arcane Blast which may be the superior damage option. Arcane Blast is a 1 AP heavy with a big mana cost. It's useful when you don't have the full AP to fire a heavy.

Finally, Circuit Reset lets you spend 1 AP to reduce the mana cost of an ability by 1 in the future. It's not really worth using circuit reset just for the reduced mana cost if you have other options, but if you have a spare AP it may be more worth it than making a light.

name: Spearmaster
descriptionHtml: An expert spear fighter.
descriptionText: An expert spear fighter.
guideHtml:
The spearmaster combines the damage dealing capabilities of the lancer and improves on them, adding some controlling power to the mix.

Versatile range allows you to manipulate your own threatened range. This is useful in spread out melee fights where enemies may have too much freedom to move away from you otherwise.

Draw-In can either be used as a tool to help position enemies for your allies or it can be combined with Lunge to draw an enemy far away into the range of your polearm.

Spear Charge is effectively an upgraded version of the basic Charge that the fighter class has. It gives you a small damage bonus, but you are limited to a single attack even if you spend 4 AP. You can also combine it with Wild Thrusts. Wild Thrusts is an AoE attack that leads into a zone denial. The box it strikes in is fairly small, so it is most powerful in tight spaces. It disables your other attacks of opportunity, but you may cancel it when an enemy is about to provoke to attack them.

The secret art Gae Bolg is a fairly simple move. With a relatively low cost for a secret art, it's a guaranteed hit with a heavy attack unless fate decides otherwise. It doesn't add any extra damage, so against Block type defenders it may not be worth the mana cost.

keyAbilityRef.name: Versatile Range
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may treat your polearm’s threatened range as 5ft instead of 5-10ft. You must declare this during your turn and it lasts until the start of your next turn.

ability1Ref.name: Draw-In
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, they are pulled up to 5ft towards you.

Combo: If used after Lunge, the range of this attack and the pullback distance are doubled. This consumes the Lunge effect.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, they are pulled up to 5ft towards you.

Combo: If used after Lunge, the range of this attack and the pullback distance are doubled. This consumes the Lunge effect.
ability1Ref.requirementHtml: Polearm only.
ability1Ref.requirementText: Polearm only.

ability2Ref.name: Wild Thrusts
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You rapidly thrust your weapon in front of you, making a Light attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide, 10ft long box in front of you. Until the start of your next turn, you may make Attacks of Opportunity against any enemy walking through that box. You cannot make any other Attacks of Opportunity while this effect lasts.

You may cancel this effect for 0 RP.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You rapidly thrust your weapon in front of you, making a Light attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide, 10ft long box in front of you. Until the start of your next turn, you may make Attacks of Opportunity against any enemy walking through that box. You cannot make any other Attacks of Opportunity while this effect lasts.

You may cancel this effect for 0 RP.
ability2Ref.requirementHtml: Polearm only.
ability2Ref.requirementText: Polearm only.

ability3Ref.name: Spear Charge
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may spend 2 or 4 AP on this ability and move as though you had spent that AP on movement. After you finish moving, you may spend half of the AP you used on this ability on a single Light or Heavy attack.

For every 10ft moved, this attack gains +1 damage up to a maximum of +10.

If you spent 4 AP on this ability, you may also choose to use Wild Thrusts instead of making a Light or Heavy attack. These attacks only gain the damage bonus listed above for the initial attacks (and not the constant attacks of opportunity) and Wild Thrusts mana cost is reduced to 0 if used in this way.

These attacks only gain the damage bonus listed above for the initial attacks (and not the constant attacks of opportunity) and Wild Thrusts mana cost is reduced to 0 if used in this way.

ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Make a Heavy attack against the target. Unless your accuracy roll is a 1, your roll is treated as if you rolled a 20 and it gains Sure Hit. 

It is treated as a critical hit even though it's Sure Hit, unless the target blocks.

keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Draw Elements II.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Draw Elements II
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may choose an element. Until the start of your next turn, you are considered to have mastered that element.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may choose an element. Until the start of your next turn, you are considered to have mastered that element.

ability1Ref.name: Spellblade's Preparation
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You unsheathe your weapon and enchant your weapon with an element you've mastered. Your weapon now deals the chosen element’s damage instead of its normal damage.

If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.

The weapon gains a +1 Power bonus to attacks until the end of the encounter.
ability1Ref.descriptionText:
You unsheathe your weapon and enchant your weapon with an element you've mastered. Your weapon now deals the chosen element’s damage instead of its normal damage.

If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.

The weapon gains a +1 Power bonus to attacks until the end of the encounter.

ability2Ref.name: Elemental Sweep
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You may make a Light Attack against all enemies within range. On damage, your attack applies an additional effect based on one of your attack's damage type. (If you are able to have multiple damage types on the attack such as through Holy weapon, you may pick any of the effects).

Arcane: The target loses 1 mana.
Fire: The target becomes Burning X, where X equals your Power x 3.
Water: The target becomes Soaked X, where X equals your power/2 (rounded down), until they take Lightning damage.
Lightning: The target is afflicted with Static X, where X equals your Power x 3. 
Earth/Frost: The target becomes Slowed until the start of your next turn.
Wind: You may push the target back 10ft.
Holy: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Power+5.
Dark: The target becomes Blinded 5 until after their next attack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You may make a Light Attack against all enemies within range. On damage, your attack applies an additional effect based on one of your attack's damage type. (If you are able to have multiple damage types on the attack such as through Holy weapon, you may pick any of the effects).

Arcane: The target loses 1 mana.
Fire: The target becomes Burning X, where X equals your Power x 3.
Water: The target becomes Soaked X, where X equals your power/2 (rounded down), until they take Lightning damage.
Lightning: The target is afflicted with Static X, where X equals your Power x 3.
Earth/Frost: The target becomes Slowed until the start of your next turn.
Wind: You may push the target back 10ft.
Holy: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Power+5.
Dark: The target becomes Blinded 5 until after their next attack.

ability3Ref.name: Double Elements
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml: Your next Elemental Strike or Elemental Sweep ability used this turn may add in the effect of another element. This element must be one you have mastered and different from the effect that's already dealt by the attack.
ability3Ref.descriptionText: Your next Elemental Strike or Elemental Sweep ability used this turn may add in the effect of another element. This element must be one you have mastered and different from the effect that's already dealt by the attack.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Secret Art: Spectrum Blade
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Until the end of the encounter, you enchant your weapon with every single element. When using Elemental Strike or Elemental Sweep, you may choose which effect you want regardless of the damage type your attack deals. When you deal damage, you apply the damage type that would deal the most damage.

In addition, your weapon gains a +1 Power bonus until the end of the encounter. If it already had a +1 Power bonus through an enchantment, this bonus is instead increased to +2.

If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.

name: Spellblade
guideHtml:
The spellblade is a melee class that relies on its versatility of its weapon enchantments to gain an edge in battle.

Imbue Weapon allows you to always do damage that an enemy is weak to if you're aware of it. Greater Imbue Weapon is similar, paying 1 mana cost for +1 power bonus. Most of the time, if you are planning to stick with 1 enchantment, you will want to use the Greater version. In fights where you are constantly swapping your enchantment, it may be more worth to stick with the normal version.

Draw Elements is what allows the spellblade to pick whatever element they want. Outside of elemental damage bonuses, this is mostly relevant for Elemental Strike.

Elemental Strike is a light attack that can apply a whole variety of effects, based on what your weapon is currently enchanted with. Which one of these is the most effective depends heavily on the situation.

Spellstrike lets you use attack magic despite being in melee combat. It is effectively the same as casting a spell without provoking attacks of opportunity, but since it is applied through your weapon it does benefit from Greater Imbue Weapon or similar weapon related buffs.
guideText:
The spellblade is a melee class that relies on its versatility of its weapon enchantments to gain an edge in battle.

Imbue Weapon allows you to always do damage that an enemy is weak to if you're aware of it. Greater Imbue Weapon is similar, paying 1 mana cost for +1 power bonus. Most of the time, if you are planning to stick with 1 enchantment, you will want to use the Greater version. In fights where you are constantly swapping your enchantment, it may be more worth to stick with the normal version.

Draw Elements is what allows the spellblade to pick whatever element they want. Outside of elemental damage bonuses, this is mostly relevant for Elemental Strike.

Elemental Strike is a light attack that can apply a whole variety of effects, based on what your weapon is currently enchanted with. Which one of these is the most effective depends heavily on the situation.

Spellstrike lets you use attack magic despite being in melee combat. It is effectively the same as casting a spell without provoking attacks of opportunity, but since it is applied through your weapon it does benefit from Greater Imbue Weapon or similar weapon related buffs.
skills: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Intimidation, Magic, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
heart: You gain +1 Fitness or Reason.
soul: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

keyAbilityRef.name: Draw Elements
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: You may use Draw Elements.

ability2Ref.name: Elemental Strike
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
You make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, your attack applies an additional effect based on one of your attack's damage type. (If you are able to have multiple damage types on the attack such as through Holy weapon, you may pick any of the effects).


Arcane: The target loses 1 mana. 
Fire: The target becomes Burning X, where X equals your Power x 3. 
Water: The target becomes Soaked X, where X equals your power/2 (rounded down), until they take Lightning damage.
Lightning: The target is afflicted with Static X, where X equals your Power x 3
Earth/Frost: The target becomes Slowed until the start of your next turn.
Wind: You may push the target back 10ft. 
Holy: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Power + 5.
Dark: The target becomes Blinded 5 until after their next attack.
ability2Ref.descriptionText:
You make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, your attack applies an additional effect based on one of your attack's damage type. (If you are able to have multiple damage types on the attack such as through Holy weapon, you may pick any of the effects).

Arcane: The target loses 1 mana.
Fire: The target becomes Burning X, where X equals your Power x 3.
Water: The target becomes Soaked X, where X equals your power/2 (rounded down), until they take Lightning damage.
Lightning: The target is afflicted with Static X, where X equals your Power x 3
Earth/Frost: The target becomes Slowed until the start of your next turn.
Wind: You may push the target back 10ft.
Holy: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Power + 5.
Dark: The target becomes Blinded 5 until after their next attack.

ability3Ref.name: Spellstrike
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
You choose an ability with the Spell keyword that you can use. You make a melee attack against a target in range for the same AP and mana as the Spell. 

Your attack functions like the chosen Spell does, but does not provoke an Attack of Opportunity and is not treated as casting a Spell (meaning it can be used while Raging and Anti-Spell effects don't work). If the chosen spell was an AoE effect, the target point would be the target you strike with the spell. The requirements for applying any effects remain the same. 

The spell you chose cannot be used again in the same round like normal, and if used with Secret Arts it counts as your Secret Art use for that encounter.

Only works with Spells that are attacks.

Only works with Spells that are attacks.

ultimateAbilityRef.name: Greater Imbue Weapon
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may enchant your weapon as though you had used Imbue Weapon, but it also gains a +1 Power bonus in addition to the normal benefits. If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.
ultimateAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may enchant your weapon as though you had used Imbue Weapon, but it also gains a +1 Power bonus in addition to the normal benefits. If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.

name: Starcaller
descriptionHtml:
Astrea Lu Novar stood on a cliff overlooking the plains in the distance. Already she could see the Westrian forces approach in a tight combat formation towards the severely outnumbered Sorthen forces that hugged the edge of the forest. They were weakened and the Westrian had brought many paladins for this fight. However, the Westrians probably werent expecting her.

She looked at her younger brother and nodded. He shouted commands and all around her she could feel their subordinates set up their defensive barrier of stars. Sorthen saboteurs could probably sneak through it, but Westrians? They would kill themselves trying to break through.

As this happened, the Westrians had begun to engage the forces below. Astra raised her hand to the sky and dozens followed after her. Target the healers. She gave a simple command and uncountable stars began to form above the heads of the humans. Their panic and attempts to flee and find cover did little to save them from the magical energy that exploded onto the battlefield.

With the healers weakened or killed, she directed her attention to the paladins still cutting through her fellow demons in the frontline. A point of white-gold light ignited in the air above their leader. The air tightened around it, distorting faintly like a heat haze with a ring of plasma forming around it. And then it ruptures, exploding outwards in a violent flare leaving nothing but a crater where the man once was.

The rest scramble for a counteroffensive, but that too would be pointless. After all, how could one fight the sky itself?

When you see a falling star, do not wish. Run.

guideHtml:
The starcaller is a long range DPS mage class.

Your key ability Celestial Artillery lets you ignore cover when attacking from long range with the spells from this class. Comet is a simple long range spell to let you do this reliably without spending mana.

Starfall is a zone denial tool that's interesting in that it punishes whoever doesn't move out more due to suddenly taking all the hits. It's only light attacks though, so it won't do much vs tanks.

Corona Burst is a simple Heavy attack with added damage on top.

Starlance is a precise attack that lets you ignore a good portion of the target's guard. This makes it effective against high agility targets who generally have low guard to begin with, while still letting you do acceptable damage to people with medium guard levels too.

Finally, Stardust Reverie is another zone control tool that helps you prevent enemies from rushing your position and denying your long range passive. You can use it early in a fight and keep adjusting the range as enemies approach you.

keyAbilityRef.name: Celestial Artillery
keyAbilityRef.benefit1: When attacking an enemy that’s further than 80ft away, your spells with the Celestial keyword gain the Artillery keyword.
keyAbilityRef.benefit2: You may use Comet.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Comet
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Light (1 AP) or Heavy (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Arcane damage.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Light (1 AP) or Heavy (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Arcane damage.

ability1Ref.name: Starfall
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml:
You target a 25x25 square within range. On the start of your next turn, 4 stars fall down in the area.

At the start of your next turn, you make 4 light attacks that deal Arcane damage, evenly divided amongst enemies in the area. If there are less enemies than stars, you may hit an enemy more than once (though you must still attempt to hit each enemy an equal amount of times if possible).
ability1Ref.descriptionText:

ability2Ref.name: Corona Burst
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml: You make a Heavy attack against the target that deals Fire damage. For every 20ft of distance the target is away from you, this deals +1 damage. (Up to a maximum of +12)
ability2Ref.descriptionText: You make a Heavy attack against the target that deals Fire damage. For every 20ft of distance the target is away from you, this deals +1 damage. (Up to a maximum of +12)

ability3Ref.name: Stardust Reverie
ability3Ref.descriptionHtml:
Declare a distance up to the range of this spell. You create a celestial dome at that range from you that harm those that pass through it.

Until the start of your next turn, whenever a creature willingly enters that distance from you, you may make a Light attack against them that deals Arcane damage. You may choose to spend an RP before making this attack to make it a Heavy attack. A character that Sidesteps or Teleports through your stars does not provoke this attack.

Upkeep: 1 mana. When you pay the upkeep cost, you may change the distance of the stars.

name: Still Stone Style
descriptionHtml:
The Nio have stood at the gates of shrines, temples, and holy places for centuries, carved in stone yet bound in spirit. Many of them never stir, sleeping away decades in statue form. But history remembers the few who did awaken when their sacred grounds were threatened.

The most famous account comes from the fall of the Twin Gate of Renkoji, The temple,one of the oldest temples that existed in Kirara, was guarded by two great Nio known as Agyo and Ungyo

When an enemy force descended upon the shrine, the monks inside knew that they would not be able to hold it. But the wardens stepped down from their pedestals for the first time in living memory. They fought without weapons, their arms and fists breaking spears like kindling, their bodies weathering blows that would have felled a dozen warriors. When enemies slipped past, the guardians leapt to intercept, placing themselves between blade and priest.

The monks of RenkMji observed their movements in awe. After the battle, when the twin wardens finally fell and their bodies shattered at the temple threshold, the surviving disciples etched every move into scrolls. They called it the Still Stone Style, named so out of respect for the guardians who gave their life to protect them.

To this day, Nio and other devoted martial artists practice the style, its philosophy unchanged: To become the wall between your enemies and your friends.

Let your body be like jade, unyielding and pure. -Still Stone Style 5th scroll.

You enter a stance that grants you the toughness of the earth. You gain a +2 bonus to your guard and a +6 bonus to your Block.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance.

Combo: If used after Steadfast Earth Stance, the mana cost is reduced by 1.

name: Strategist
descriptionHtml:
Shannon studied the field as she idly leaned to avoid a bolt of magical fire. Kane and Mara were making a fighting retreat not too far away, the burly guardian ensuring the much smaller demon woman was safe while providing her the openings to harass the three enemies pressuring them. Behind the line of melee, Kaede was darting around a frustrated pyromancer, most of her attacks deflecting off hasty arcane barriers as she disrupted his attempts to bring his firepower to bear. Shannons own pistol weighed heavily in her hand but the enemy mage was out of range and she didnt want to risk hitting her team by firing into the melee. And besides, her best contribution wasnt necessarily to provide ranged fire support, but to be slightly removed from the battle to help guide it.

And suddenly, a moment. The enemy teams highlander was screened by his teammates and was making an all or nothing push against Mara.

Fall back! she commanded, mana surging through her voice and the air to lend it that much more punch. Her fan snapped open and closed in a triple rhythm, carrying an equal amount of meaning to her order. Not a full retreat but a bait to break their ranks and counter attack.

Kaede, you too! The aggressive catgirl blinked and then darted away from the pyromancer shed been hassling, ending up slightly behind the enemy front line. That confused them even more as they struggled to decide if breaking Shannons defensive formation was the play or to punish the now seemingly vulnerable Kaede. But before they could make up their mind, Kane and Mara suddenly planted their feet and turned, taking the chasing highlander by surprise as they re-engaged him.

Now swing the hammer! Anvil formation!

Kaede seemed to glide right through the staggered enemy frontline formation and slammed into the back of the highlander. The man went tumbling from her axe strike and didnt get back up. Suddenly, the battlefield had reversed itself and the pressure had instantly shifted. The enemy confusedly reformed their battered front line and a fireball crashed into Kanes shield to no seeming effect.

But that breath of a moment had allowed her team to recover their footing and bearings. It was time to end it.

Secret Art: Shock and Awe!

Energy swirled around Shannon as she snapped her fan shut and pointed it directly at the reeling enemy. Take them!

It was over seconds later and she barely heard the judge announce Team Shannon as the winners over the cheering crowd.

guideHtml:
The strategist is a continuation of the tactician class and like it focuses on supporting their team.

Sound the retreat is limited in its use, but very useful if you do have to run away from an enemy you can't beat. Feigned retreat on the other hand is used for a temporary retreat, functioning like Order: Retreat but working on more than 1 ally. The free attack of opportunity is also nice and your allies can choose whether they want to make a light attack, or spend the 1 RP for a heavy one.

Anvil tactics is another ability that lets an ally make an attack of opportunity. If they can spare the RP, getting a heavy attack with lock on can be pretty valuable. Even without it, the low cost on anvil tactics still makes it worthwhile to use as long as you can guarantee the attack.

Wall Formation is for when you need to stand your ground, giving all allies next to eachother a noticeable increase to guard and block. Do be wary that this type of positioning may make you more vulnerable to AoE attacks.

Finally, your secret art: Shock & Awe is best used as soon as possible as long as your allies can use all the AP it gives to gain momentum.

associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You sidestep 4 times your speed, moving as far away from visible enemies as possible. After that, your allies may sidestep up to twice their speed towards you.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You sidestep 4 times your speed, moving as far away from visible enemies as possible. After that, your allies may sidestep up to twice their speed towards you.

ability1Ref.name: Wall Formation
ability1Ref.descriptionHtml: Until the start of your next turn, every ally within range gains Wall Formation. If they are adjacent to another ally gains a +1 bonus to their guard and a +4 bonus to their block value.
ability1Ref.descriptionText: Until the start of your next turn, every ally within range gains Wall Formation. If they are adjacent to another ally gains a +1 bonus to their guard and a +4 bonus to their block value.

ability2Ref.name: Feigned Retreat
ability2Ref.descriptionHtml:
All allies in a 15x15ft square within range may Sidestep up to their speed towards you. 

If they successfully left an enemy’s melee range and are not in an enemy’s melee range after moving, then the first time an enemy enters their melee range before the start of their next turn, they may make an Attack of Opportunity against that enemy.
